observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the MGSO to ensure that perfected contracts and copies of approved POs, together with its supporting documents are properly accomplished and submitted to the Office of the Auditor within five working days upon execution or issuance in compliance with COA Circular No. 2009-001.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality's current practice involves the submission of the approved copies of POs on the actual delivery of goods and services. However, this practice does not align with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001.
observations.reason: The Municipality's current practice involves the submission of the approved copies of POs on the actual delivery of goods and services. This practice affected the timely review, inspection and evaluation of the contracts. Reiterated in Part II, Audit Observation No. 8, Page Nos. 46 to 47.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
1) Direct the City Accountant to ensure consistent, timely submission of the bank reconciliation statements of all funds of the LGU; and
2) Request from the authorized government servicing banks the paid checks as attachments to the bank statements as basis for the preparation of the bank reconciliation statement, and submit the same to the Auditor for verification.
observations.managementAction: The City Treasurer’s Office, City Accounting Office, and other concerned offices will revisit the financial process for revisions as may be necessary and will maintain close coordination between and among another.
observations.reason: Similar deficiency was still noted in CY 2024 due to the delayed receipt of source documents and reports from the depository bank, the City Treasurer’s Office, and the City Accounting Office. Observations and recommendations are included in Part II of this report.

observations.managementAction: The Management will comply. Additional staff shall be assigned to assist and comply with the procedural flow.
observations.reason: Similar deficiency pertaining to the procedural flow was still noted in CY 2024. Observations and recommendations are included in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant prepare and maintain complete and updated Supplies Ledger Card (SLC) for each item of inventory and utilize the same to compute for the cost of supplies issued applying the moving average method. Likewise, we recommended that the CGSO and the Stock Custodians of the Inventories in the different offices maintain Stock Cards pertaining to the inventories in their custody. The City Accountant and the CGSO shall regularly reconcile their respective records to determine any errors in recording of inventory procurement and issuances.
observations.reason: Similar deficiency pertaining to stock cards was still noted in CY 2024. Observations and recommendations are included in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Assessor and General Services Office to facilitate the titling of the 10 untitled parcels of land amounting to ₱83.59 million. Further, require the City Budget Officer to appropriate funds for the payment of transfer taxes and other fees so that the processing of the titles could be facilitated.
observations.managementAction: The Management is still processing the corresponding Transfer Certificate of Titles for the remaining three (3) lots.
observations.reason: Research about the three lots is ongoing at the City Registry of Deeds.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the Management instruct the Inventory Committee and the City Accountant to implement the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts in accordance to COA Circular No. 2020-006 and submit the required reports thereof to the Auditor.
observations.managementAction: The Inventory Committee has already commenced with the inventory taking under the one-time cleansing for PPEs in the custody of the city and other schools. Schedules for the inventory taking for various schools were already set.
observations.reason: Lack of material time to fully implement the one-time cleansing of PPEs.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management initiate the formal transfer of the properties to the respective Barangays through the execution of a Deed of Donation with prior approval from the Sangguniang Panlungsod and prospectively ensure compliance with regulations on donated assets. Further, determine other properties in the records of the City which are in the actual custody and used by other entities (aside from barangays) and follow the same procedure in transferring these assets to the said offices.
Henceforth, Management direct the City Accountant to prepare JEVs to derecognize in the books the assets that were transferred and donated; and recognize in the books of the respective barangays the carrying amount of the assets donated and transferred to them. Further, the CGSO also derecognize these donated and transferred assets in their property records.
observations.managementAction: Deed of Donation will be executed and the concerned offices will check the records to determine other properties in the record of the City that are in the actual custody and used by other entities.
observations.reason: Similar deficiency was still noted in CY 2024. Observations and recommendations are included in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the CAO attach to the Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs), in addition to the Obligation Budget Request (OBRs), documentary evidence that the goods or services were delivered to, or received by, the City that would warrant the recording of Accounts Payable, such as but not limited to, Delivery Receipts and Inspection and Acceptance Reports, in order to establish that the recorded obligations arose from valid claims; and strengthen the accounting and processing system to ensure that financial transactions processed and recorded are properly accomplished, dated, and signed by approving authorities.
observations.managementAction: Memorandum dated February 25, 2025 was issued by the City Mayor urging strict compliance of all City offices to the audit recommendation. The Local Finance Committee shall convene to come up with a Financial Internal Policy (FIP) regarding the processing and recording of financial transactions.
observations.reason: Similar deficiency was still noted in CY 2024 due to a lack of documentary requirements for the claims. Observations and recommendations are included in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management exert its best effort to implement all programmed projects under the 20 percent EDF during the current year to fully attain the desired socioeconomic development and environmental management outcomes essential to the promotion of the general welfare of its constituents. Further, Management expedite the implementation and completion of the partially implemented and unimplemented PPAs, during the ensuing CY 2024. We also recommended that Management instruct the City Engineering Office to include in the Report on Government PPAs the development projects funded by the Barangay Augmentation Fund and conduct a periodic monitoring on the status of implementation thereof and submit report to the CPDO copy furnished the City Accountant and CBO.
observations.managementAction: The Management has instructed the City Engineering Office and the Bids and Awards Committee the processing of documents for the non-implemented projects which were already obligated. Further, the City Engineering Office was instructed to monitor the said projects and include the status of such projects in the report of utilization.
observations.reason: Similar deficiency was still noted in CY 2024 due to poor planning, inefficient procurement process, and delay in the preparation of pre-engineering works. Observations and recommendations are included in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management instruct the CDRRMO, in coordination with the CBO and CAO, to ensure the efficient and effective utilization of the DRRMF thru implementation of programs and projects designed to avoid, lessen or limit the potential adverse impact of disasters, such as projects pertaining to disaster prevention, mitigation and preparedness.
observations.managementAction: Coordination with Local Finance Committee will be made. A time and motion study will be conducted to determine if revisions are necessary in the existing operational and financial systems relative to Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management services being provided by the City Government.
observations.reason: Similar deficiency was still noted in CY 2024. Observations and recommendations are included in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the Local School Board strictly adhere to the planning and budgeting guidelines for the Special Education Fund (SEF) as required under DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017. We also recommended that the Auditing Office be furnished with documents that supported the formulation of plans and programs chargeable against SEF for verification and evaluation.
observations.managementAction: The management and the LSB will prioritize the identified PPAs in the submitted SIPs. A coordination meeting will be conducted between and among the top management, the Local Finance Committee and the Local School Board to address the issues and concerns.
observations.reason: Similar deficiency was still noted in CY 2024. Observations and recommendations are included in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City LSB also consider the implementation of PPAs that would address the nutritional needs of the students as specified in the SIPs to lessen, if not eliminate the nutritional problems in the concerned schools in Legazpi City.
observations.managementAction: The GAD Office is now focusing on the GAD Database in updating the required data/ information that will serve as basis for gender responsive planning, programming and policy formulation.
observations.reason: The project was not included due to limited funds.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our prior years’ recommendation that Management and the LSB direct all those concerned to disburse claims charged to SEF strictly in accordance with the guidelines provided under DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular (JC) No. 1, s. 2017 dated January 19, 2017 and DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular (JC) No. 1, s. 2020 dated August 27, 2020 and avoid disbursement that do not fall under the categories enumerated in the said JMCs.
observations.reason: Similar deficiency was still noted in CY 2024. Observations and recommendations are included in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that Management, through the GAD Focal Person, spearhead the setting up and maintenance of the sex disaggregated database to serve as basis for a gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation based on the relevant data for proper identification of existing gender issues and ensure that approved PPAs to be incorporated in the GPB are gender responsive.
observations.managementAction: The GAD Office is now focusing on the GAD Database in updating the required data/ information that will serve as basis for gender responsive planning, programming and policy formulation.
observations.reason: Lack of personnel to perform the GAD Database.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management together with the Sangguniang Panlungsod give priority to the revision and updating of the 2007 Revenue Code of the City, pursuant to the provisions of the Local Government Code, in order to increase tax revenues and receipts through updating of the rates in the existing tax code and provision for new revenue sources, which will augment their budget for a better delivery of government services.
observations.managementAction: Research is being done as regards taxes and fees to determine the costs/ amounts in accordance with existing laws, rules and policies.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the CHRMO to formulate a Human Resource Plan and issue a uniform policy guideline on the hiring of JO personnel to determine the necessity of the hiring as well as to strengthen the internal controls pertaining thereto; and Management categorically stipulates in the Job Order Contract or Contract of Services the fund charging and the nature of work assignments and/or duties and responsibilities, and the quantity of work to be accomplished, and the desired work outputs to facilitate comparison and evaluation of actual work accomplishments of hired job order personnel.
We also recommended that the LCE require the CHRMO to adopt a schedule of standard rates for JOs per position that is based on qualification and the nature of work/assignment, taking into consideration the prevailing minimum wage in the locality, in order not to create inequality in the workforce of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: The CHRMO has been instructed to complete the Contract of JOs and/or the appointment document indicating the fund charging, work assignment, specific duties and responsibilities and the quantity of work to be accomplished. The City Human Resource Management Office and the Local Finance Committee shall convene in a meeting to discuss standard rates and other related concerns with consultation from the different City Departments/Divisions.
observations.reason: Similar deficiencies continued due to lack of coordination and the need to finalize policies and rates.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management strengthen efforts to implement the approved programs, projects, and activities (PPAs) outlined in the LDRRMFIP to ensure the timely delivery of services for which the fund was created; conduct a thorough assessment and prioritize PPAs that address urgent needs and are feasible for immediate implementation, particularly concerning the unimplemented PPAs; and ensure proper coordination among the CDRRMO, Accounting Office, and GSO to facilitate the effective and timely implementation of PPAs under LDRRMF.
observations.managementAction: Coordination with the Local Finance Committee will be made. A time and motion study will be conducted to determine if revisions are necessary in the existing operational and financial systems relative to Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management services being provided by the City Government.
observations.reason: Similar deficiency was still noted in CY 2024 due to lack of coordination among offices.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Sangguniang Panlungsod strictly adhere to the stipulations under RA 10121, particularly on the proper allocation of the 30 percent QRF and 70 percent Mitigation Fund of the LDRRMF to adequately support disaster preparedness, response, and recovery efforts; and ensure that items procured under LDRRMF-QRF align with established standards for emergency relief, prioritizing non-perishable and essential food items that are suitable for immediate distribution and prolonged storage. We also recommended that the City Budget Officer and City Accountant conduct a thorough review of fund sources and disbursements to ensure that LDRRMF is efficiently utilized, relief procurement is compliant with regulations, and that all purchases serve their intended purpose under disaster response programs.
observations.managementAction: The Management respectfully informed the Audit Team that the appropriation was charged against the unappropriated surplus and not the regular sources of income. The concerned offices will conduct a review of fund sources and disbursements and strengthen prioritization measures.
observations.reason: Auditor’s Rejoinder noted that only certain sources may be appropriated and concerns remain about full compliance with RA 10121 and suitability of procured items.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the concerned officers and employees to immediately submit the lacking supporting documents stated, otherwise their non-submission would warrant the issuance of a Notice of Suspension/Disallowance; instruct the City Accountant to ensure that the Disbursement Vouchers are supported with complete documentation before forwarding the same to the Audit Team for verification; and strictly comply with the provisions of Section 4(6) of P.D. No. 1445 that claims against government funds shall be supported with complete documentation.
observations.managementAction: The concerned officers and employees have submitted the lacking documents, subject to the verification of the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the GAD Focal Person revisit the unimplemented PPAs to identify those that are urgently needed and feasible for implementation within the current year; and require the City Engineering Office to prepare a detailed Accomplishment Report or Status of Implementation of the PPAs attributed to GAD and submit the same to the Audit Team for verification.
observations.managementAction: Management committed to following the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the City Engineering Office to prepare a detailed Accomplishment Report or Status of Implementation of the PPAs attributed to GAD and submit the same to the Audit Team for verification.
observations.managementAction: The management will comply with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management, together with the Sangguniang Panlungsod, give priority to the revision and updating of the 2007 Revenue Code of the City, pursuant to the provisions of the Local Government Code; require the Budget Officer to render a systematic and realistic revenue forecasting; and require the City Treasury and other Departments involved in generating revenues to meet its targets by conducting periodic review of collection performance and analyze reasons for shortfalls.
observations.managementAction: The management will comply. Research is being done as regards taxes and fees to determine the costs/ amounts in accordance with existing laws, rules and policies. The management committed to complying with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Budget Officer to render a systematic and realistic revenue forecasting, taking into account prior years’ experiences and statistics as aid in setting targets; and require the City Treasury and other Departments involved in generating revenues to meet its targets by conducting periodic review of collection performance by the concerned officials to ascertain whether collection targets are attained and to analyze the reasons for the shortfall in collections and find alternative solutions to minimize, reduce, or avoid incurrence of shortfall in the future.
observations.managementAction: Management committed to complying with the audit recommendation.
GSO to record the unserviceable properties in the IIRUP, conduct proper disposal with the disposal committee and require the Municipal Accountant to drop the unserviceable properties from the books
observations.reason: Disposal procedures not yet started due to the busy schedule of the disposal committee.
Reiterated in Part II of this AAR

observations.managementAction: There is still a delay in the notification.
The MGC is committed to implementing the recommendations in the ensuing years.
The MGC is committed to implementing the recommendations in the ensuing years.

observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this AAR
Reiterated in Part II of this AAR

observations.managementAction: The draft of the proposed Municipal Ordinance, which includes provisions for the collection of a garbage fee, was submitted to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this AAR
Reiterated in Part II of this AAR

observations.managementAction: The Management will intensify its tax collection strategies to increase its revenues.
observations.reason: Tax remedies such as levy of the real properties require the involvement of the Provincial Government for its execution.
The requested copies of the contracts remain unsubmitted.
The procurement and handling of inventories are not being properly managed.
Reiterated in Part II of this AAR

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to submit promptly the required monthly and quarterly financial reports on or before the due dates in compliance with Item 4.1 of COA Circular No. 2010-001 dated March 2, 2010.
observations.reason:
Reiterate in the Part II of AAR

Due to manpower deficiency.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Require the concerned Accountable Officer to submit the accounts and financial reports to the Municipal Treasurer pursuant to the timeliness of the abovementioned existing law, rules and regulations;

Direct the Municipal Treasurer to submit to the Municipal Accounting Office all the disbursements vouchers, payrolls, official receipts, including the corresponding reports for each month on or before the fifth day of the ensuing month or pursuant to the abovementioned pertinent provisions of rules and regulations, as applicable; and

Direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly comply with the timeliness on the recording and submission of accounts to the Auditor within ten days of the ensuing month
observations.reason:
Reiterate in the Part II of AAR

Due to manpower deficiency in the respective offices.

Reiterate in the Part II of AAR

Due to manpower deficiency in the respective offices.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Local Chief Executive:

Instruct the GSO to initiate the process of disposing the unserviceable properties by preparing the IIRUP in accordance with Section 125 of the Manual on the NGAS, Volume I. 

After the disposal, direct the Municipal Accountant to facilitate the derecognition of the unserviceable properties from the books of account.
observations.reason: Incomplete knowledge of the disposal process.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend the following courses of action: 

Direct the Municipal Accountant to strengthen the accounting and processing system being part of the accounting and fiscal control process and ensure that transactions processed, paid and recorded are properly approved and duly supported with complete documents and/or technical requirements and proper signatures; and  

Require the concerned Municipal personnel to use the Checklist provided for under the Revised Documentary Requirements for Common Government Transactions prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012-001 and the Revised RIRR of RA No. 9184 as a useful guide in the processing of claims for payment.
observations.reason: Due to manpower deficiency in the respective offices.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE inform the ATL within 10 days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity of any postings of project signboards and/or public notice for validation by the Audit Team.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer provide the Audit Team at the beginning of the year, a list of all on-going and to be implemented PPAs during the year 	with detailed information prescribed under COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.

We recommended that Management direct the BAC to Strictly comply to regularly prepare and submit an approved PMR in accordance with the pertinent provisions of 	RA No. 9184 and in the format prescribed in GPPB Circular No. 03-2017.

observations.reason:
Reiterated in the Part II of AAR

The Municipal Accountant could not retrieve any relevant documents.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board (MSWMB) to:

strictly implement the “No Segregation, No Collection Policy” to decrease the residual waste for disposal; and

conduct massive IEC on waste segregation at source, reuse and recycling of wastes.

We also recommended that the LCE require the MENRO to maintain the records relative to record of daily weights or volumes of waste received and processed and tracking of success of waste reduction programs.
observations.reason:
Reiterate in the Part II of AAR

Due to manpower deficiency in the MENRO.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management, through the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS), expedite the full establishment of the GAD database in order to have a basis for planning and programming PPAs and formulating policies that are gender responsive and to be able to undertake thorough gender analysis based on relevant data to properly identify existing gender issues and ensure that the approved PPAs to be incorporated in the GAD Plan and Budget are gender responsive.
We reiterated our recommendation to ensure the timely submission and faithful compliance of the monthly the CRAAF in order to prevent possible loss and misuse of accountable forms.
observations.reason: The delay was caused by insufficient manpower.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management stop the practice of prematurely issuing certificate of acceptance and comply with the set timeline to maximize the one-year defects liability period. Furthermore, require to require the contractors to submit valid substitute, as prescribed in the IRR of RA 9184, for the norescribeed retention money.   

Likewise, we reiterated our recommendation to instruct the Bids and Awards Committee to remind the bidders for the contracts to be bidded by the Municipal Government of the requirement of retention money on projects as provided in the above cited provisions of the Revised IRR of RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: The Bids and Awards Committee has also been instructed to remind the contract bidders of the requirement of retention money as provided in the above cited provisions.

observations.recommendation:
To ensure proper accounting and promote better control in the receipt and issuance of Supplies and Materials, we reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the LCE to require:

The Property Officer to maintain perpetual inventory records of Supplies and Materials purchased and to ensure that complete records of the items issued to requisitioning departments/offices are kept. At the end of each month, he shall prepare the monthly Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued, a copy of which shall be furnished the Municipal Accountant for recording.

The Municipal Accountant to record all regular purchases of Supplies and Materials under the proper inventory account following the perpetual inventory method;

At the end of each semester, the Property Officer shall conduct an inventory, in coordination with the concerned department/office, of all unused Supplies and Materials and shall prepare a consolidated report thereon and furnish the Municipal Accountant a copy thereof; and

The Municipal Accountant to take up the necessary adjustment, if any, in the respective inventory and expense accounts for Supplies and Materials based on the consolidated inventory report provided by the Property Officer.
observations.reason:
Reiterate in the Part II of AAR.

Due to manpower deficiency in the respective offices.

Reiterate in the Part II of AAR.

Due to manpower deficiency in the respective offices.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Enforce the payment of liquidated damages against erring contractors for non-performance of their obligations to the LGU within the periods stipulated in the contracts

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare and submit the complete set of financial statements on time for all funds to allow the timely audit thereof and the timely submission of the required report. We also recommended adequate internal controls on the maintenance and security of financial data.
observations.reason: Reiterated in CY 2024

observations.recommendation:
We recommended Management to file the request for authority, to write-off the dormant unliquidated cash advances supported with the pertinent documents prescribed under the COA Circular No. 2016-005.

Henceforth, we recommended Management to require the Municipal Accountant to monitor and submit a complete report of cash advances and properly maintain adequate subsidiary records thereof.

Require Accountable Officers to refund immediately any excess/unutilized cash advance upon liquidation; and

Require the Municipal Accountant to refrain from granting additional cash advances to officers and employees without liquidating first their previous cash advances.

Impose firm policies and require the officers and employees to liquidate within the reglementary period in accordance with COA Circular No. 97-002; implement sanctions in PD No. 1445 and DBM MC No. 2023-1 if necessary; ensure verification, recording, and submission of liquidation documents to the audit team within ten (10) days after receipt; ensure supporting documents for liquidation are intact and complete.

GSO to (i) install and execute control procedures to monitor all inventories procured and received under their custody and (ii) ensure the preparation and timely submission of reports pertaining to the issuance of inventory items (RIS and weekly SSMI); and Accounting Office to record the procurement of inventories as assets and consequently recognize these as expenses upon issuance to the end-users.

Direct the GSO to complete the reconciliation of RPCPPE and PPE account balance; Require the Inventory Committee to conduct the “One-Time Cleansing of PPE accounts” as provided under COA Circular No. 2020-006; Order the Inventory team to identify if the variances are non-existing/missing or unrecorded PPE as well as the persons accountable for proper disposition based on Sections 7 to 9 of COA Circular No. 2020-006; Direct the GSO to reclassify the previously classified PPE items costing below ₱50,000.00 which are still in their custody to the appropriate semi-expendable property account, prepare ICS for semi-expandable property acquired in CY 2022 onwards, and prepare the Report on the Physical Count of Semi-Expendable Property; and GSO to maintain complete Property Cards in accordance with the forms prescribed in Annexes 9 and 37 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume II.
observations.reason: Reiterated in CY 2024

observations.recommendation: Create a committee responsible for the appraisal of unserviceable property and supplies; Direct the GSO and the Municipal Accountant to reconcile their records pertaining to the unserviceable PPE particularly the cost and carrying amounts; Direct the Property Unit to prepare the IIRUP for all unserviceable property, and the WMR for all waste materials; and Direct the GSO to dispose the unserviceable property and supplies in accordance with the rules and regulations on supply and property management in local government units.

Require the Municipal Accounting Office to review thoroughly the long-outstanding payables and prepare appropriate adjusting entries for the reversion to surplus and/or to re-establish the valid claims; and in case of trust fund, determine whether the project has been completed and coordinate with the source agencies for the appropriate disposition of the remaining funds.
Require the Municipal Accountant to review the accounting errors noted and prepare adjusting entries to correct the balance of the Accounts Payable account.
observations.reason: Similar finding was observed in this year’s audit.

observations.recommendation: Inquire in writing from the Source Agencies the intended purpose of the fund transfers and whether the unutilized funds should be returned to the Source Agency; and exert effort in tracing relevant information such as the date of receipt of funds, Source Agencies, the intended purpose of the funds, and the status of implementation of the PPAs funded by the transfers.
observations.reason: The fund transfers remained outstanding in the books. Similar observation was issued in CY 2024.

observations.recommendation: Give more effort in the filling up of the other mandatory positions of the Municipality’s DRRM Office as required under NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC JMC Circular 2014-01 and Rule 6 Section 2 of the IRR of RA No. 10121.

Ensure that the prescribed planning/budgeting activities adhere to the required timeline in the preparation, review and approval of the proposed SEF Budget; prepare the SEF Budget following the prescribed format in the determination of estimated income for a particular budget year.
observations.reason: Fully Implemented (reiterated in CY 2024)

observations.recommendation: Revisit the GAD PPAs to consider prioritizing realizable programs adaptable to the current situation to optimally utilize allocated funds and commitments to gender equality and GAD goals.
observations.reason: Restated in CY 2024

observations.recommendation: Municipal Accountant to conduct the necessary analysis and reconciliation to properly ascertain the correct valuation of the Due to Pag-IBIG account; Municipal Treasurer to remit the full amount withheld from the employee’s salaries together with the government’s share in accordance with HDMF Circular No. 275.
observations.reason: On-going reconciliation

observations.recommendation: Exert more effort in securing the necessary supporting documents cited under COA Circular No. 2016-005 to be able to apply for request for write-off of the dormant accounts, except for the undeposited collection of Mr. Anthony B. Bisona amounting to ₱192,018.00 which was subjected to a Notice of Charge.
observations.reason: Reiterated in CY 2024

observations.recommendation: Require the GSO to conduct the physical count of inventories semestrally and submit the report to the Audit Team within the prescribed period; require the Municipal Accountant and GSO to maintain the supplies ledger card and stock cards and observe proper inventory management and internal control procedures.

Cause the reversion to the unappropriated surplus of the identified long outstanding payables where the obligation to pay has already prescribed; facilitate the prompt release of retention monies; coordinate with concerned personnel in relation to the long outstanding guaranty/security deposits payable.
Require the BAC to strictly comply with the procurement guidelines and procedures prescribed in the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.

Claim the liquidated damages against the retention money and/or other securities posted by the contractor and impose the necessary penalties and sanctions on the persons involved in the inaccurate reporting of 100% completion of the project.
observations.reason: Management claims that there were no delays in the project implementation.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Municipal Treasurer to expedite the completion of the List of Real Property Tax Delinquencies with the corresponding penalties thereon.

Prepare and execute lease contracts with the market stallholders and enforce strict compliance; comply with the Municipality’s Revenue Code in relation to issuance of business permits; review, revise and update its 1992 Municipal Tax Code pursuant to Section 191 of the LGC of 1991.

Establish specific guidelines on the grants of financial assistance to indigent constituents in line with DSWD Memorandum Circular No. 11, series of 2019 to ensure equitable distribution of financial assistance among qualified beneficiaries.
Create a plantilla for the Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Officer (MENRO) to effectively oversee the duties and functions of the office.

Continue its effort in titling the remaining 14 lots in the name of the Municipality; require the Inventory Team to prepare a complete inventory report of PPEs, including the real properties, and reconcile the report with the GSO records; instruct the General Services Officer to indicate in the RPCPPE complete information relative to these lots to facilitate the reconciliation of the report with the accounting records.
observations.reason: Seven lots currently occupied by DepEd are proposed for donation to the agency, which will then assume responsibility for titling. Meanwhile, the Approved Subdivision Plan for four other lots is still pending.

observations.recommendation: Require the General Service Officer to render Road Map and keep a complete and updated Local Road Network Property Card (LRNPCs) for all roads and components; require the Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant and General Service Officer with complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects.

Require the LSB to formulate the required three-year strategic program; consider the recommendations of the DepEd PSDS and SDS as well as the SIPs as inputs in its preparation and furnish the Audit Team a copy; adhere to provisions of the Local Government Code and DepEd-DBM-DILG JC No. 1 in preparation of the School Board budget; consider the proper method in preparation of SEF budget so that only the deficiency in the DepEd Division budget shall be provided funds therein.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare and submit the complete set of financial statements on time for all funds to allow the timely audit thereof.
observations.reason: Reiterated in CY 2024.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management direct the Accounting Office to:

Prepare and submit the Bank Reconciliation Statements for all the current accounts maintained by the Municipality in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 96-011;

Conduct analysis of the dormant trust fund accounts to review the purpose of such funds and utilize it according to its intended purpose; and

Cause the reversion thereof to the General Fund if warranted, in accordance with the requirements set by the LBP on dormant accounts.
observations.reason: Reiterated in CY 2024.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Re-evaluate the placement of funds in a time deposit account and review the fund sources to establish its idleness; and

Submit documents showing prior authority from the Sangguniang Bayan and approval of the Local Chief Executive authorizing the placement of the funds in the time deposit. Otherwise, close the time deposit account and use the fund to prioritize implementation of projects for the general welfare of the constituents.
observations.reason: No justification given.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to prepare subsidiary ledgers of the receivable and payable accounts of the LGU for proper monitoring of accounts.
observations.reason: Restated in CY 2024

observations.managementAction: No request for write-off was initiated by the agency.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Management to file the request for authority to write-off the dormant unliquidated cash advances supported with the pertinent documents prescribed under the COA Circular No. 2016-005. We also recommended the Municipal Accounting Office to properly maintain the accounts and records of the Municipality and monitor the cash advances.

observations.recommendation:

We recommended the following courses of action be taken by the Management:

Direct the Municipal Accountant to strengthen the accounting and processing system being part of the accounting and fiscal control process and ensure that transactions processed, paid and recorded are properly approved and duly supported with complete documents and/or technical requirements and proper signatures; and

Utilize the checklist provided for under the Revised Documentary Requirements for Common Government Transactions prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012-001 and the Revised RIRR of RA No. 9184 as a useful guide in the processing of claims for payment.

We recommended that Management:

Require all of the end-users of the procured laptops and desktop the submission of the specific brands, models, justification on the use of the laptop and desktop computers and other documents to support the same for consolidation and submission to the Audit Team for further verification; and

Observe prudence in the incurrence of expenditures by giving due consideration of the actual needs and requirements and by always adhering with the economic measures in every procurement activity to be undertaken.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendations that Management:

Expedite the recruitment and hiring of a full-time and qualified GSO;

Direct the Inventory Committee to determine and indicate in the RPCPPE the actual quantity on hand and the physical condition of the properties for proper measurement of its carrying amount in the financial statements; and

Direct the Property Officer to assign the corresponding Property Name and Number for all PPE items for proper identification.

Additionally, we recommended that Management implement the “One-Time Cleansing of PPE accounts” as provided under COA Circular No. 2020-006 to establish PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition and accountability.

We recommended that Management:

Direct the Municipal Accountant to record the balance of the unpaid billing of the completed project (Municipal Slaughterhouse) as Accounts Payable to complete the recorded cost of the Construction in Progress. Henceforth, reclassify the cost of the said completed project from CIP account to the appropriate PPE account and thereafter record the journal entry for the transfer of the said PPE from the TF account to the GF account. Accordingly, provide for the annual depreciation thereof from CYs 2018 to 2022 for the fair presentation of the pertinent accounts in the financial statements. 

In addition, direct the Municipal Engineer to follow up the release of the remaining fund from the Source Agency (SA) for the said project and facilitate the processing of the final payment to the contractor. Thereafter, he shall prepare the necessary documents and submit the final Liquidation Report to the SA.

Direct the Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant with copy of the Certificate of Project Completion of the completed projects, to serve as their basis in recording completed projects as PPE. Periodic depreciation shall likewise be provided to allow fair presentation of the affected accounts in the year-end financial statements;

We recommended that Management:

Direct the Municipal Accountant to further analyze the SL for PPE and determine the semi-expendable items acquired and issued on CY 2022 and prior years. Thereafter, the carrying amount shall be charged to expense or Prior Period or expense, as the case may be, in the books of accounts; and 

Direct the Municipal Accountant and Property Officer to, henceforth, follow the new capitalization threshold of newly acquired PPE and other guidelines set forth in COA Circular No. 2022-004 in the issuance of semi-expendable items and its recording in the books of accounts and other records.

We recommended that Management direct the BAC and the BAC-TWG:

To include in their post-qualification a comparative evaluation showing the number of projects with the same equipment and key personnel pledged and assigned by the same lowest calculated bidder for different projects with simultaneous or overlapping implementation period;
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this Report

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Require the MHRMO to formulate a Human Resource Plan and issue a uniform policy guideline on the hiring of JO personnel to appropriately determine the necessity of the hiring thereof and as well as to strengthen the internal controls pertaining thereto;

Prioritize filling up the vacant plantilla positions instead of hiring of JOs; and

Evaluate and determine the appropriate personnel complement of each office/department in the Municipal Government and effect the necessary amendments in their respective plantilla, if warranted.

We recommended that Management observe the above-cited regulation pertaining to the wages of the JO as prescribed by the RTWPB for Region 5. Henceforth, Management come up with a workable solution in complying with the mandated daily minimum wage rate prescribed by the RTWPB for Region 5 given the limited resources of the LGU such as reviewing the propriety of the number of JOs being hired.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive

Direct the Property Officer to facilitate the immediate disposal of the aforementioned unserviceable properties and waste materials to recoup any salvage value thereof.  

Additionally, we recommend the following measures:

Create an Appraisal and Disposal Committee, whose members are required to attend relevant trainings on property management, particularly on the disposal of unserviceable properties and waste materials, should they lack the necessary technical competence;

Conduct an appraisal of the unserviceable properties and waste materials and furnish the auditor a copy of the appraisal report thereof; and

Dispose the unserviceable properties based on the most appropriate mode of disposal pursuant to the provisions of Sections 379 to 381 of RA No. 7160 and COA Circular Nos. 89-296 and 92-386 following the procedures shown in flow chart of the disposal of property (Annex B).

Further, for the properties damaged during typhoon Rolly, we recommended that the Management:

Direct the concerned AO’s to immediately submit a request for relief from accountability and complete supporting documents enumerated in COA Audit Memorandum No. 92-751 (Annex C) for all the properties damaged;

Direct the Municipal Accountant to disclose the loss in the notes to financial statements pending the result of the request for relief from accountability and when request is granted, prepare a JEV to derecognize the assets in the books of accounts. If request is denied, the loss shall be taken up as a receivable from the accountable officer/persons liable and shall be credited to the appropriate asset account.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend, and the management agreed, that the City Mayor direct the:
 
City Accountant to prepare the adjusting journal entry to record the collected Building Permit Fee in the Trust Liability account and correct the balances of the affected accounts; and
 
City Engineer and City Treasurer to submit a Report on the Collection of Building Permit Fees as basis of the City Accountant for the preparation of the appropriate journal entry.

We recommended, and the management agreed, that the City Mayor:
 
 a. Direct the City Planning and Development Coordinator, together with the Technical Working Group (TWG) of the LPOC to ensure that:
 
The PAPs reflected on the POP for the year is anchored and taken from the three-year Peace and Order and Public Safety Plan; and

The approval of POP and release of the CF shall be evidenced by a certified copy of the minutes of meeting specifying the computation of the at least 2/3 votes of the LPOC.
 
b. Direct the City Accountant to:

Submit within seven (7) working days from the release of check, the cash advance voucher and its supporting documents to the COA ATL concerned; 

Observe the proper timeframe on the liquidation of cash advance for confidential fund; and

Always provide the COA ATL concerned a copy of the transmittal letter, duly received by the Intelligence and Confidential Fund Audit Office (ICFAO).

We recommended, and the management agreed, that the City Mayor direct the:
 
City Accountant to prepare correct journal entries for the receipt and transfer of fund;

b. The Local Finance Committee to:

prepare the Report on Fund Utilization and Status of Program Implementation within 20 days after the end of the quarter;

post the same report in the Full Disclosure Policy Bulletin Board; and

reconcile the reported completion and accomplishment in the SWA, RGPPA and RFUSPI.

c. City Planning and Development Officer to:

change the password and other credentials, and update the information in the City’s website; and

assign personnel that is tasked and focused on posting the Quarterly Report on Fund Utilization and Status of Program Implementation of LGSF-SBDP;

BAC Chairman to submit the contract within five working days from the time the contract is perfected; and

City Engineer to monitor and inspect the implementation of the LGSF-SBDP, and make sure that the project will be completed on the target date of completion.

We recommended, and the management agreed, that the City Mayor direct:

a. City Planning and Development Officer to:

ensure that the approved AIP is derived from the LDIP;

lead in identifying which plans are still relevant and which plans need to be updated based on the LGU vision and responsiveness to prevailing situation, and coordinate with the department heads concerned and sectoral committees;

properly screen the projects and prioritize those that are readily implementable and procurement ready to come up with readily feasible projects to ensure their implementation and completion;

work with the City Budget Officer in ensuring that proposed projects chargeable against the 20% DF are properly itemized in the AIP and the related information such as the specific timeline and expected output are defined in the AIP; and

collaborate with the City Engineer and City Budget Officer in planning and monitoring the utilization and inclusion of projects in the priority list to be consistent with the AIP, LDIP, and CDP.

b. City Budget Officer to:

prepare the budget document based on the approved AIP and LDIP; and

assist the CPD Officer in updating the LDIP should there be changes in the AIP.

c. City Engineer to:

conduct field survey and site investigation before endorsement to CPDO of the listing of the projects in the AIP, LDIP, and CDP; and

work with the CPDO in monitoring and inspecting the implementation of the projects under 20% Fund, and making sure that the project will be completed on the target date of completion.

City Accountant and City Treasurer to submit official receipt to support payments to contractors.

observations.reason: Please see related discussion in Part II, Finding No. 8 of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended, and the management agreed, that the City Mayor direct the:

City Engineer and City Treasurer to reconcile their records to determine the correct amount of collected Building Permit Fees and charges; and

City Treasurer to compute the 5% share of the DPWH from the correct Building Permit Fees collected from CY 2013 to 2023, and remit the same to DPWH through the Land Bank of the Philippines.

We recommended, and the management agreed, that the City Mayor direct the:
 
City Accountant to: 

ensure sufficient and complete documentation of consultancy services; 
 
apply the correct way of paying consultancy services, which is directly to individual consultant rather than through cash advance; and

comply with the withholding of 10% tax on management and technical consultants and remit the same to BIR, otherwise penalties and interests shall be borne by those responsible for the withholding and remittance of taxes.


City Human Resource and Management Officer to update the Database of the COS and furnish a copy of the same to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: Please see related discussion in Part II, Finding No. 7 of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended, and the management agreed, that the City Mayor require the:

Local School Board to formulate a strategic prioritization policy for the allocation and utilization of Special Education Fund for Projects, Programs, and Activities that are geared toward building a sturdy foundation for quality education as enumerated in the Joint Circular, and not to fund ineligible disbursements.

City Budget Officer to charge expenditures under the appropriate fund; and
 
City Treasurer to pay expenditures from the appropriate fund.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor require the:

City Treasurer and City Accountant to reconcile their respective records pertaining to the balances of RPT/SET receivables.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the:

Acting City Treasurer and other concerned offices to coordinate with the suppliers and city personnel to inform them of the unclaimed payables, otherwise provide certification to the City Accounting Office that there are no valid claimants for the long outstanding payables, to facilitate the appropriate reversion of funds.

City Accountant to:

Analyze the existence of those liabilities paid in excess of what was booked, as well as the reclassification of erroneous entries booked in the Accounts Payable account, and make the necessary adjusting entries to ensure that the accounts are accurately presented in the financial statements.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the:

City Accountant to:

enforce the immediate liquidation and/or refund of the unliquidated petty cash; 

stop the grant of additional cash advance unless the previous one has been settled, and issue certification to that effect; and

prepare adjusting entry for recording the amount in excess of the established fund.

To investigate and reconcile the unliquidated petty cash advances and prepare demand letters, if warranted; 

Accountable Officers to:

request replenishment of cash advances when disbursement reach at least 75%, and

refrain from submission of petty cash vouchers in excess of the established fund. 

We also recommended that the City Mayor designate Petty Cash Custodians who will solely handle the petty cash funds for the various offices in the City, and shall not allow the transfer of funds from the PCCs to the city officials, instead, follow the appropriate procedures in the disbursement of Petty Cash Fund.

to submit the required certification; 

direct the Department Heads to monitor and check the correctness of the data entered in the DTR of the JO and COS workers under their supervision; and 

direct the City Human Resource and Management Officer to:

monitor and update the database of JO and COS workers; and

maintain position description form of existing regular employees, and consolidate such in the Job Description Manual.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local School Board, of which the City Mayor is the chairman, formulate a strategic prioritization policy for the allocation and utilization of SEF to Projects, Programs, and Activities that are compliant with the provisions of Section 272 of RA No. 7190 and DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017.  

Also, require the immediate submission of the lacking supporting documents of the aforementioned disbursements.

observations.reason: Lacking Supporting Documents, such as Deed of Sale, etc.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended, and Management agreed, to direct the City Accountant to:

immediately require all responsible personnel to attach complete documentation to claims, and

ensure the validity as well as correctness of the transactions.  Significantly, management should strictly adhere to the policy of no payment without complete documentation.
observations.reason: Weakness in internal control

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management observe and implement the guidelines on one-time cleansing of all covered dormant accounts provided under COA Circular No. 2023-008 thereby facilitating their subsequent derecognition, in order to attain fair presentation of the Other Receivables account in the financial statements.

We recommended that management conduct the disposal proceedings necessary for those PPE items that are both fully depreciated and unserviceable, to allow fair presentation of balances in the financial statements.

Also, we recommended that management take heed and observe the updated guidelines on the disposal of government properties set by the newly-issued COA-DBM Joint Circular No. 2024-1 to facilitate systematic and timely disposal proceedings which are essential to avoid further deterioration of the properties and consequent depreciation in their value.

We recommended that management steers towards the digitalization of documents through implementing the preservation of electronic vouchers, supporting documents, and other records and reports, and their corresponding submission to the Audit Team in electronic form pursuant to existing accounting and auditing rules and regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:

a. Submit to the Audit Team all the necessary documents such as Delivery
Receipts, Acknowledgement Receipts, Contractor’s Status of Work
Accomplishments, Certificate of Inspection and Acceptance and other pertinent
documents to prove the validity of the recorded Accounts Payable, otherwise
make the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts; and

b. Refrain from recording Accounts Payable merely on the basis of Obligation
Requests and Notice of Award, instead, ensure that all relevant supporting
documents are attached to the claims.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:

a. Municipal Treasurer to prepare Schedule of Unreleased Checks monthly and
forward the same to the Municipal Accountant, in compliance with Item 2.3 of COA
GAFMIS Circular Letter No. 2002-001; and

b. Municipal Accountant to prepare a JEV at year-end to record the restoration of
unreleased checks and recognize the appropriate liability accounts to reflect the
correct balances of the affected accounts in the financial statements, in
compliance with Item 2.4 of COA GAFMIS Circular Letter No. 2002-001.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:

a. Derecognize the donated motor vehicles by debiting the Accumulated
Depreciation account by P793,107.45 and Prior Period Adjustments account by
P4,929,892.55 and crediting Motor Vehicles by P5,723,000.00, in compliance with
Paragraph 82(b) of IPSAS 17 and Paragraph 95 of IPSAS 23; and

b. Ensure completeness in accomplishing future Deeds of Donations by specifying
the execution date, to facilitate timely and accurate recording.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:

a. MGSO to furnish the Municipal Accountant with a copy of the Deed of Donation
immediately upon execution to serve as a basis for proper recording in the
books;

b. MGSO and Inventory Committee to strictly follow the preliminary activities,
ensuring complete records before inventory taking. They should also prepare a list
of PPE items recorded in the RPCPPE but not reflected in the PPELCs, and vice
versa and use this list as a basis for preparing the PIP aligned with the updated
records and RPCPPE;

c. Inventory Committee to tag all counted PPE items with property stickers,
containing complete vital information and ensure that property numbers follow
the prescribed format to ensure uniformity and facilitate easy tracking in both
PPELCs and RPCPPE; and

d. MGSO, Municipal Accountant and Inventory Committee to ensure full and timely
implementation of the cleansing process as prescribed under Commission on
Audit (COA) Circular No. 2020-006.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:

a. Record construction projects in the CIP account and the related liability account,
based on the Statement of Work Accomplished (SWA), ensuring that only completed
work is recognized, in accordance with Section 4(s) of the Manual on the New
Government Accounting System (MNGAS) for Local Government Units (LGUs),
Volume I; and

b. Present the unaccomplished portion of the construction projects as obligations not
yet due under the “timing difference” in the reconciliation of actual amounts on a
comparable basis in the SCBAA and the Statement of Financial Performance, as
presented in the Notes to Financial Statements.

b. Nine infrastructure projects totaling P1,279,630.55, have remained idle in the CIP-
Infrastructure Assets account for three to five years as of December 31, 2024,
contrary to Section 50 of MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I and Item 1.1 of COA Circular No.
2022-004, thus the accuracy of the asset recognition could not be determined.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:

a. Municipal Engineer to verify the status of the listed projects, issue a Certificate of
Completion for each project found to be completed during the inspection, and submit
it to the Municipal Accountant for proper recording; and

b. Municipal Accountant to prepare necessary adjusting journal entries to reclassify
completed projects to the appropriate PPE account and evaluate these projects in
accordance with the PPE recognition criteria and updated capitalization threshold.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to:

a. Inform in writing the concerned suppliers and contractors about their claims,
advising them that the amounts will be reverted to the unappropriated surplus of
the general fund if proper claims are not made within a reasonable period; and

b. Cause the immediate reversion of the Accounts Payable totaling P1,871,085.28 to
the unappropriated surplus of the general fund, as these payables have remained
outstanding for more than two years and were not supported with complete
documentation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal General Services
Officer (MGSO) to:

a. Ensure that all POs are completed with the necessary information as outlined in Item B of
COA Circular No. 96-010, instead of merely copying the data provided in the Purchase
Requests (PRs), to facilitate thorough evaluation and transparency in the procurement
process; and

b. Align the PO numbering with the prescribed format in Item 3 of Annex 29 of the
MNGAS for LGUs, Volume II, to ensure that POs are numbered in a continuous series
each year, allowing consistent tracking and improved procurement management.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal General Services
Officer (MGSO) to:

a. Ensure that copies of approved POs, along with all the supporting documents, are
submitted to the Office of the Auditor within five working days from execution or
issuance, in compliance with COA Circular No. 2009-001 to facilitate its timely review
and evaluation; and

b. Furnish the Audit Team with copy of delivery documents within 24 hours from
acceptance as required under COA Circular No. 2009-002 to facilitate timely conduct
of inspection of deliveries.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to:

a. Adequately plan its procurement activities in the succeeding year by conducting a
thorough planning and estimates of the supplies and materials needed for the given
period. Each department should ensure the prompt submission of their respective
Project Procurement Management Plans (PPMPs) to develop an effective procurement
strategy, which includes the consolidation of purchases with similar nature or purpose
to determine the most appropriate procurement method to be employed, and should be
incorporated into the approved Annual Procurement Plan (APP); and

b. Procure supplies and materials in bulk through competitive bidding, except in cases
explicitly allowed under the alternative modes of procurement as outlined in the RIRR
of R.A. No. 9184, to secure the most advantageous prices for the government.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to cause the
immediate submission of the lacking documents with aggregate amount of P2,767,737.11
relative to the purchase of medicines and repairs and maintenance of various service
vehicles and ensure that succeeding payments for various transactions are duly supported
with complete documentation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Budget Officer to:

a. Ensure that only programs and projects with approved and valid contracts are
obligated; and

b. Revert all unexpended balances of appropriations to the unappropriated surplus of
the general fund at year-end. However, if the unimplemented projects are intended to
be carried out in the succeeding year, incorporate their appropriations in the
continuing appropriations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to refrain
from granting cash advances for purposes other than those allowed under COA Circular
No. 97-002 to minimize the risk of loss or misuse of government funds.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and the
Municipal Budget Officer to maintain separate RAAO for each class of expenditures to
facilitate the proper monitoring of the appropriations and obligations during the year. The
RAAO maintained by both offices should be reconciled at the end of each month.

Moreover, we also recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer prepare Statement of
Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations (SAAOB) based on the RAAO. The SAAOB
should be submitted to the Municipal Accountant before finalizing the SCBAA to ensure
that accurate balances are reflected.

We recommended that Management:
a. Instruct the City Treasurer to coordinate with the known payors, especially at year-
end, for the timely issuance of Official Receipts. Otherwise, accrue the income
reflected in the reports and reverse it at the beginning of the next accounting period;
b. Direct the City Treasurer to submit immediately to the City Accounting Office
copies of the bank credit memoranda to ensure immediate recording thereof in the
books of accounts; and
c. Require the City Accountant to conduct a thorough analysis of the book reconciling
items, collate all the necessary supporting documents to substantiate the same, and
prepare Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs) to record in the books, if necessary, for a fair
presentation of the affected accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that Management direct:
a. The City Treasurer to send written notices to the payees of unclaimed checks at
least one month before a check becomes stale; and
b. The City Treasurer to cancel the unclaimed stale checks and furnish the
accountant with the original disbursement vouchers (DV) and its supporting
documents, for the preparation of the Journal Entry Voucher/s.

We recommended that the City Accountant record the corresponding expenses
related to inventory issuances in the period that they were issued or used, in accordance
with the accrual method of accounting and the perpetual inventory system.

We recommended that Management initiate the formal transfer of the
properties to the respective Barangays through the execution of a Deed of Donation
with prior approval from the Sangguniang Panlungsod and prospectively ensure
compliance with regulations on donated assets. Further, determine other properties
in the records of the City that are in the actual custody and used by other entities
(aside from barangays), and follow the same procedure in transferring these assets
to the said offices.

Henceforth, Management direct the City Accountant to prepare JEVs to
derecognize in the books the assets that were transferred and donated; and recognize
in the books of the respective barangays the carrying amount of the assets donated and
transferred to them. Further, the CGSO also derecognize these donated and
transferred assets in their property records.

We recommended that the City Accounting Office:
a. Attach to the Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs), in addition to the Obligation
Budget Request (OBRs), documentary evidence that the goods or services were
delivered to, or received by, the City that would warrant the recording of
Accounts Payable, such as but not limited to, Delivery Receipts and Inspection
and Acceptance Reports, to establish that the recorded obligations arose from
valid claims; and
b. Strengthen the accounting and processing system to ensure that financial
transactions processed and recorded are properly accomplished, dated, and
signed by approving authorities.

We recommended that Management require:
a. The City Accountant and the City General Services Officer to exhaust all
remedies to retrieve the records/reports and source documents to establish
details of the recorded lump-sum prior years’ balance, including the
entries/postings without explanations, so that the balances appearing in the PPE
Lapsing Schedule may be ascertained. Likewise, exert more effort to determine
the details of the entries/postings without explanations;
b. The City Accountant to prepare a detailed breakdown of all the PPE recorded
under the Special Education Fund for reconciliation to the inventory of PPE
and CGSO records;
c. The City Accountant and the CGSO to maintain complete, accurate, and
updated PPELCs and PCs, respectively; and
d. Both officers to reconcile the results of the physical inventory with the PCs and
PPELCs maintained by their respective offices and to effect the necessary
adjustments for any discrepancy noted.

We reiterated the prior year’s recommendations that Management:
a. Direct the City Accountant, in coordination with the City Engineer, to
conduct a thorough analysis and reconciliation of the CIP accounts.
Completed projects shall be closed to the appropriate PPE accounts. Any
unpaid amount due to a delay in the submission of the billing by the contractor
shall be recognized as payable if it is still feasible under existing rules and
regulations;
b. Direct the City Accountant to reconcile those with negative and unusual debit
balances due to an error in recording and make an adjusting entry to the CIP
account and other related accounts, such as the Advances to Contractors
account;
c. Direct the City Engineer to provide the City Accountant and City General
Services Officer with a copy of the Certificate of Project Completion of the
completed projects, to serve as their basis in recording completed projects as
PPE. Periodic depreciation shall likewise be provided from the time the
project is completed and available for use to allow fair presentation of the
affected accounts in the year-end financial statements;
d. Require the City Accountant to disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements
under the Construction in Progress account the balance of the projects that
were not completed and discontinued; and
e. Require the City Accounting Office to maintain the appropriate CIPLC for
each specific type of asset constructed, whether through a straight contract
or by administration.

We reiterated the prior year’s recommendations that Management:
a. Direct the City Accountant and CGSO to retrieve the records/reports and
source documents that would establish details of the irreconcilable items in
the SL and RPCPPE and determine the adjustments that would be recorded
in the books;
b. Conduct a comprehensive inventory and reconciliation of all its real
properties and ascertain which are covered with OCTs/TCTs;
c. Instruct the City Accountant to record in the books the land which was
included in the RPCPPE. Likewise, instruct the Inventory Team and the
CGSO to update the RPCPPE and records, respectively, for the Land
recorded in the books;
d. Further determine the fair market values of the two lots with a total area of
14,923.00 square meters which were included in the submitted RPCPPE but
not recorded in the books, and the five lots included in the CTO inventory of
land owned by the LGU and with title under the name of the City as basis for
recording in the books of accounts;
e. Instruct the City Assessor to further investigate and establish the proof of
ownership of the LGU on the 181 lots with a total area of 915,943.00 square
meters which currently only have tax declaration in the name of the City as
the supporting document by verifying whether a record exists in the Registry
of Deeds where the properties are located and afterward, check if the lots
have a decree with the Land Registration Authority.
f. For titled properties, but the original copies of the OCTs/TCTs could not be
located, initiate the process to reconstitute the titles;
g. For untitled properties, conduct cadastral surveys and expedite the
processing of the titles in the name of the City; and
h. Instruct the City Accounting Office and the CGSO to maintain complete
subsidiary records based on the inventory conducted and perform regular
reconciliation of the balances.

We recommended that Management instruct the City Accountant and the City
Treasurer to evaluate and determine the cause/s of the unrecorded reconciling
items, record the necessary adjusting entries after a thorough verification and
analysis has been made thereof, and collate the necessary supporting
documents to substantiate the same for a fair presentation of the affected
accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that Management direct the City Accountant to prepare a
reconciliation statement with updated bank balance and prepare Journal Entry
Vouchers (JEVs) for the required adjustments.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendations that Management:
a. Submit to the Audit Team the liquidation report or proof of refund/s, if there
are any, of the outstanding cash advances amounting to ₱531,084.36 as of
December 31, 2024, for post-audit;
b. Direct the City Accounting Office to issue demand letters for employees with
unliquidated cash advances beyond their prescribed liquidation period and,
henceforth, withhold the salaries and/or any amount due to those Accountable
Officers who were unable to comply with the pertinent rules and regulations on
cash advances; and
c. Require all Accountable Officers to settle their respective cash advances within
the prescribed period to reflect the correct balances of the accounts affected in
the books of accounts of the Agency at year-end.

We recommended that Management fast-track the cleansing of the dormant
receivables in the books of the agency, in accordance with COA Circular 2023-008 to
ensure the correct presentation of the receivable accounts in the financial
statements.
We further recommended that the City Accountant prepare and submit the
quarterly Schedule of Dormant Accounts to the Audit Team and ensure that
receivables under ten years are properly documented and promptly addressed to
prevent them from becoming dormant.

We commended the Inventory Committee for substantially completing the
inventory taking of the inventories and the City Accounting Office for reconciling
their records with the said Committee, resulting in the reconciled balance of the
inventory accounts except for the Accountable Forms, Plates and Stickers, and
‘Agricultural and Marine Supplies for Distribution’.
However, we recommended that Management:
a. Direct the CGSO, through the Inventory Committee, and the City Accounting
Office to complete the inventory taking, and conduct reconciliation of their
respective records, supplies and materials with the RPCI, SLCs, and SCs;
b. Require the Inventory Committee to timely prepare and submit the
corresponding RPCI semestrally and provide the required details in the report
before submission to the Audit Team for verification; and
c. Direct the City Accounting Office to maintain a complete and updated Supplies
Ledger Card for each inventory item, ensuring that it includes all necessary
information relevant to the inventory items, and take up adjustments needed to
ensure that the Inventory accounts are properly stated in the Financial
Statements.

We recommended that Management:
a. Direct the City Accountant to determine the validity of the unliquidated
obligations amounting to ₱1,344,194.78, which are outstanding for more than
two years, by verifying through confirmation to prove the existence and
validity of these liability accounts. Moreover, if the creditors of these
recorded payables were found to have no legitimate claims against the City,
cause the reversion of the same to the unappropriated surplus by preparing
the necessary journal entry voucher (JEV) as basis for recording in the books
of the City; and
b. Require the City Budget Officer to ensure that only those claims for goods
and services delivered/rendered and supported with proper documentation
are obligated and recognized as payables.

We recommended that Management instruct the City Accountant to recognize
the accrued interest expenses by making the necessary adjusting entries by
debiting Prior Period Adjustment (for Interest Expense) and crediting Interest
Payable accounts by ₱246,721.31, and henceforth, strictly observe the accrual
basis of accounting in recording such expenses to ensure the correct account
balances at the end of the reporting period.

We recommended that Management require the City Accountant to maintain
complete subsidiary ledgers for both the Due to GOCCs and Due to LGUs
accounts for all funds to support the reported balances in the financial
statements.

We recommended that the Management direct the said AO to prepare and
maintain a cashbook to facilitate reconciliation of the cash advances in the books
of accounts.

We recommended that Management reassess the accountabilities of the
Accountable Officers and file an application to increase the fidelity bond of those
whose maximum accountabilities have already increased to ensure adequate
protection of government funds in compliance with the Revised Schedule of
Premium rates or Annex C of Treasury No. 02-2019.

We recommended that Management:
a. Consider hiring additional staff in the City Accounting Office to address the
voluminous transactions of the City Government; and
b. Direct the City Accountant to strictly comply with the timelines on recording
and also submission of accounts to the Auditor within ten days of the ensuing
month.

We reiterated the prior year’s recommendations that Management exert its best
effort to implement all programmed projects under the 20% Economic Development
Fund during the current year, to ensure that its objectives of desirable socio-economic
and environmental outcomes will be achieved.
Further, we recommended that Management expedite the implementation/
completion of the 39 ongoing projects and the 33 unimplemented CY 2024 EDF
projects in the calendar year 2025.

We recommended that the Management provide the Audit Team copies of the
perfected contracts for infrastructure projects and its complete supporting
documents within five working days from execution to allow for a systematic,
timely, and effective review process.

We recommended that Management submit a list of all ongoing government
projects/programs/activities and those that are to be implemented in CY 2024, and
submit the same at the beginning of each year.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive inform the Office of the
Supervising Auditor and the Audit Team Leader within ten days after the award of
the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity of any
postings of project signboards and/or public notice for validation by the Audit
Team.

We recommended that the City Planning and Development Office furnish the
Regional Offices of the DILG and DBM copies of the Annual Investment Program
immediately after its approval to enable the oversight agencies to create/maintain
the necessary database and to monitor the utilization of the 20 percent
Development Fund.

We reiterated that Management:
