We recommended that the Sanggunian Bayan create and/or adopt the establishment of the Disability Affairs Officer by duly filling-up the positions of the Disability Affairs Officer following the qualification standards, position titles and salary grades set by RA No. 10070 subject to the qualification requirements as approved by the CSC.
Management’s Comment:
The Management will create the PDAO organizational structure and staffing pattern for approval by the Sangguniang Bayan. With this, when the basis of funding of position is established, it will be prioritized for supplemental budget for CY 2025.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to provide the details or breakdown of Other Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses (MOOE), Other Receipts and Other Expenses as of December 31, 2023 and ensure that supporting information for the items presented on the face of the statements of financial position, statement of financial performance, statement of changes in net assets/equity, or cash flow statement were adequately disclosed in the Notes to Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: The proper disclosures for the accounts presented in the Financial Statements have been included in the Notes to the Financial Statements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a. Municipal Accountant to reclassify the 30 completed infrastructure projects totaling P10,147,175.19 to their appropriate PPE accounts and provide the same with corresponding depreciation expense; b. Inventory Committee to validate the existence and conditions of the above-mentioned projects and correctly report the same in the Report on Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE); and c. Municipal Engineer to furnish the Municipal Accountant with the list of the completed and on-going projects as basis in the transfer of completed projects to its proper PPE accounts.
observations.managementAction: The 30 completed infrastructure projects were already reclassified to their appropriate PPE accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to draw a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to derecognize the above-mentioned properties using the following entry: Debit: Prior Period Adjustment P820,346.44 Accumulated Depreciation P1,455,586.56 Credit: Property, Plant and Equipment P2,275,933.00; b. Municipal General Services Officer (MGSO) to remove the noted 69 semi-expendable properties from the RPCPPE; and c. MGSO and the Municipal Accountant to validate the existence of the recorded other infrastructure asset totalling P198,540.96. In addition, to continue the periodic reconciliation of their records and make the necessary corrections or adjustments to the discrepancies noted between their records.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has prepared a JEV for derecognizing the mentioned properties using the following entry: Debit: Prior Period Adjustment P820,346.44 Accumulated Depreciation P1,455,586.56 Credit: Property, Plant and Equipment P2,275,933.00. The noted semi-expendable properties have been removed from the RPCPPE.
observations.reason: A discrepancy of P2,754,825.03 was noted between the accounting books and RPCPPE as of December 31, 2024 due to the non-conduct of periodic reconciliation and non-implementation of one-time cleansing of PPE accounts. No actions were taken.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to: a. Ensure that all the procurement is done through competitive bidding except in those cases allowed under the alternative mode as provided for in RA No. 9184; and b. Require the suppliers to submit the noted lacking documents such as mayor/business' permit and proof of registration with Philippine Government Electronic Procurement System (PhilGEPS). Also ensure that all the transactions were duly supported with BAC resolution recommending and justifying to the Head of Procuring Entity (HOPE) the use of alternative mode of procurement. Moreover, that the Municipal Accountant ensure that succeeding payments for procurements are supported with complete documentary requirements as prescribed under the provisions of RA No. 9184 to avoid suspension or disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction: To promote efficiency and cost-effectiveness, the BAC will direct all departments to consolidate purchases of similar nature, such as meals for programmed activities, office supplies, various equipment and materials, medicines and furniture and fixtures, in accordance with the respective approved APP. Furthermore, the BAC will conduct bidding as primary mode of procurement, in adherence to the provisions of RA No. 9184 and its RIRR. The use of negotiated procurement shall be limited to cases permitted by law and only for items and services essential to the functions and operations of the LGU. This approach will reinforce fair competition among suppliers and optimize the use of public funds.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management prioritize and consider filling-up the vacant positions by promoting deserving employees among the present pool of manpower resources with exemplary performance and hiring new employees for lower-level positions to further improve its services, provide equal job opportunities to the community and attain the full implementation of programs, plans and activities which will in turn benefit the Municipality as a whole.
observations.managementAction: The filling of vacant positions has been prioritized by the Municipality for CY 2024.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the concerned officials to: a. Exercise tighter control over the use of government vehicles through properly accomplished and duly serially numbered Driver’s Trip Tickets, a summary of which shall be made at the end of the month in a Monthly Report of Official Travels for audit purposes; b. Ensure that the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption and Monthly Report of Official Travels are submitted to the Office of the Auditor for verification purposes; and c. Submission of justification or explanation on why fuel was given to privately owned vehicles out of the funds of the Municipality and ensure that only government-owned motor vehicles are issued with gasoline or official business.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor has instructed to exercise tighter control and the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption and Monthly Report of Official Travels are submitted to the Office of the Auditor. For CY 2024, only government-owned motor vehicles were issued gasoline.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and other concerned officials to submit immediately the lacking documents for the transactions with aggregate amount of P10,899,894.39 to the Office of the Auditor and ensure that succeeding payments for infrastructure transactions are supported with complete documentation as prescribed under the provisions of Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 to avoid suspension or disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction: The required supporting documents for the 11 infrastructure projects were submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Human Resource Management Officer (HRMO) to coordinate with the GSIS to update the payroll of employees in the GSIS system and that the Municipal Treasurer remit the outstanding balance of P97,081.79 and ensure that all the amount withheld are remitted intact within the first 10 days of the calendar month following the month to which the contributions apply.
observations.managementAction: The HRMO has coordinated with the GSIS to update the payroll of employees in the GSIS system. The outstanding balance of P97,081.79 has already been remitted to the GSIS. Moreover, all withheld amounts have been remitted intact within the first 10 days of the calendar month following the month to which the contributions apply.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Treasurer require the Liquidating Officer to:
3) Validate thoroughly the remittances made by the Collectors to avoid the under or over remittance of their collections; and
4) Check carefully the appropriate depository banks/fund of the collections to avoid the error of inadvertently depositing collections to another account.

We recommended that Management:
5) Require the Accountable Officers to liquidate/settle their outstanding cash advances without delay by submitting the duly accomplished liquidation reports on or before the prescribed period; and
6) Instruct the City Accountant to issue demand letters to concerned officers and employees who incurred delays in the settlement.
observations.managementAction: The management will issue demand letters to Accountable Officers signed by the City Accountant and noted by the City Mayor with emphasis on strict compliance.
observations.reason: Similar deficiency on current year transactions, observed in CY 2024. Observations and recommendations are included in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
12) Direct the City Accountant to coordinate with the CEO in determining the actual status of all reported on-going projects and to effect the necessary adjustment in the books of accounts based on the result of validation in order to reflect the accurate balance of the CIP accounts;
13) Instruct the City Accountant to ensure that all completed and paid projects are immediately classified to their appropriate PPE accounts and to compute for the required depreciation thereof; and
14) Observe proper planning and coordination to avoid complications during the project implementation and ensure that infrastructure projects are completed on time.
observations.managementAction: The City Accounting Office, in coordination with the CEO, has already prepared the adjustments for those 19 completed projects from the Construction-in-Progress and reclassified them to appropriate PPE accounts.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
15) Establish a policy in the capitalization of cost of repairs and other subsequent costs of PPE which will be uniformly adopted by CAO, CGSO, CEO, CBO and Inventory Committee in compliance to the relative provisions of MNGAS for LGUs Vol. III.
16) Direct the CAO, in coordination with the CGSO and CEO, to further evaluate the qualification of the existing capitalized cost of repairs in their records and thereafter, prepare adjusting entries for the derecognition of those which do not meet the capitalization requirements using the following pro-forma entry:
Account Debit Credit
Prior Years Adjustment xx
Accumulated Depreciation – PPE Account xx
PPE Account xx
observations.managementAction: The City Accounting Office in coordination with the GSO & CEO have already re-evaluated the nature of repair of five (5) projects and made the necessary adjustments.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the City Accountant to determine the validity of the unliquidated obligations or Accounts Payable amounting to ₱12,686,034.57 which are outstanding for more than two years and if found to have no legitimate claim against the City, cause the reversion of the same to the unappropriated surplus.
observations.managementAction: The City Accounting Office has already done cleansing of the long outstanding obligations in the amount of ₱12,686,034.57.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management review the Memorandum of Agreements or coordinate with the source agency to determine the correct disposition of each fund transfer, otherwise revert the unutilized balances to the source agency or the Bureau of Treasury, whichever is appropriate, in accordance with existing rules and regulations.
Also, we recommended that Management coordinate with the source agency of the prizes they received regarding the conditions of the use of the said prizes. In the absence of any conditions for its use, treat the prizes as outright income to fund appropriate PPAs of recipient departments/divisions.
observations.managementAction: Coordination was made between the concerned city offices and NGAs to facilitate the fund transfer and reversion of unutilized balances to the source agencies. The Fund transfers for the barangay augmentation fund were supported by Memorandum of Agreement between the LGU and the recipient Barangays.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require all transfer of funds to barangays for the implementation of projects and programs, be covered by a MOA and other documentary requirements as enumerated in Section 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 to establish proper accountability and monitoring of the proper utilization of funds.
observations.managementAction: Fund transfers for the barangay augmentation fund were already supported by Memorandum of Agreement between the LGU and the recipient Barangays.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the wearing of personal protective equipment of all workers in the Sanitary Landfill Facility, specifically those directly exposed to the wastes/garbage and performing the composting process of the compostable waste to minimize exposure to hazards that can cause serious workplace illness. Furthermore, conduct strict monitoring of areas in the City where indiscriminate dumping of wastes is observed and enforce regulations including imposition of penalties for non-compliance. Also, instruct the CENRO to regularly monitor the landfilling activity in the Sanitary Landfill to avoid uncovered garbage/waste; provide additional heavy equipment for use in the moving, compacting and landfilling the garbage/waste delivered every day in the facility; and schedule regular maintenance service of the heavy equipment to avoid disruption in the activities, particularly landfilling, in the SLF.
observations.managementAction: The Management will direct the OCENR to provide and require the wearing of PPEs to all workers in the SLF; conduct strict monitoring of indiscriminate dumping of wastes; regularly monitor the landfill activity to avoid uncovered garbage; provide additional heavy equipment; and schedule regular maintenance of heavy equipment.

observations.recommendation: No specific recommendation beyond compliance noted.
observations.managementAction: The Management complied with the Property Insurance Law or RA No. 656, as amended by PD No. 245 on the insurance of all insurable assets and interests of the government with the Government Service Insurance System (GSIS).

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive requires the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entry to reflect the valid reconciling items in the books of accounts.
We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive to instruct the MA to:  

 Continue the preparation and submission of BRS within 10 days upon receipt of the BS;  
 
 Immediately prepare correcting/adjusting entries for discrepancies/errors or other reconciling items requiring corrections in order to reflect the correct balance of cash account in the financial statements; and  

 Immediately record all unrecorded unreleased checks as credit to the CIB, LCCA account.  Henceforth, all checks issued shall be recorded in the CkDJ on the date of the check.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to conduct immediate reconciliation of the individual balances of the Cash-in Local Treasury account with the pertinent Cashbooks and to make the necessary adjustments thereon.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to record the DRRM Funds in the Special Trust Fund Books under the Trust Liability – DRRM account.
We recommended that the management require the Municipal Accountant to submit the required reports to the audit team pursuant to the aforecited regulations.

We recommended that the Implementing Office to fast track the implementation of the on-going projects so that the target beneficiaries could avail of the benefits of the 	programs and improve the living and economic condition of the constituents of the 	Municipality.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the:

MDRRM Council to monitor properly the utilization of LDDRM Fund;

MDRRM Council to use the unexpended and excess balances of the LDRRMF by incorporating these in the LDRRMFIP and submit the same, including supplemental Investment Programs, if any, to the AT within the prescribed period; and

LDRRMO and the MAO to coordinate properly so that accurate figures will be incorporated in the LDRRMFIP.
observations.reason: Reiterate in the Part II of AAR

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LDRRM Council (LDRRMC) coordinate with the Municipal Budget Office so that accurate figures are included in the supplemental LDRRMF Budget. 

Also, we reiterated our recommendation that the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management (LDRRM) Council to optimize the utilization of the LDRRMF of the LGU in order to strengthen its disaster preparedness and avoid potential damages during disasters.
We recommended that the GAD Focal Person submit the CY 2023 GAD Plan and Budget as well as GAD Accomplishment Report to the Audit Team for evaluation and verification.

observations.reason:
Reiterate in the Part II of AAR

Due to manpower deficiency in the respective offices.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Petty Cash Custodian ensure that the PCF balance is maintained at a level where replenishment could be made before the fund is fully used, such as the processing of replenishment as soon as the total disbursements thereof reaches 75 per cent.
Require the concerned personnel to submit the government contracts, purchase orders and the supporting documents within five days after perfection pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-001.
Require the Municipal Engineering Office to monitor the implementation of the projects by the Barangays and provide the necessary assistance for the timely completion thereof.

Require the Municipal Accountant to properly record the fund transfers to the recipient barangay for the implementation of various infrastructure projects and the liquidation thereof, in accordance with COA Circular No. 2015-009; require all fund transfers to Barangays to be covered by a Memorandum of Agreement and other documentary requirements; require the concerned Barangay Officials to immediately submit the Liquidation Reports of the previously downloaded amount to them.
Require the Sangguniang Bayan to comply with the prescribed legislation as regards the realignment of funds to ensure the regularity of expenditures thereon.

Re-assess the DRRM plan and the period of implementation of programs, projects and activities in the LDRRMF Investment Plan; incorporate in the LDRRMFIP the projects to be sourced from previous years’ unexpended balance and donations together with the current year’s allocation; require the MDRRMO and Municipal Accountant to closely coordinate in monitoring and utilization of the LDRRMF.

Require the Municipal Accountant to strictly observe the preparation and submission of the required reports in its proper format (SEF Budget Accountability Form No. 1) and within the prescribed period (on or before February 15).
Reconcile and trace the negative balance in the Due to BIR account to correct said balance.

We recommended that Management require the Accounting Office to apply generally accepted accounting practice in the recording of Inventories, that is credit the account upon issuance to end-users, transfers, write down or other disposal.

We recommended that Management:

Submit the Municipality’s duly accomplished Property Inventory Form (PIF) to the Audit Team Leader and the GIF of GSIS on or before April 30 of each year to be used as basis for the assessment of the general insurance coverage; and

Insure with the GIF of GSIS the Municipality’s insurable assets and properties for indemnification in case of damage or loss due to fire, earthquake, storm or other fortuitous events.

We recommended Management to require the Bids and Awards Committee to conduct pre-procurement conference for Public Bidding of Goods costing above ₱2,000,000.00, Infrastructure Projects costing above ₱5,000,000.00, and Consulting Services costing above ₱1,000,000.00 in accordance with Section 20 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: The minutes on pre-procurement conference were still not attached to the disbursement vouchers, thus, the conduct thereof could not be established.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to:

Deduct the appropriate retention money from first progress billings for infrastructure projects in accordance with Section 6 of Annex E of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184; and

Ensure that it shall only be released upon final acceptance (that is one year after completion) of the project or upon the request of the contractor to substitute the same with a valid financial instrument.

Observe the preparation and submission of the required reports in its proper format (SEF Budget Accountability Form No. 1) and within the prescribed period (on or before February 15) to give ample time in the verification of the allocation and utilization of the Special Education Fund; and

Continue to maximize the utilization of its SEF in order to deliver its services to the intended beneficiaries.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management expedite the implementation of the unutilized balance of the LGSF-SBDP-NTF-ELCAC in the ensuing year, amounting to ₱96,730,198.93 to address the existing issues and development gaps in their barangays.
We recommended that Management stop the practice of prematurely issuing certificate of acceptance and comply with the timeline set to maximize the one-year defects liability period.

We recommended that management require the contractors to have the materials tested to laboratories duly accredited by DOST/BRS and submit the results thereof to ensure the quality of the materials to be used for infrastructure projects, and the Municipal Engineer to ensure compliance thereof.

We recommended that the LCE provide justification why the mandatory requirement for a CPES is not observed.  Henceforth, we enjoin LCE to revisit the provisions as provided under Section 13 of the Annex “E” of the R.A. No. 9184 and its IRR on the evaluation of contractor’s performance.  Further, that concerned department representatives be convened to discuss on the subject matter to ascertain commitment/s in terms of agency resources towards conformance to the mandatory requirement for a CPES and its necessary impact to the operations and structure of the LGU.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) determine and recommend to the Local Chief Executive the appropriate mode of procurement or alternative method of procurement to be used based on the prevailing conditions as cited under the IRR of RA 9184.

We recommended that the Management:

Require the submission of liquidation report and complete supporting documents with pertinent details within the required period after completion of the travel prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012-001, otherwise, unsupported claims shall not be allowed. Also, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant verify and thoroughly review supporting documents of claims to ensure that only valid claims will be allowed; and

Direct the Municipal Accountant to only allow for daily travelling expenses (DTE) in accordance with E.O. No. 77, to avoid overspending and incurrence of excessive and unnecessary expenses. Likewise, meal component of the DTE should not be claimed if the registration fee already includes for the full-board meals; 

Restrict grant of DTE only to those days which are necessary in the conduct of business; and 

Adhere to the guidelines of DBM NBC No. 563 dated April 22, 2016 for the allowable payment for registration fee for trainings/seminars organized by non-government organizations and private institutions.

No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 89-296.

(14) We commended Management for the substantial implementation of the projects under the 20% Development Fund and recommend that it continue to improve practices in programming, prioritizing and implementing programs and projects to maximize the utilization of the Fund in attaining desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes for the benefit of the community.

(15) We commended Management for appropriately transferring the unexpended balance of the 5% LDRRMF for CY 2023 to the Special Trust Fund in compliance to Section 21 of RA 10121 and Items 5.1.10 and 5.1.13 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

coordinate with the project coordinators for the submission of MOA and other pertinent documents related to the utilization of fund for proper filing and documentation; and

City Treasurer to:

immediately secure the SARO/NCA and respective MOA/MOU for all fund transfers from National Government, and provide the Audit Team a copy;
 
City Planning and Development Officer to:

prepare a revised implementation schedule of programs, projects and activities;

We recommended the following:

The Municipal Treasurer provide the Municipal Accountant the certified list of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible from them as required by Section 20 of the Manual of NGAS, Volume 1;

The Municipal Accountant maintain SLs based on the certified list of taxpayers provided by the Municipal Treasurer to support the reported RPT/SET Receivable control accounts; and

Henceforth, the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant update both their records of any changes in the list of taxpayers to reflect realistic RPT/SET receivables and collections.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant identify the discrepancies between the reciprocal accounts, Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds, reconcile the same, and effect the necessary adjustments, as warranted, to reflect the correct account balances for fair presentation of accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended to management the following:
Direct the Municipal Health Officer to inform and/or report to the GSO and Municipal Accountant all the receipt and distribution of donated vaccines and medicines;
Create an Appraisal Committee to assess the value of these vaccines and medicines still on hand; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant to record the same in the books by debiting Drugs and Medicines for Distribution and crediting Grants and Donations in Kind accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reclassify the previously classified PPE items with carrying amounts from ₱15,000.00 to below ₱50,000.00 as semi-expendable property, subject to the computation of the corresponding impairment. Secondly, tangible items to be acquired in the ensuing years shall be properly recorded as semi-expandable property.

We also recommended that the GSO use the new forms, registries, and reports prescribed in the Circular, where necessary, to strengthen controls over the semi-expandable items.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer adopt necessary control measures in the handling and deposit of cash collections so as not to expose the scarce government monies to the risk of possible loss and misuse by establishing a cut-off period for collections at the end of the year to give ample time to deposit the same within the year.

We recommended the following:

The Local Chief Executive to enjoin the department head’s compliance with the requirements of the circular and direct the Municipal Engineer to provide the local accountant and the general services officer with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects;

That thereafter, the Municipal Accountant and General Services officer to prepare and maintain all required reports, records and ledgers as mentioned in COA Circular No. 2015-008; and

Lastly, the Municipal Accountant to revisit the Infrastructure Assets Accounts, ascertain the estimated useful life used for each component of the asset, determine the proper valuation of the local road assets and compute depreciation to arrive at the correct book value of each component as of December 31, 2023.

We recommended that the Budget Officer properly assess and validate the charging of PPAs to the LDRRMF and ensure that such expenditures are in accordance with the policies and guidelines provided under NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC JMC No. 2014-1 to efficiently utilize the funds and to do away with PAPs that are not related to disaster risk reduction management activities.

We recommended to Management that the Gender and Development (GAD) Focal Point System endorse to the Committee on Family, Women, Children and Elderly of the SB, the passing of an ordinance localizing RA No. 11313, which if passed, shall be posted in conspicuous places. Further, include in the ordinance the following: 
a) measures to prevent gender-based sexual harassment in educational institutions, b) imposition of fines/penalty for violation thereof, and c) creation of sexual harassment hotlines.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant for CY 2023 and onwards prepare and submit on time the BRS for a timely verification and audit of the accounts and stop the practice of preparing the latter by batches.

We recommended the following to management:

Require the Municipal Accountant to submit the Monthly Trial Balance within the 20th day after the end of the month and the Quarterly FS to enable the Auditor to conduct a timely verification and audit of the accounts of the Municipality otherwise the sanctions as provided in Section 122 of P.D. 1445 be imposed; and

Submit justification for failure to submit the required above-mentioned reports.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant revisit the Infrastructure Assets Accounts particularly Road Network, Sewer System and Water Supply Systems, ascertain the estimated useful life used for each component of the asset, and compute the depreciation to arrive at the correct book value of each component as of December 31, 2022.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the MDRRMO to refrain from using the five percent LDRRMF to pay various operating expenses of the MDRRMO and other expenses not related to the purpose of the fund.
observations.managementAction: No action was implemented by the management.
observations.reason:
The same Programs, Projects and Activities (PPAs) were included in the CY 2023 Annual Budget thus, disbursements totaling P1,433,845.00 which were not directly related to the four thematic areas of disaster risk reduction management were still charged against the LDRRMF of the Municipality for CY 2023.
Reiterated in Part II, Audit Observation No. 9, page nos. 42-46

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management prioritize and consider filling-up the vacant positions by promoting deserving employees among the present pool of manpower resources with exemplary performance, allowing the entry of new applicants for lower-level positions, in compliance with the existing guidelines and rules and regulations of the CSC to attain full implementation of the program, plans, and activities which will in turn benefit the Municipality as a whole.
observations.managementAction: No action was implemented by the management.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II, Audit Observation No. 6, page nos. 37-39

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management direct the GSO to record the unserviceable properties in the Inventory and Inspection Report of the Unserviceable Property for proper disposal and, after which, the Municipal Accountant to drop the unserviceable items from the books of account.
observations.managementAction: GSO to record the unserviceable properties in the IIRUP, conduct proper disposal with the disposal committee and require the Municipal Accountant to drop the unserviceable properties from the books
observations.reason: Disposal procedures not yet started due to the busy schedule of the disposal committee.

observations.managementAction: The GSO is yet to comply with the preparation of SSM based on RIS, they are expediting the process and commits to submit by CY 2024.

observations.reason: Reiterated in CY 2023 Audit

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management submit henceforth, copies of all perfected government contracts involving acquisition of land including all supporting documents within five working days from execution pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-001 to facilitate timely auditorial and technical review thereof.

Further, for Management to provide a copy of the DENR Clearance and CAR to the Audit Team considering that considerable length of time has already passed from its payment. Also, to apprise the Audit Team of the status of transfer of title in the name of the LGU for this land acquisition.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct:

The Municipal Treasurer to provide the Municipal Accountant with the List of Delinquent Market Stallholders with amount due and penalties before the end of each year to facilitate timely recording of appropriate receivable and income accounts; and

The Municipal Accountant to recognize the total unpaid rentals, business taxes, surcharges and interests as of December 31, 2022 in the books of the Municipality to the appropriate receivable and income/equity account, in compliance with the provisions of PPSAS 13 in order to present fairly the financial statements of the Municipality.
observations.reason: Reiterated in CY 2023 Audit

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Require the Municipal Human Resource Management Office to assess the existing organizational structure so that the ideal number of plantilla personnel will be established and submit a report with recommendation thereon to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) for approval by appropriate bodies;

Create policies and guidelines to address the necessity and propriety of hiring JOs taking into consideration the existing plantilla personnel; and

Require applicants to submit primary personal identification, undergo an interview and evaluate the performance of each personnel that would give assurance that the risks identified above are addressed.

We reiterated our prior years’ recommendations that Management:

Stop the practice of giving different rates to JOs having the same position in the LGU in order not to create inequality in the workforce of the LGU; and

Require the HRMO to adopt a schedule of standard rates for JOs per position that is based on qualification and the nature of work/assignment taking into consideration the prevailing minimum wage in the locality.
We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipality stop the hiring of street sweepers as they perform the same services already being rendered by the barangay street sweepers.

We recommended that Management maximize the utilization of the LDRRMF to cover all the four thematic areas of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management in order to enhance the preparedness of the Municipality against disasters and avoid, lessen or limit their adverse impacts to the community.
observations.reason: Reiterated in CY 2023 Audit
Reiterated in CY 2023 Audit
Reiterated in CY 2023 Audit

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
 Require the concerned Accountable Officer to submit the accounts and financial reports to the Municipal Treasurer pursuant to the timelines of the abovementioned existing law, rules and regulations; 

 Direct the Municipal Treasurer to submit to the Municipal Accounting Office all the disbursements vouchers, payrolls, official receipts, including the corresponding reports for each month on or before the fifth day of the ensuing month or pursuant to the abovementioned pertinent provisions of rules and regulations, as applicable

 Direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly comply with the timelines on the recording and submission of accounts to the Auditor within ten days of the ensuing month; and 

 Identify and address the cause/s for the poor performance in the submission of monthly financial reports/accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant continue the timely submission of the year-end financial statements, together with its supporting schedules, and continue to improve her performance in the submission of the monthly trial balances and the quarterly financial statements to enable the timely verification thereof by the Auditor.
observations.managementAction: Management will continue to improve its process to fast track submission.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this AAR

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BAC, in coordination with the Municipal Engineer to submit the required Procurement Monitoring Report on a semestral basis pursuant to Section 12.2 of the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 and GPPB Circular 03-2015 dated March 20, 2015.

We also recommended that Management continue its practice of:
 Requiring the Municipal Accountant and Property Officer to reconcile the discrepancies noted between the accounting records and the RPCPPE and effect the necessary adjustments to come up with the correct balances.

 Henceforth, require both officials to maintain and regularly update the PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards to facilitate reconciliation of the account balances.

We recommended that the Management:
 Require the BAC to strictly comply with the procurement guidelines and procedures prescribed in the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184;

 Enjoin the BAC to meticulously plan their procurement to avoid incongruency in procurement period coverage

 Submit justification for the Audit Team’s evaluation as to why the said transaction should not be disallowed in audit.

We recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan review, revise and amend the existing Local Revenue Code of the Municipality.  

 We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Assessor and other concerned Department Heads to prepare and submit to the Sangguniang Bayan (SB), for approval, the proposed adjustments on tax rates to increase revenue collections.

We recommended that the municipality strive to improve its efficiency on the utilization of the 20 per cent EDF and to fully implement the priority PPAs, in order to attain the desired outcomes on the socio-economic development that will benefit the intended beneficiaries. 

 We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer and Municipal Planning and Development Officer to hasten, monitor and prioritize the implementation and completion of the remaining Social and Economic Development Projects under the 20 per cent EDF.

We recommended that Management adhere strictly with the provision of Item 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 and submit regularly to the Audit Team the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF, prepared by the Municipal Accountant using the proposed format by the Audit Team in “Annex A”, on or before the 15th day after the end of each month through the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council and the Municipal Development Council.

We recommended to the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management (LDRRM) Officer to optimize the utilization of the LDRRMF of the LGU in order to strengthen its disaster preparedness and avoid potential damages during disasters. 

 Likewise, to address the gaps in DRRM planning, implementation and monitoring of the programs, projects and activities, we recommend close coordination among LGU officials and other DRRM officer to ensure effective formulation and implementation of the DRR Projects as listed in the LDRRMFIP.

We reiterated our recommendation that Management, through the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) facilitate the full establishment of the GAD database in order to have a basis for planning and programming PPAs and formulating policies that are gender-responsive and to be able to undertake thorough gender analysis based on relevant data to properly identify existing gender issues and ensure that the approved PPAs to be incorporated in the GAD Plan and Budget are gender responsive.
We recommended that Management fully accomplish the PPAs identified in the Work and Financial Plan of the LCPC in order to deliver the vital services to the children of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Management to ensure a more efficient and strategic utilization of the appropriated Special Education Fund and implement immediately the identified/programmed in the Local School Board Budget.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to conduct immediate reconciliation of the individual balances of the Cash-in Bank account with the pertinent Cashbooks and the Cash-in Bank accounts and to make the necessary adjustments thereon.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to review and take appropriate steps to collect the dormant receivables of the LGU. Thereafter, request for authority to write-off all accounts deemed uncollectible, in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

 Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant to conduct regular monitoring and analysis of receivables to ensure that these are collected timely when the accounts become due and demandable.

We recommended that Management require concerned officials to submit copies of Contracts/POs to the Auditor, with the required supporting documents attached thereto, within five days after perfection thereof, and the related Notification of Delivery of Services/Supplies and Materials, within 24 hours after acceptance of such deliveries of services/supplies and materials, in compliance with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2009-001 and COA Circular 96-010.

We reiterated our recommendation to the Management to:

Furnish the Audit Team, at the beginning of the year, a list of on-going and programs, projects and activities that will be implemented during the year.

Submit to the Audit Team the Monthly Monitoring Report on the Status of Programs, Projects and Activities within ten days after the end of each month.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all cash advances for payment of wages of job orders for the calendar year 2021 and henceforth, be duly supported with Approved Job Order Contracts (every new contract and/or renewal of contract) and Daily Time Records.

Further, we recommended that Management direct the Disbursing Officer to ensure that all liquidation of cash advances for payment of wages of job orders for calendar year 2021 and henceforth, be duly supported with duly verified/accepted accomplishment report, pursuant to specific sections of PD 1445 and COA Circular 2012-001.

observations.managementAction: Management has noted the observation accordingly and commit to improve our compliance with the prescribed guidelines in granting and liquidation of cash advances.
observations.reason: Due to the manpower limitation of the Municipal Government and the voluminous workloads of concerned personnel.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our previous years recommendation that Management:

Require the concerned Accountable Officer to submit the accounts and financial reports to the Municipal Treasurer pursuant to the timelines of the abovementioned existing law, rules and regulations; 
 
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to submit to the Municipal Accounting Office all the disbursements vouchers, payrolls, official receipts, including the corresponding reports for each month on or before the fifth day of the ensuing month or pursuant to the abovementioned pertinent provisions of rules and regulations, as applicable; and

Direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly comply with the timelines on the recording and submission of accounts to the Auditor within ten days of the ensuing month.
observations.reason: Due to manpower deficiency in the respective offices.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management require the Municipal Accountant to make the final reconciliation and accounting of these account balances, then prepare the DVs together with the supporting schedule for the Municipal Treasurer to issue a check payable either to the Source Agency or direct to the National Treasury. 

We further recommended that remaining balances which are considered as special purpose funds should be retained and fund balances corresponding to original releases such as financial assistance or subsidy, as well as funds which the disposition of the unused balance is not specified, maybe reverted to the unappropriated surplus of the Municipality for future utilization thereof subject to the usual accounting, auditing and budgeting laws and regulations.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this AAR

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management (LDRRM) Officer to optimize the utilization of the LDRRMF of the LGU in order to strengthen its disaster preparedness and avoid potential damages during disasters.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this AAR

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management stop the practice of prematurely issuing certificate of acceptance and comply with the set timeline to maximize the one-year defects liability period. Furthermore, require to require the contractors to submit valid substitute, as prescribed in the IRR of RA 9184, for the norescribeed retention money.   
 
Likewise, we reiterated our recommendation to instruct the Bids and Awards Committee to remind the bidders for the contracts to be bidded by the Municipal Government of the requirement of retention money on projects as provided in the above cited provisions of the Revised IRR of RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: The Bids and Awards Committee has also been instructed to remind the contract bidders of the requirement of retention money as provided in the above cited provisions.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following courses of action:

Direct the Municipal Accountant to strengthen the accounting and processing system being part of the accounting and fiscal control process and ensure that transactions processed, paid and recorded are properly approved and duly supported with complete documents and/or technical requirements and proper signatures; and

Require the concerned Municipal personnel to use the Checklist provided for under the Revised Documentary Requirements for Common Government Transactions prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012-001 and the Revised RIRR of RA No. 9184 as a useful guide in the processing of claims for payment.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this AAR

observations.recommendation:
To ensure proper accounting and promote better control in the receipt and issuance of Supplies and Materials, we reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the LCE to require:

The Property Officer to maintain perpetual inventory records of Supplies and Materials purchased and to ensure that complete records of the items issued to requisitioning departments/offices are kept. At the end of each month, he shall prepare the monthly Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued, a copy of which shall be furnished the Municipal Accountant for recording.

The Municipal Accountant to record all regular purchases of Supplies and Materials under the proper inventory account following the perpetual inventory method;

At the end of each semester, the Property Officer shall conduct an inventory, in coordination with the concerned department/office, of all unused Supplies and Materials and shall prepare a consolidated report thereon and furnish the Municipal Accountant a copy thereof; and

The Municipal Accountant to take up the necessary adjustment, if any, in the respective inventory and expense accounts for Supplies and Materials based on the consolidated inventory report provided by the Property Officer.
observations.reason: Due to manpower deficiency in the respective offices.
Due to manpower deficiency in the respective offices.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that Management, through the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) facilitate the full establishment of the GAD database in order to have a basis for planning and programming PPAs and formulating policies that are gender-responsive and to be able to undertake thorough gender analysis based on relevant data to properly identify existing gender issues and ensure that the approved PPAs to be incorporated in the GAD Plan and Budget are gender responsive.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this AAR

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Enforce the payment of liquidated damages against erring contractors for non-performance of their obligations to the LGU within the periods stipulated in the contracts

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant exert more effort in securing the necessary supporting documents cited under COA Circular No. 2016-005 to be able to apply for request for write-off of the dormant accounts, except for the undeposited collection of Mr. Anthony B. Bisona amounting to ₱192,018.00 which was subjected to a Notice of Charge, for the fair presentation of the financial position of the Municipality.

We reiterate our prior year’s recommendation that Management require:

The GSO to conduct the physical count of inventories semestrally and submit the report to the Audit Team within the prescribed period. The RPCI shall be reconciled with the records maintained by the Accounting; and

The Municipal Accountant and GSO to maintain the supplies ledger card and stock cards, respectively, and observe proper inventory management and internal control procedures in accordance with the NGAS Manual for LGUs.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to:

Cause the reversion to the unappropriated surplus of the identified long outstanding payables where the obligation to pay has already prescribed; 

Facilitate the prompt release of retention monies to prevent accumulation of balances of the said account; and

Coordinate with the concerned personnel in relation to the long outstanding guaranty/security deposits payable.

We recommended that Management claim the liquidated damages against the retention money and/or other securities posted by the contractor and impose the necessary penalties and sanctions on the persons involved in the inaccurate reporting of 100% completion of the project.

We recommended that Management:

Prepare and execute lease contracts with the market stallholders and enforce strict compliance with the terms and conditions of such rentals; and

Comply with the Municipality’s Revenue Code in relation to the issuance of business permits.

We likewise reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that Management review, revise and update its 1992 Municipal Tax Code pursuant to Section 191 of the LGC of 1991.

We also recommended that Management formulate or develop effective and practical strategies that could enhance collections of all unpaid accounts from market stalls/operation and the water system and impose penalties/surcharges to defaulting lessees/consumers.

We recommended that Management require the:

MDRRMO to submit the annual LDRRMFIP and monthly Report of Utilization of DRMMF to the OCD and COA audit team in accordance with the cited guidelines; and

Municipal Accountant to prepare the monthly Report of Utilization of DRRMF and submit the same to the MDRRMO.
observations.reason: The management submits the report annually, not monthly.

observations.recommendation: Create a plantilla for the Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Officer (MENRO) to effectively oversee the duties and functions of the office.

We recommended that Management, through the GAD Focal Person, spearhead the updating and maintenance of the GAD database to serve as a more reliable basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation.

The Municipality is still in the process of titling all lots.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require:

The General Service Officer to: (a) Render Road Map; and, (b) Keep a complete and updated Local Road Network Property Card (LRNPCs) for all roads and its components;

The Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant and the General Service Officer with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects; and

The Municipal Accountant to: (a) Prepare the JEV to record the beginning balance of the local road network and its components; (b) Prepare and maintain Local Road Network Ledger Cards (LRNLCs); (c) Keep and maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network; (d) Prepare a lapsing schedule for the computation of the depreciation for each component at the end of the year; and (e) Include the necessary disclosure on the road network system in the Notes to the Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: Documents were not prepared by the GSO.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that Management require the LSB:

Prepare the SEF Budget following the prescribed format in the determination of estimated income for a particular budget year as provided in the Circular; and

Strictly observe the timelines in the planning and budgeting process as prescribed under the DepEd, DBM & DILG Joint Circular.

Henceforth, strictly adhere with the provisions of the Local Government Code and DepEd-DBM-DILG JC No. 1 in the preparation of the School Board budget to ensure proper allocation of funds.

Further, we recommended that the School Board consider the proper method in the preparation of SEF budget so that only the deficiency in the DepEd Division budget shall be provided funds therein.

We recommended that Management (a) prepare proper documentation for the items that cannot be traced and (b) prepare a formal request to write-off receivable accounts which were dormant for more than 10 years after all the remedies have been exhausted, following the procedures set forth in COA Circular No. 2016-005.
observations.reason: No justification given

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE instruct the concerned personnel to submit to the Audit Team the required documents/reports as of December 31, 2022 and maintain proper documentation for the succeeding years.

We recommended that the LCE require the Sangguniang Bayan to update the Revenue Code of the Municipality pursuant to Section 186 and 191 of RA No. 7160 to increase the revenue collection from local taxes that can be used to finance more beneficial PPAs.
observations.reason: Local Revenue Code of the Municipality was still not updated.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Treasurer to: 

Prepare demand letters to be sent to the delinquent taxpayers and post and publish notice of delinquencies in accordance with the provisions of Section 254 of Local Government Code; and

Take actions to intensify the collection of delinquent real property taxes and determine whether RPT that have long been outstanding are already uncollectible.
observations.reason: No justification given

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE require the:

Malinao Waterworks Management Officer (MWMO) to (i) formulate effective and practical strategies to enforce collection of the unpaid accounts, including disconnection of water supply of delinquent consumers; (ii) conduct regular inspection and monitoring of reservoirs, distribution pipelines, and conduit pipes to detect illegal connections and to deter residents from destroying the properties of Malinao Waterworks (MW); and (iii) institute administrative and legal actions against persons caught destroying MW properties or those with illegally tapped distribution pipelines; and

Municipal Accountant to prepare reconciliation of Accounts Receivable to determine its correct amount and to record income from waterworks system operations based on the monthly billings.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Management require the Municipal Accountant to record the transfer of unexpended balance of QRF and DRRM -MOOE to the Special Trust Fund under the Trust Liability – DRRM account in the trust fund books at year-end. We also recommended the Municipal Accountant to re-compute the unexpended balance, if there’s any adjustments on the disbursements charged against the LDRRMF.
observations.reason: No actual transfer of cash was made. See related audit findings in Part II.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE:

Require the GAD Focal Point System to use the HGDG as tool in identifying PPAs attributed to GAD budget for efficiency in identifying the issues, analysis, and monitoring of its effectiveness.

Direct all members of the GAD Focal Point System to undergo trainings on HGDG, PIMME and FIMME to build their competence in the conduct of gender analysis of programs and projects for purposes of GAD attribution.
observations.reason: No justification given

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Property and Accounting Units adhere to the procedures outlined in COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020, with emphasis on the derecognition of non-existing/missing PPEs for which accountability could not be established despite exhaustion, to establish the correct balance of the PPE and record the necessary adjustments.  Further, for the Inventory Committee to prepare the RPCPPE in accordance with the prescribed format and signed by the members thereof.
observations.managementAction: Reiterated in CY 2023 AAR
observations.reason: No Inventory Committee

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Management to take the necessary actions for the revision of the Schedule of Fair Market Values of real properties in the Municipality and publication of the same to avoid further loss of additional income from the increased assessment.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Assessor henceforth request the concerned Registrar of Deeds to furnish their Office every year a copy of the abstract of the Registry of Property, accompanied by copies of corresponding deeds of sale, donation, or partition, or other forms of alienation pursuant to the above-cited Manual.
observations.managementAction: Sending a letter request asking for the necessary documents to the Registry of Deeds.
observations.reason: The first person/ office who should provide us with a copy of necessary documents is Registry of Deeds.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that Management employ the HGDG tool for regular/flagship programs and projects of the LGU and identify which part of the project cost/expenditure is attributable to the GAD Budget/Expenditure. 

We likewise reiterated our recommendation that the members of the GAD Focal Point System attend seminars on HGDG to enhance compliance with the proper preparation of the GAD PB/AR.

Lastly, we recommended that Management ensure that the GAD M & E System of the LGU is in place and fully functional so as to assess the effectiveness and efficiency of their gender mainstreaming efforts.
observations.managementAction: Coordination/ Lobbying to the Department concern for the conduct of orientation/ T.A on HGDG tool Application. Re-constitution of GFPS Executive Order and Monitoring and Evaluation Team System of the LGU.
observations.reason: Lack/No orientation on the HGDG tool Application.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Stop the charging and payment of MOOE which are not included in the list of expenses chargeable against the SEF; and

Ensure that the SEF be expended solely and exclusively for expenditures provided under DepEd-DBM-DILG JC No. 1, s. 2017 and its addenda.

We recommended that Management may either:

Undertake public bidding for the procurement of POL, wherein bid price shall remain unchanged during the duration of the contract, unless would qualify as procurement of POL in bulk using the index-based pricing scheme following the guidelines as prescribed per Appendix 22 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184, which considers price adjustments under extraordinary circumstances; or

Undertake direct retail purchase of POL with a reputable supplier under an-on credit arrangement with a periodic billing system as allowed per Section 53.14 of the RIRR of RA 9184.

We recommended that Management:

Direct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary correcting entries for the erroneous entries made in recording the 15% advance payment for mobilization by recognizing it in the Advances to Contractors account. Moreover, the retention money for each progress payments shall be recorded under Guaranty/Security Deposits Payable;

Direct the Municipal Accountant to record the balance of the unpaid billing of the completed project (Municipal Slaughterhouse) as Accounts Payable to complete the recorded cost of the Construction in Progress. Henceforth, reclassify the cost of the said completed project from CIP account to the appropriate PPE account and thereafter record the journal entry for the transfer of the said PPE from the TF account to the GF account. Accordingly, provide for the annual depreciation thereof from CYs 2018 to 2022 for the fair presentation of the pertinent accounts in the financial statements. 

In addition, direct the Municipal Engineer to follow up the release of the remaining fund from the Source Agency (SA) for the said project and facilitate the processing of the final payment to the contractor. Thereafter, he shall prepare the necessary documents and submit the final Liquidation Report to the SA.

Direct the Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant with copy of the Certificate of Project Completion of the completed projects, to serve as their basis in recording completed projects as PPE. Periodic depreciation shall likewise be provided to allow fair presentation of the affected accounts in the year-end financial statements; and

Require the Municipal Accountant to disclose in the notes to financial statements under the Construction in Progress account the balance of the projects which were not completed and discontinued.

We recommended that Management:

Direct the Municipal Accountant to further analyze the SL for PPE and determine the semi-expendable items acquired and issued on CY 2022 and prior years. Thereafter, the carrying amount shall be charged to expense or Prior Period or expense, as the case may be, in the books of accounts; and 

Direct the Municipal Accountant and Property Officer to, henceforth, follow the new capitalization threshold of newly acquired PPE and other guidelines set forth in COA Circular No. 2022-004 in the issuance of semi-expendable items and its recording in the books of accounts and other records.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to indicate the date of all the transactions in the SL of the disbursing officer. Additionally, instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure complete supporting documents in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-001 before the issuance of cash advance to the disbursing officer.

We recommended that Management:

Require the concerned Implementing Offices (IOs) of the unimplemented projects to determine if the same will still be implemented. Otherwise, refund/return the unutilized funds to the respective SAs. 

Instruct the Municipal Accountant to conduct cash analysis of the trust accounts this CY 2022 to determine which of these trust receipts no longer have cash back-up and need to be adjusted. The LGU may request for the write-off of these accounts from the Government Accountancy and Financial Management Information System (GAFMIS), Commission on Audit Central Office, with the working papers and analysis of the accounts, as supporting documents before they can be dropped from the books.

We recommended that Management direct the BAC and the BAC-TWG:

To include in their post-qualification a comparative evaluation showing the number of projects with the same equipment and key personnel pledged and assigned by the same lowest calculated bidder for different projects with simultaneous or overlapping implementation period; and 

To consider formulating a policy on the sufficiency and availability of equipment pledged by the contractors with regard to projects with simultaneous or overlapping implementation period awarded to the same contractors to avoid delay in project implementation and setting the limit for the number of contracts handled by key personnel, to still reasonably perform its duties and functions, without affecting the quality and timeliness of the project implementation.

We recommended that Management exert its best effort to implement all programmed projects under the 20 per cent EDF during the current year, to ensure that its objectives of desirable socio-economic and environmental outcomes will be achieved. 

Further, we recommended that Management expedite the implementation/completion of the 41 on-going projects and the 39 unimplemented CY 2022 EDF projects for calendar year 2023.

We recommended that Management instruct the LDDRMO to incorporate in the LDRRMFIP the unspent prior years DRRM fund, and henceforth update the said plan as necessary so that transparency in the utilization of the said fund is achieved and to ensure that the expenditures charged to the said fund are included in the priority PPAs of the LGU for their disaster mitigation, prevention and preparedness activities/projects pursuant to the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that Management thru the GFPS maximize the utilization of the identified GAD program, projects and activities for realization of intended result and extending the expected benefits to beneficiaries.

We recommended that Management observe the above-cited regulation pertaining to the wages of the JO as prescribed by the RTWPB for Region 5. Henceforth, Management come up with a workable solution in complying with the mandated daily minimum wage rate prescribed by the RTWPB for Region 5 given the limited resources of the LGU such as reviewing the propriety of the number of JOs being hired.

We recommended that Management:

Require the MHO to render a report to the Accountant on the receipt, issuance, and balances of vaccines and other inventories directly received from outside sources; and 

For the Accountant, in coordination with the MHO and various outside sources of COVID-19 vaccines (i.e. DOH, PHO, etc.) to determine the full cost of ending inventory of vaccines and ancillary supplies as at year end and henceforth recognize and record future receipts and issuance of the same.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive

Direct the Property Officer to facilitate the immediate disposal of the aforementioned unserviceable properties and waste materials to recoup any salvage value thereof.  

Additionally, we recommend the following measures:

Create an Appraisal and Disposal Committee, whose members are required to attend relevant trainings on property management, particularly on the disposal of unserviceable properties and waste materials, should they lack the necessary technical competence;

Conduct an appraisal of the unserviceable properties and waste materials and furnish the auditor a copy of the appraisal report thereof; and

Dispose the unserviceable properties based on the most appropriate mode of disposal pursuant to the provisions of Sections 379 to 381 of RA No. 7160 and COA Circular Nos. 89-296 and 92-386 following the procedures shown in flow chart of the disposal of property (Annex B).

Further, for the properties damaged during typhoon Rolly, we recommended that the Management:

direct the concerned AO’s to immediately submit a request for relief from accountability and complete supporting documents enumerated in COA Audit Memorandum No. 92-751 (Annex C) for all the properties damaged;

direct the Municipal Accountant to disclose the loss in the notes to financial statements pending the result of the request for relief from accountability and when request is granted, prepare a JEV to derecognize the assets in the books of accounts. If request is denied, the loss shall be taken up as a receivable from the accountable officer/persons liable and shall be credited to the appropriate asset account.
observations.managementAction: Property Custodian Designated has already conducted partial inventory of unserviceable properties
observations.reason: Lack of Manpower

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Designate additional disbursing officer who will be granted with other cash advances and be responsible for liquidating the same.

We recommended that Management instruct the ICO-Municipal Treasurer to:

Require all collecting officers to prepare RCDs at the close of each business day and effect remittance of their collections daily to the Liquidating Officer. In the case of the deputized collectors in the barangays, require their remittance at least once a month or as soon as the collections reaches the threshold of ₱5,000.00.

Require all the Accountable Officers with unaccounted accountable forms to formally render a report thereon. In the event that these can no longer be located/presented, a request for relief from accountability for the loss of accountable forms shall be made in accordance with the requirements under COA Circular No. 84-233 dated August 2, 1984.

Further, we recommended that Management take appropriate action against the accountable officers for failure to account for missing accountable forms under their custody despite various reminders and follow-ups.
We recommended that the Municipal Government optimize the utilization of the LDRRMF in order to strengthen its disaster preparedness and avoid potential damages during disasters.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the GAD Focal Person and the TWG to establish fully the GAD database for them to have an effective basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation. Likewise, GAD Monitoring and Evaluation Team shall also be established pursuant to the aforecited JMC for them to monitor and evaluate the Municipality’s GAD policies, programs and projects.

We recommended that Management return its investment in time deposit to the regular General Fund bank account of the LGU upon its maturity in order that the same may be used in the implementation of priority programs, projects and activities for the benefit of its constituents.

We recommended that the Management immediately hire a staff, thru job order contract, pending the approval of the HRMPSB by the Sanggunian, subject to availability of funds, who shall assist the designated Property Officer to maintain property subsidiary records such as Property Cards and Local Road Network Property Cards (LRNPCs) of all existing constructed and acquired properties of the Municipality based on the documents and records such as Certificate of Completion and Acceptance, As-Built Plans, etc., of the MEO. 

Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Engineer provide the designated Property Officer with the relevant information, documents and records such as Certificate of Completion and Acceptance, As-Built Plans, etc. of all the existing constructed and acquired properties of the Municipality. Thereafter, immediately or on regular basis inform the designated Property Officer of all the completed projects after their completion and final acceptance and provide to him copies of relevant documents and records thereof.

Moreover, the Inventory Committee to determine and indicate in the RPCPPE the physical condition of the infrastructure assets, buildings and other structures for proper measurement of its carrying amount in the financial statements and to update the current use of lots/land for proper classification in the books by the Municipal Accountant; and

Finally, for the Sanggunian to expedite their review and approval of the HRMPSB submitted by the Management to facilitate the organization and establishment of the General Services Office to be headed by a permanent Department Head to take on the duties and responsibilities that are provided under Section 490 of the Local Government Code, due to the increasing number of assets owned by the LGU that needs to be accounted for.

Inventory Committee to indicate and properly identify the accountable officer for each item of property in the RPCPPE.

41. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) institutionalize the PSCP by creating a working group that will perform the following activities: (a) conduct risk assessments considering new variables after the pandemic; (b) identify critical processes and functions; (c) determine recovery scenarios that may disrupt normal operations; (d) conduct risk and impact analyses; (e) formulate the PSCP; and (f) submit the plan to the CDRRMO for review, to the LCE for approval, and to the Sangguniang Panglungsod for the passage of a resolution to institutionalize the PSCP.
42. We further recommended that Management take proactive measures in being informed and updated of relevant issuances by authorities that may affect their local governance performance scorecards.

The City Mayor issued EO No. 2022-051, amending EO No. 2016-008 (Organizing the Composition of the LGU Gender and Development Focal Point Council). Instead of establishing an M&E team, the GAD Focal Point Council was given responsibilities that include the assessment and evaluation of all the GAD focused policies, programs, accomplishments, and actual costs.
The GAD Focal Point Council has not yet consolidated a GAD Evaluation Report to be submitted to the DILG Regional Office.
