observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to cause the immediate reconciliation of the unremitted amount and refund immediately excess withholdings tax, if warranted and to remit in full the unremitted taxes withheld to the BIR, within the period prescribed under Revenue Regulation No. 2-98.
observations.managementAction: Adjusting entries were and substantial remittance of taxes withheld were made by the management.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the:

Municipal Accountant to conduct the necessary analysis and reconciliation to properly ascertain the correct valuation of the Due to Pag-IBIG account; 

Municipal Treasurer to remit the full amount withheld from the employee’s salaries together with the government’s share in the employees’ premiums; and 

We also recommended the Municipal Government create plantilla positions for the Human Resource Management Office to effectively manage the employment process and also maintain and update the employee records.

We recommended that Management require the Accountant to determine all the property items recognized in the PPE accounts but did not meet the capitalization threshold and prepare the adjusting entries to reclassify them into the appropriate expense and government equity accounts. Henceforth, we recommended the use of proper inventory and expense accounts to record the receipt and issuance of semi-expendable items that did not meet the capitalization threshold, respectively.
observations.managementAction: Reclassification of semi-expendable assets were already made by the Accounting Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require The Accounting Office to prepare adjusting entries to correct the recording of unpaid water fees as of December 31, 2020 and henceforth, apply the accrual basis in recording revenues in accordance with the PPSASs.

We recommended that Management require the BAC, through BAC Secretariat, to comply with the posting requirements and submission to the GPPB of APP, its supplemental and PMRs, to ensure transparency and integrity in the procurement process.

We recommended the Management require the concerned personnel to submit the government contracts, purchase orders and the supporting documents within five days after perfection pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-001.
observations.managementAction: The Management failed to submit government contracts, PO, and supporting documents to COA within 5 days

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the MDRRMO to plan/identify PPAs, which shall be incorporated in the LDRRMFIP, to utilize the various cash donations according to its specific purpose and to address the needs of the intended beneficiaries.
observations.managementAction: The management explained that executing a MOA may not be favorable to the LGU due to fluctuating prices of goods.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management execute a Memorandum of Agreement with the supplier in the procurement of goods for stockpiling in preparation for eventualities/calamities that may occur in the future to avoid instances of issuing/distribution the relief goods even in the absence of a calamity for fear that the goods shall be spoiled due to the absence of a proper and adequate storeroom.
observations.managementAction: The management explained that executing a MOA may not be favorable to the LGU due to fluctuating prices of goods.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management collect from the supplier liquidated damages amounting to ₱40,141.98. Thereafter, exercise prudence in processing transactions pertaining to claims against the municipal fund and ensure the imposition of liquidated damages for late deliveries pursuant to the provisions in the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 to prevent suppliers from neglecting their responsibilities to comply with the conditions of the contract and to avoid delay in the implementation of projects/programs.
observations.managementAction: Liquidated damages could no longer be imposed due to the period that elapsed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare and submit the complete set of financial statements on time for all funds consisting of: (a) Pre- and Post-closing Trial Balance; (b) Adjusting and closing Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs); (c) Detailed and Condensed Statement of Financial Position; (d) Detailed and Condensed Statement of Financial Performance; (e); Statement of Changes in Net Assets/Equity; (f) Statement of Cash Flows; (g) Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts; (h) Notes to Financial Statements; (i) Statement of Management Responsibility for Financial Statements; (j) Subsidiary Schedule of General Ledger (GL) Accounts, to allow the timely audit thereof and the timely submission of the required report.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the Municipal Accountant to maintain subsidiary ledgers for the receivable and payable accounts of the LGU in compliance with Sections 114, 121 and 122 of PD No. 1445.

We recommended that Management require the Collecting Officer/s to turn over their collections daily and regularly to the Municipal Treasurer in accordance with the guidelines prescribed in the NGAS Manual for LGUs. Likewise, we recommended that Management require the Treasurer to verify the RCD and accountable forms, and to deposit intact all the collections turned over to her with the authorized depository bank daily or not later than the next banking day. We also recommended the Management to consider designating a Liquidating Officer to facilitate the receipt and verification of collections.
We recommended that Management require the Collecting Officers and Treasurer to prepare and submit the Monthly RAAFs not later than the fifth day of the ensuing month in accordance with the NGAS.
We recommended the Collecting Officer to observe the proper handling of Cashbooks and maintain separate books for special funds received as prescribed in the afore-cited guidelines.
We recommended that Management strictly observed the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 in the grant, utilization, and liquidations of cash advances.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant exert more effort in securing the necessary supporting documents cited under COA Circular No. 2016-005 to be able to request for write-off of the dormant accounts for the fair presentation of the financial position of the Municipality.

We recommended that Management require:

The GSO to conduct the physical count of inventories semestrally and submit the report to the Audit Team within the prescribed period. The RPCI shall be reconciled with the records maintained by the Accounting; and

The Municipal Accountant and GSO to maintain the supplies ledger card and stock cards, respectively, and observe proper inventory management and internal control procedures in accordance with the NGAS Manual for LGUs.
We recommended that Management require the Municipal Engineer to completely report the implementation of government PPAs in compliance with COA Circular No. 2013-004.

We recommended that Management require the BAC to submit justification why the posting of performance security was not imposed on the awarded bidder and why the contract was still awarded in the absence thereof. Henceforth, we recommended that the BAC strictly comply with the performance security requirements.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to withheld retention money equivalent to 10% from each payment and ensure that it shall only be due for release upon final acceptance of the project or upon the request of the contractor to substitute the same with an irrevocable standby letter of credit/surety bond callable on demand or other acceptable documents. Henceforth, management ensure the strict compliance with the procurement laws.

We recommended that Management, through the BAC and/or Municipal Accounting Office, require the submission of Mayor’s Permit and PhilGEPS registration number from the suppliers prior the award of contract, otherwise, payments shall be disallowed in audit.

We recommended that Management:

Stop the practice of splitting of requisitions/contracts and consolidates the same to strictly adhere to the provisions of RA No. 9184 and COA Circular No. 76-41 in all procurements;

Enjoin the BAC to meticulously plan their procurement and deliberate properly on the appropriate mode of procurement to avoid resorting to splitting of contracts; and

Submit valid justification on why said transaction should not be disallowed in audit for violating the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.

We recommended that Management:

Enjoin the Local Finance Committee and Department Heads to improve the budget preparation process to ensure the submission of the Annual Budget within the prescribed timeline; and

Fast-track the implementation of the identified development projects to provide the desired socio, economic and environmental benefits to the constituents. Henceforth, ensure the timely implementation thereof.
observations.reason: The Audit Team noted a substantial increase in utilization.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Strictly observe the guidelines on the appropriation and utilization of the 20 percent Development Fund as provided under DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 2020-01;

Ensure that the PPAs under the 20 percent DF are embodied in the AIP and LDIP, which should be harmonized with the RDP and PDP; and

Require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to maintain the pertinent registries and SAAOB, respectively.
observations.reason: Guidelines were strictly observed and the Municipal Accountant was able to maintain the appropriate registries.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE require the HRMO to:

Prepare and issue a staffing pattern plan and policy guidelines in the hiring of employees under job order and contract of service including the submission of necessary identification documents of JOs and COS workers; and 

Assess the manpower needs and staffing requirements per office or department taking into consideration the existing plantilla personnel.

We recommended that LCE require the HRMO to adopt a schedule of standard rates for JOs per position based on the qualifications and nature of work or duties performed and indicate in the Job Order contracts the duties and responsibilities and expected outputs of such job order workers.

We recommended that Management through the Solid Waste Management Board properly implement the recently approved 10-year plan and to assess its effectiveness and relevance in relation to developments in the field of solid waste management.

We recommended that Management and the LSB:

Ensure that the prescribed planning and budgeting tools are effectively utilized; and

Strictly observe the timelines in the planning and budgeting process as prescribed under the DepEd, DBM & DILG Joint Circular.
observations.reason: Delay in the approval of the SEF Budget was still noted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management/LSB:

 Strictly observe the preparation and submission of the required reports in its proper format and within the prescribed period to allow the Audit Team ample time in the verification thereof; and

Continue to maximize the utilization of the SEF budget in order to deliver its services to the intended beneficiaries.

We recommended that Management:

Direct the GAD Focal Person to adhere to the approved GPB in the implementation of GAD programs and activities and use the GPB in the formulation of the GAD Accomplishment Report. In case of changes, Management should prepare a revised GPB for approval by the concerned officials and oversight agencies.

Submit their GPBs to the PPDO to ensure alignment of the Municipality’s GAD PPAs with the priorities of the province and then from the PPDO to the DILG Provincial Office for its review and endorsement.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the subsidiary ledger balance of the Due to BIR account to its balance in the Financial Statements and to remit in full the taxes withheld to the BIR within the period prescribed under Revenue Regulation No. 2-98.

observations.managementAction: To be Implemented
observations.reason: No Inventory Committee and No Inventory of Records

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that Management:

Cause the preparation of the Inventory and Inspection Report on Unserviceable Property by the Property Officer/Unit to facilitate the disposal of unserviceable properties; and

Direct the Disposal Committee to expedite the disposal of unserviceable properties following the procedures set forth in COA Circular No. 92-386 to prevent further deterioration and to realize income therefrom if found to be saleable.
observations.managementAction: Reiterated in CY 2023 AAR
observations.reason: No Inventory Committee

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE require:

The GSO to maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map and keep a complete LRNPCs for all roads and its components;

The Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects; and

The Municipal Accountant to keep and maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network and reconcile the same with the LRNPCs and the result of the physical inventory taking at the end of each year and submit upon request of the Audit Team for verification.
observations.managementAction: Coordination from Engineering and Accounting Office.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Apply or renew the registration of all serviceable motor vehicles with the LTO to ensure security in case of fortuitous events that may have an adverse effect on the said property; and

Properly monitor the registration of all properties with the LTO to avoid payment of unnecessary penalties, surcharges and damages to third party liabilities in case of vehicular accidents.
observations.managementAction: To continue to renew road licenses to LTO and insure MV with expired Insurance to GSIS
observations.reason: The Municipality was able to apply and renew road licenses to LTO

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that Management submit immediately the lacking required supporting documents. Further, for Management to observe the submission of contracts and supporting documents within five working days from its execution to enable the Audit Team to immediately conduct its review.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant revert the total amount of stale checks outstanding to the Cash-in Bank and Accounts Payable accounts to ensure the proper presentation of the account balances.

We commended the Municipality of Manito for its effort in achieving a vaccination rate of 80.1 per cent of its target population, thus reducing hospitalization cases of its constituents. 

We recommended that Management continue its implementation of the vaccination and booster dose program and widen the scope of its vaccine information drive to strengthen its constituents’ knowledge and protection against hospitalization and death from COVID-19.

We recommended that Management execute a Contract of Service or Memorandum of Agreement when hiring JO personnel, stating therein the duties and responsibilities, expected outputs, and the rate per day of the workers.
We recommended that the Municipality strictly observe the rules and regulations in procuring consulting services under the RIRR of RA 9184 whenever hiring personnel to work as a consultant.

We recommended the Management to adhere strictly with the provision of Item 5.1.5 of COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 and submit regularly to the COA Audit Team of the Municipality the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund on or before the 15th day after the end of each month through the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council and the Municipal Development Council.
observations.managementAction: Reiterated in CY 2023 AAR

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management maximize the utilization of the LDRRMF to cover all the four thematic areas of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management in order to enhance the preparedness of the Municipality against disasters and avoid, lessen or limit their adverse impacts to the community.
observations.managementAction: Reiterated in CY 2023 AAR
observations.reason: Being new as an LDRRM Officer and staff not yet knowledgeable in utilizing the funds.

observations.managementAction: Reiterated in CY 2023 AAR
observations.reason: Formulated the PSCP but not yet approved by the Sangguniang Bayan

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the agency to require TWG members, with the assistance of the BAC Secretariat to prepare the bid evaluation report in accordance with the provisions of Generic Procurement Manual Volume 3.
We recommended that the BAC invite observers in all stages of the procurement process in accordance with Section 13 of the RIRR of RA No. 9184.

We commended the Management for improving its methods in programming, prioritizing and implementing programs and projects to achieve the desired socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes for the community.

observations.managementAction: Ensure that COA recommendation will be properly implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the agency provide justification on non-imposition of liquidated damages despite the delay, otherwise, take appropriate action to collect liquidated damages from the supplier for every day of delay of delivery. Further, ensure that the terms and conditions indicated in the purchase orders and contracts are strictly complied with.

We recommended that Management further improve its performance in the submission of financial reports and accounts by:

Requiring the concerned Accountable Officer to submit the accounts and financial reports to the Municipal Treasurer pursuant to the timelines of the abovementioned existing laws, rules and regulations; 

Directing the Municipal Treasurer to submit to the Municipal Accounting Office all the disbursements vouchers, payrolls, official receipts, including the corresponding reports for each month on or before the fifth day of the ensuing month or pursuant to the abovementioned pertinent provisions of rules and regulations, as applicable; and

Directing the Municipal Accountant to strictly comply with the timelines on the recording and submission of accounts to the Auditor within ten days of the ensuing month.

The Audit Team commended the Office of the Municipal Accountant for the efforts done in submitting all the Monthly Trial Balances and Quarterly/Year-end FS within the timeline, except for one Monthly Trial Balance.

Furthermore, we recommended that Management continue to improve its performance in the submission of the Trial Balances and the Financial Statements.

We commended the Local Chief Executive, the Municipal Accountant and staff for the early submission of the CY 2022 Year-End Financial Statements and other financial reports, prepared in conformity with IPSAS. 

We also recognize the assistance and cooperation of all the officers and personnel of the LGU, thus, allowing the Municipal Accountant to be able to come up with the required reports on time.

Further, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant continue the timely submission of the year-end financial statements, together with its supporting schedules, to enable the timely verification thereof by the Auditor.

We recommended that Management continue to improve the preparation and submission of the BRS together with the bank statements and attachments within the prescribed period and to promptly prepare the adjusting entries for the reconciling items to arrive at a correct balance for the Cash in Bank account.

We recommended that Management maximize the     utilization of the ER 1-94 Funds to contain, mitigate and eliminate the transmission of COVID-19 and manage and mitigate the effects thereof to the community and individuals by implementing eligible projects enumerated under Section 5 of DC 2020-04-0008. Further, for Management to maintain separate subsidiary ledger for the receipt of Electrification Fund, for proper recording and monitoring.

We recommended that Management submit immediately the lacking required supporting documents. Further, for Management to observe the submission of contracts and supporting documents within five working days from its execution to enable the Audit Team to immediately conduct its review.

We recommended that the LGU, through the Office of the Human Resource Management, should review and justify the necessity of hiring too many job orders and to review the present organizational set-up with a view to improving the efficiency and effectivity of the LGU workforce.

Further, we recommend that the LGU stop the practice of giving different rates to JOs having the same position in the LGU in order not to create inequality in the work force of the LGU and to require the HRMO to adopt a schedule of standard rates for JOs per position that is based on the nature of work/assignment, qualification and number of years in service taking into consideration the prevailing minimum wage in the locality.
observations.reason: Adapted standard wages matrix | Hiring of JOs/ CoS is a prerogative of the LCE

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Human Resource and Management Officer to discuss and make the necessary arrangement with the Agency’s GSB for the use of ATM in the payment of salaries, wages and other benefits of the Officials and Employees of the LGU, so that government funds may be safeguarded against loss or misuse and to ensure efficiency and economy in its Cash Management.
observations.managementAction: Reiterated in CY 2023 AAR

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to:

Properly record the collections and deposits to reflect the correct balances of Cash in Local Treasury and Cash in Bank account in the financial statements; and

Have a thorough analysis of the total reconciling item amounting to ₱113,848.96 and to prepare JEV to take up reconciling items already determined as book error.

We recommended that the Management require the Municipal Accountant to henceforth, record unpaid terminal leave benefits as at year-end in the Leave Benefits Payable account instead of Accounts Payable account and correct the error in recording terminal leave benefits as Accounts Payable as of December 31, 2022.

We recommended that Management require:

The Municipal Accountant to provide the Audit Team the JEV, together with the complete supporting document for the receipt of the LGSF-FA for verification; and

The IOs to coordinate with the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to ensure that all fund transfers from different SAs are properly recorded in the books of the Agency in accordance with Sections 4.2 and 4.4 of COA Circular 94-013 to reflect the correct balances of the liability accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to:

properly record the collections and deposits to reflect the correct balances of Cash in Local Treasury and Cash in Bank account in the financial statements; and

have a thorough analysis of the total reconciling item amounting to ₱160,051.47 and to prepare JEV to take up reconciling items already determined as book error.

We recommended that Management ensure the implementation of these projects in the ensuing years to attain its commitment to its constituents to deliver the benefits that can be derived from the projects.

In view of the above deficiencies, we recommended to the Management the following:
Assign or hire personnel who will take full responsibility as an ESWM focal person of the LGU for him/her to monitor a systematic, comprehensive and ecological solid waste management program required under RA No. 9003.

Update the status of the submitted Ten-Year ESWM Plan of the LGU so that they will have a basis/guide in implementing proper solid waste management.

Coordinate with the barangays the immediate rehabilitation of the dilapidated MRFs. Also provide assistance in the information dissemination to the residents on the use and purpose of the MRFs established in their barangay and the proper segregation process of the wastes/garbage.

Provide necessary budgetary allocation for the MENRO in order for the ESWM to operate effectively for the benefit of the residents of the municipality.

Establish a sanitary land fill in the Municipality in accordance with guidelines set forth in RA 9003.

We recommended that, henceforth, the Management prioritize urgent appropriation ordinances, particularly those intended to address the COVID 19 pandemic to ensure that there is sufficient, adequate and readily available funding for the immediate delivery of basic necessities and services to families and individuals affected by the pandemic and to promote and protect the collective interests of the people of Oas in these challenging times.

We recommended that Management, follow up with the Sangguniang Bayan its proposal for the creation of the Human Resource Merit Promotion and Selection Board (HRMPSB) in accordance with the provisions of the 2017 Omnibus Rules on Appointments and Other Human Resource Actions (revised July 2018). Thereafter, hire qualified personnel for the vacant positions, particularly the mandatory positions under the Local Government Code.

We also reiterated our last year’s recommendation that the Sangguniang Bayan prioritize the deliberation and approval of the proposed HRMPSB to facilitate the recruitment and promotion of personnel in order to fill up the existing vacant positions to enhance the effectiveness and efficiency of its operations and the delivery of services.
observations.managementAction: The LGU hired 8 permanent employees for CY 2022

observations.recommendation:
We commended the Municipality of Pioduran for its effort in achieving a vaccination rate of 87.80 per cent of its target population, thus reducing hospitalization cases of its constituents. 

Further, we recommended that Management continue its implementation of the vaccination and booster dose program and widen the scope of its vaccine information drive to strengthen its constituents’ knowledge and protection against hospitalization and death from COVID-19.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:

Municipal Health Officer to furnish the Municipal Accountant with the receipt and utilization of all donations-in-kind received by the municipality; and  

Municipal Accountant to recognize in the books the donations-in-kind received and the corresponding inventory accounts in accordance with existing regulations.
We recommended that Management state in the Job Order Contract the duties and responsibilities and expected outputs, and the rate per day of the workers.

We recommended that Management to require the HRMO to adopt a schedule of standard rates for JOs per position that is based on qualification and the nature of work/assignment taking into consideration the prevailing minimum wage in the locality.

We recommended that Management submit all lacking documents relating to the cash advance and liquidation documents for the salaries and wages of JO personnel as required under COA Circular No. 2012-001 and in compliance with Section 4(6) of PD 1445.

We recommended that, henceforth, the HoPE approve the BAC Resolution Declaring the LCRB and Recommending Approval after the conduct of Post-Qualification and that the Municipality expedite the preparation and submission of requirements to the DA for the issuance of  NOA within 10 calendar days from receipt of the No Objection Letter 2 (NOL2), as required in Section B.30 of the PRDP Module 1: Guidelines for the Procurement of Infrastructure Subprojects under PRDP Second Additional Financing with EU Grant co-financing.

We recommended that, henceforth, the Municipality enter into contract within 15 calendar days from receipt by the winning bidder of the NOA, as required under Section B.30 of the PRDP Module 1: Guidelines for the Procurement of Infrastructure Subprojects under PRDP Second Additional Financing with EU Grant co-financing.

We recommended that Management ensure that cash advances granted to the SDO do not exceed his maximum cash accountabilities covered by his fidelity bond, otherwise, effect the increase of his fidelity bond commensurate to his accountabilities, in accordance with the Bureau of Treasury Circular No. 02-2009 dated August 6, 2009, in order to protect the interest of the Municipal government.
observations.managementAction: Cash Advances now monitored along with the Accountable Officer’s limit. The bond of the AO was also increased.

Strictly comply with the pertinent provisions of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular 97-002 in the grant, utilization, and liquidations of cash advances and enforce the immediate settlement of these cash advances; and

Request for write-off for dormant or non-moving accounts as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2016-005. The request should be duly supported with the documents required in the said Circular.

Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical inventory of the Municipality’s properties. Thereafter, prepare the RPCPPE in the prescribed form, i.e per PPE classification and submit the same to the Auditor not later than January 31 of each year.

The approved plan identified top seven hazards: (1) weather and geological (earthquake); (2) landslides; (3) erosions; (4) agricultural pests and diseases; (5) disease outbreak; (6) fire hazards and (7) traffic hazards. These were all considered urgent and priority. Therefore, the CCTV was charged to the LDRRMF.
Management informed that monitoring updated issuances which could affect their local governance performance scorecards is being undertaken. Monitoring updated issuances which could affect their local governance scorecards is being implemented.

observations.managementAction: Management took advantage of the media broadcasting to mitigate the worst effects of the pandemic and jumpstart a more resilient recovery. Management explained that media broadcasting amplified the plans, programs, and recovery agenda of Bayawan City to a wider audience including non-Bayawanon audience, encouraged growth of the local economy and captured investors’ interests.

No. 7160.

observations.recommendation:
58. We recommended further that, in subsequent trainings, Management ensure that personnel nominated to attend trainings are regular employees of the City.
59. We recommended further that require the JOs who attended the training to immediately cascade the knowledge and skills they learned from the training.
observations.managementAction:
Trainings and seminars are now attended by regular employees.
The JOs who attended the training already shared their knowledge to the CDRRMO.

observations.recommendation: 62. We recommended that the City Accountant issue demand letters to farmer-beneficiaries and submit liquidation report to account for the release/disposal of seedlings and fertilizers and submit a status report on the JPIP.
observations.managementAction: Management explained that some farmer-beneficiaries with overdue account for more than 10 years have been included in a Letter Request for Write-off submitted to COA.

observations.recommendation: 63. We recommended that Management henceforth observe strictly the applicable regulations on the grant of any fringe benefits.
observations.managementAction: Management already stopped the practice and ensured compliance with applicable laws and regulations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management facilitate the implementation of the projects that were not yet implemented. For those partially implemented PPAs, we recommended that management monitor their implementation to be able to complete the projects according to the specified contract duration, pursuant to DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No.2020-1.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer immediately enforce the issuance of ORs only after actual receipt/collection of payment pursuant to Section 68 of P.D. 1445, Section 29 of the Manual on NGAS, Volume I and Section 90 of Local Treasury Operations Manual, in order to install sound internal control practice in the collection process. Otherwise, the Municipal Treasurer and collectors shall be jointly liable for the loss of funds/collections following improper practice.

We recommended that management formulate innovative schemes and exercise robust measures to achieve target revenues and maximize collection thereof in consonance with Section 305 (g), Chapter I, Section 444 (a) and (3) Chapter III of the Republic Act (R.A.) No.7160 and keep up with the provisions of DOF D.O. No.006-2015 in order to achieve satisfactory performance in revenue generation.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to withhold the disallowed amount from the salaries of person or other compensation due the person liable in satisfaction of the disallowance or charge so as to restitute the local government unit of the much-needed fund to be used for its daily operations or other worthwhile projects.

10. We recommended that management, especially the Municipal Engineer and Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, immediately work on securing the necessary documents as required by COA Region Technical Services Office so as to establish the propriety and validity of these infrastructure contracts. Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant, henceforth, properly review the supporting documents attached to the disbursement vouchers to ensure that these consist of all the necessary documents required to be submitted.

11. We recommended that management instruct the Municipal accountant to properly monitor and check payments to BIR so as to avoid double remittances. Likewise, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant trace in the previous accounting records those balances in the “For Identification” account, in order to arrive at the correct Due to BIR account balance.

12. We recommended that management set aside funds for the installation of the prescribed e-NGAS both in the accounting and budget departments and henceforth, make use of the system in the submission of pertinent financial reports.

15. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive secure and maintain with responsible insurers such insurances for these completed projects to cover risk of fire, flood and other damages in accordance with the Tripartite Memorandum of Agreement for the DILG-BEA Fund to minimize risk of loss of funds and depletion of financial resources.

16. We recommended that Management submit immediately the necessary supporting documents required by the Office of the COA Chairman and furnish us copies of the duly received transmittal letters for the liquidation of CY 2011 and 2015 unliquidated cash advances of the present mayor, as well as those of the former mayors, if already transmitted. 
Also, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant refrain from granting additional cash advances if liquidation vouchers/reports for the previous cash advances are not yet submitted to the COA Chairman, duly supported by a received copy of the transmittal letter.

18. We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to ascertain whether or not these payable accounts are still due for remittance to concerned offices.  If these are still due for remittance, instruct the Municipal Treasurer to remit immediately the amount involved in order to protect the interest of the employee-members concerned and clear the books of these prior years’ payable accounts.

19. We recommended that Management submit copies of the perfected government contracts, purchase orders and all supporting documents within five (5) working days from execution/issuance in compliance with COA Circular No. 2009-001 to facilitate timely auditorial and technical review of contracts and purchase orders and to avoid imposition of administrative disciplinary action prescribed by laws.

20. We recommended that management and/or officials responsible for or in charge of accepting deliveries of procured items regardless of amount shall, within twenty-four (24) hours from such acceptance, furnish the Auditor with copies of the delivery receipts and inspection and acceptance report and notify the said Auditor of the deliveries in order to enable to prepare for and perform timely inspection of the same.

22. We recommended that the MDRRMO, MSWDO and all Punong Barangays submit written justifications duly signed by all officials and personnel concerned and involved in the materials distribution, as to the non-observance of the terms of the MOA between the NHA and LGU-Sagbayan and likewise, furnish COA documents that would support their assertions as to the agreement between the constituents and Sangguniang Barangay which transpired before the actual distribution of materials.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Petty Cash Custodian to take charge on the Petty Cash Fund by transferring his accountability to another municipal employees pursuant to Section 75 of PD 1445.

We recommend that management direct the Municipal Engineer, Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and other implementing officers to review, coordinate and strategize to optimally utilize the 20% Development Fund and regularly monitor the status of utilization and implementation of PPAs.

We recommend the Municipal Accountant to adhere to the accounting guidelines set forth in IPSAS 17 in the recording and accounting of completed projects.
8 We also recommend the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments in transferring the completed projects from the Trust Fund to the General Fund books in accordance of Section 104 of the NGAS Manual in order to present fairly the financial statements as of December 31, 2022.

We recommend that management require the Local Finance Committee and the Municipal Budget Officer to prepare a budget based on a reasonable income so as not to incur overdraft or loss in its market operations.
16 Likewise, minimize the expenses in relation to the said enterprise to at least break even by limiting total disbursements to fifty percent (50%) of the uncollected estimated revenue accruing to the fund in addition to the actual collections, provided that no cash overdraft shall be incurred, pursuant to Section 158 of the Government Accounting and Auditing Manual (GAAM), Volume I, in order not to incur net loss of current income.

We recommend that management require the municipal officials involve in the disbursement process to evaluate its payment procedures and institute strategies and corrective measures on the lapses without deviating from the existing laws, rules and regulations to minimize the delay in the settlement/payment of obligations. Also, management has to facilitate the preparation and evaluation of the supporting documents of the gasoline consumption for the period July to December 2021 and prepare the necessary adjusting entries, if necessary.

We recommend that the Management direct the MPDO to evaluate and re-assess the projects covered in the continuing appropriations if the same are still responsive to existing needs of its constituents, otherwise, reprogram the funds to more viable and significant projects in accordance with the socio-economic needs of the Municipality.
30 We also recommend that Management direct the MPDO to coordinate with the Municipal Engineer and the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to fast track the implementation of the same so that any cause of suspension and/or problems in the course of implementation can be immediately addressed.

Instruct the Municipal Assessor, Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer and all other concerned personnel to work for immediate titling, recording or transfer of ownership of all land belonging to the LGU.
32 Record all acquired real properties in the books of the municipality upon securing proper and valid papers to support the documentation /titling.
33 Intensify the verification process and titling of the Land owing to the LGU and booked up in the LGU’s account the said lots to establish ownership and avoid the risk of court litigation from the previous owners who are interested to take back the properties.
34 Book up in the LGU books the correct land valuation in order to present an accurate land account in the financial statement.

We recommend that the Sangguniang Bayan expedite the revision and updating of the Local Revenue Code to be able to adjust and/or update the taxes and fees rates pursuant to Sections 129 and 191 of RA 7160 to meet the financial needs of the LGU.

We recommended that the OIC-City Accountant:
4. Record all the transactions of the City using the proper accounts provided in the Revised Chart of Accounts for LGUs so that transactions are faithfully and correctly presented in the financial statements to provide reliable information to its users.
5. Prepare the necessary adjusting entries to correct the balances of the Due to NGAs, Due to LGUs and Hospital Fees in the financial statements; and
6. Exert effort in substantiating the Due to NGAs account balance and make the necessary correcting entries, if necessary.
7. We also recommended that the City Treasurer verify the source/funding agency of the ₱300,000.00 financial assistance and ask for a copy of the MOA as guide for its utilization if it is still allowed to be disbursed, otherwise, return the amount to the source/funding agency concerned.
observations.managementAction: Implemented.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor:
14. Instruct the departments concerned to hasten the implementation of the on-going and unimplemented PPAs funded by various NGAs; and
15. Require the OIC-City Accountant to prepare the Disbursement Voucher for the return of the unutilized amount totaling ₱3,723,247.51 from completed projects and submit the required Liquidation Reports to the funding agencies concerned or request the transfer thereof to the General Fund of the City, if the disposition of the unexpended balances is not provided in the MOA.
16. We also recommended for the return of the unexpended amount of ₱50 million to the Office of the President considering that the intended purpose of the fund have already been given to the city, or request for a change of the MOA for a replacement of the item to be procured, considering that the City Hospital still needs a lot of equipment to be provided.
17. We further recommended that the Local Finance Committee and other concerned City personnel update and submit the Liquidation Report/Report of Disbursement on a monthly basis for all NGA funded projects.
observations.managementAction: Implemented (departments instructed; return actions and liquidation report submissions as recommended).

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor, as Chairman of the CDRRM Council, through the CDRRM Officer: 18. Conduct a quarterly monitoring on the implemented projects and activities with reference to the LDRRMFIP. 19. Re-examine and evaluate the PPAs of prior years, and determine the issues/concerns for non-implementation of certain projects, and consider them in the preparation of the Annual LDRRMFIP. 20. Prepare the LDRRMFIP with the PPAs therein stated in specific terms and aligned with the NDRRM Plan. 21. Optimally utilize the LDRRM Fund to adequately equip the constituents in their preparation to manage disaster risks towards a resilient community in times of calamities.
observations.managementAction: Implemented / Not implemented (varied by subrecommendation).
observations.reason: Reiterated in this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor: 22. File request of authority from COA with the due process to derecognize non-existing/missing PPEs and unserviceable properties; (Note: in original table the recommendation is actually: We recommended that the City Treasurer update and prepare the required RPT/Taxpayer’s Index Card and thereafter, at every beginning of the year, furnish the OIC-City Accountant with the duly certified list showing the names of the taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year, which shall be the basis for the setting up of the RPT/SET Receivable accounts.)
observations.managementAction: Implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Market Administrator, under the supervision of the City Administrator as per Section 5 of the Market Code of the City of Baybay, examine and prepare a complete and updated list of the actual qualified occupants of the market stalls and submit the same to the City Legal Officer for the preparation and execution of the Contracts of Lease. We also recommended that the Legal Officer submit to the Audit Team copies of the duly executed Contract of Lease for audit reference. We further recommended that the City Treasurer demand the collection of all the delinquent unpaid rentals and impose the necessary surcharges and enforcement remedies. Lastly, we recommended that the Market Committee headed by the City Mayor revisit the city market ordinances and provide for clear-cut procedures and amendments that will remedy the deficiencies noted to help improve market operations.
observations.managementAction: Implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor: 27. Promulgate policies, standards and guidelines in hiring of Job Orders and Contract of Services as consultants. 28. Limit the hiring of JO personnel to complement only the office where transactions are voluminous; and 29. To instruct the Legal Officer to submit a Contract of Service for each COS/JO personnel and ensure that the functions and responsibilities of the COS/JOs are well-defined and clear in their respective Contracts of Service to facilitate evaluation of work performed by the employees. We also recommended for the Human Services and Management Officer to: 30. Review the staffing pattern and service requirements in the organizational structure of the City, assess the necessity of hiring consultants as Service Providers and render a report thereon to the City Mayor. 31. Check on the Service Providers to be hired as consultants that they do not perform the functions of regular employees and/or department heads and 32. Fill up all vacant plantilla positions of the city before considering hiring of JO/Contract Service. 33. Plan for additional plantilla positions to be put up to complement for the additional work brought about by the expansion of the City’s medical and technical facilities.
observations.managementAction: Implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor: 39. Direct the Acting GSO Officer to see to it that all properties owned by the City are insured with the General Insurance Fund of the GSIS as mandated by RA No. 656, as amended by PD No. 245; and 40. Ensure that the insurance premiums of these properties are included in the proposed Annual Budget.
observations.managementAction: Implemented.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following:

a) 	(Deleted –

b) 	(Deleted –

This campaign should be conducted by proper officials, with plan set of activities separate from other activities or events in order to attain its main purpose; and

d) 	With the corroborative effort of the Municipal Treasurer’s Office and Municipal Assessor’s Office, adopt collection remedies as necessary as provided in the above cited provisions.

We recommended that the Management limit the charging to Representation Expenses to what is only lawfully appropriated in the annual budget and ensure that disbursement of funds is for public purposes and from available appropriations, otherwise, an overdraft in appropriations or an incurrence of obligations without appropriations may be incurred, and to avoid similar observation or suspension/disallowance of transaction in the future.

We recommended that the Municipal Vice Mayor, Municipal Accountant and Designated GSO be included in the list of bondable public officers of the LGU and be properly bonded with the Bureau of the Treasury.

We recommended that management maximize the utilization of the GAD funds through the implementation of GAD-related programs and projects in order to attain the objective for which the funds were provided.
We recommended that management assign a separate Responsibility Center and RC code for the GFPS which shall serve as the RC for GAD-related expenses and GAD-related financial transactions.

We recommended to management the following:

a) Local Chief Executive submit a Report on Infrastructure Projects for CY 2022 that the required appropriate signboards and/or public notices relative to the projects are already posted;

b) For CY 2023 and onwards, the Local Chief Executive inform religiously within ten (10) days after award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity that the appropriate signboards and/or public notices are already posted; and

c) For CY 2023 and onwards, the Municipal Engineer prepare and submit the Monthly Monitoring Report for all infrastructure projects.
We recommended that Management comply with the Property Insurance Law in insuring all serviceable properties with GSIS.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to draw Journal Entry Vouchers to effect the necessary adjustments to correct the misclassified accounts.
observations.managementAction: No action was taken by Management.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant was unable to adjust the misclassified account.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to review all transactions and accounting entries that affect the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts and determine the cause of the discrepancy. Likewise, make the necessary adjusting entries to bring both balances in agreement, thereby, ensuring the reliability of the reciprocal accounts.
observations.managementAction: No action was taken by Management.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant was unable to reconcile the reciprocal accounts due to the voluminous work of the accounting unit.

observations.recommendation: a. include in their Annual Investment Plan (AIP) programs, projects, and activities that have an impact on health and environmental issues to control the spread of the virus; b. provide additional allocation on PPAs for COVID-19 preventive measures as listed under Section 2.1 of the DILG-DBM JMC No. 01 dated March 27, 2020; c. evaluate the necessity of the unimplemented projects and reprogram the same to more important development projects that will cater to the present needs of its constituents; and d. ensure the optimal utilization of the fund to help achieve the desired socio-economic development and environmental outcomes of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction:

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:

the OIC-Provincial Assessor instruct the Municipal Assessors to:

Conduct inspection of the real properties within their respective jurisdictions to identify idle lands;

Identify the owners of the idle properties based on the tax map; and

Prepare a list of all idle lands located within their area of jurisdiction subject to the special levy and furnish the Provincial Assessor with a copy thereof.

the OIC-Provincial Assessor furnish the OIC-Provincial Treasurer with a copy of the listing of idle lands within the Province of Albay for her information.
observations.managementAction:
PAssO will furnish the PTO of the final list of idle lands once the management decided to impose the collection of the 5% ad valorem tax.

The Prov’l. Gov’t. of Albay through the PAssO, using the new computerized  system was able to generate the list of uncultivated land/ properties, which are considered as idle lands of the fifteen (15) municipalities of the province. The list of uncultivated lands/ properties generated from the system bears all necessary information per municipality such as number of brgys., lots, areas, market values, assessed values and the  5% ad valorem tax.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a. MGSO and the Municipal Accountant to conduct periodic reconciliation of
their records and make the necessary corrections or adjustments on the
discrepancies noted between their records; and
b. Municipal Accountant to draw a Journal Entry Voucher to drop the semi-
expendable assets from the PPE accounts and ensure that those items are
covered by ICS.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to make
sure that the Cash in Bank balances in the Statement of Financial Position should
tally with the balances in the Bank Reconciliation Statements and to carefully
review all the accounting entries and the recording of transactions in the General
Ledger.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant:
a. to review all transactions and accounting entries that affect the Due from
Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts to determine the cause of the
discrepancy and prepare the necessary adjusting entries to bring both
balances in agreement thus, ensuring the reliability of the reciprocal
accounts; and
b. conduct periodic analysis of the reciprocal account balances to prevent or
minimize the discrepancies.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
draw a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) transferring the unexpended balance of
LDRRMF to the Trust Liability-DRRMF account totaling P5,365,763.24 and provide
necessary disclosures in the Notes to Financial Statements as required in
Sections 5.1.12 and 5.1.16 of the COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal officials to
design or adopt measures and strategies to remedy the high expenditures in the
operation of the economic enterprises. Also, revisit and update the Municipality’s
Revenue Code to adjust the tax rates therein to meet the current operating costs
of these economic enterprises.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Disaster Risk
Reduction and Management Council (MDRRMC) to monitor the available budget
and utilization of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund
(LDRRMF) to effectively implement the planned PPAs within the targeted schedule
and to ensure the optimal utilization of the fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Risk Reduction
and Management Officer (MDRRMO) to refrain from using the five percent LDRRM
Fund to pay various operating expenses of the MDRRM Office and other expenses
not related to the purpose of the fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Bids and Awards Committee
(BAC) to immediately submit the required documents to support the noted
infrastructure projects.

We also recommend the Municipal Accountant ensure that all disbursements are
supported with complete documents before processing or approving future
payments.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to:
a. enforce collection of the delinquent real property taxes by conducting tax
campaigns in each barangay; and
b. send demand letters to the delinquent taxpayers, otherwise, avail of the
remedies provided under Sections 254 and 256 of RA No. 7160.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct:
a. the Municipal Accountant and other concerned officials to submit the required
lacking documents and ensure that subsequent payments are properly
supported with complete documents;
b. the Bids and Awards Committee to strictly comply with the audit regulation on
the submission of the BAC Resolution as a request for the alternative mode of
procurement;
c. the Municipal General Services Officer on the timely submission of purchase
orders to ensure timely evaluation thereof.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Person to:
a. ensure that all planned PPAs embodied in the GAD Plan and Budget are
implemented during the year so that the intent of GAD on promoting gender-
responsive governance, protecting and fulfilling women’s human rights, and
promoting women’s empowerment as mandated could be fully achieved;
b. furnish the Audit Team with a copy of GAD AR within five working days from the
end of January of the ensuing year, pursuant to COA Circular No. 2014-001; and
c. submit the GAD AR together with the summary of the reported program or
project, copies of reported policy issuances, results of HGDG tests, and actions
taken by the LGU on the COA audit findings and recommendations, in
compliance with Section 4.1.C.8 PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01, as
amended.

We recommended to management the following:
Submit a document of authority such as Office Orders or Memoranda, indicating the names of persons who were assigned to COVID-19 wards or units directly catering to or in contact with COVID-19 patients, PUIs, and PUMs, and the period of their assignment;
Submit a legal and reasonable justification on the payment for COVID-19 SRA to its employees assigned in areas who are not directly catering to or in contact with COVID-19 patients, PUIs, and PUMs, to enable the audit team to make a decision in audit; and
Strictly adhere to the provisions of RA No. 11469 of the “Bayanihan to Heal as One Act” and subsequent issuances relative thereof to address the needs on the national emergency arising from the COVID-19 pandemic.

We recommended to management the following actions:
Submit the required documents to support the completeness, validity, and authenticity of the AHDP payments such as:
MOA between the Provincial Government of Albay and the DOH
Documents of authority for the entitlement of the AHDP allowance such as certification or office/assignment orders covering assignment of HRHs to hospitals, laboratories, or medical quarantine facilities, and whose official duties and responsibilities are directly related to the healthcare response of the government to COVID-19;
DTRs or duly accomplished time records of employees;
Certified or authorized document by the head of agency/office establishing that frontline HRHs physically report for work at their assigned work stations during the state of national emergency; and
Strictly adhere to the provisions of RA no. 11494 or the “Bayanihan to Recover as One Act” and subsequent issuances relative thereof to address the needs on the national emergency arising from the COVID-19 pandemic.

We recommended that Management:
Observe the monthly preparation of Bank Reconciliation Statements to validate the balance of the Cash in Bank account in the financial statements in compliance with Section 3 of COA Circular No. 96-011;
Record and adjust immediately in the respective books the reconciling items found to be valid; and
Follow-up the requests from concerned depository banks copies of the Bank Credit Memos and Debit Memos to determine their validity and effect the necessary adjustments.

We recommended that Management instruct the Provincial Treasurer to coordinate with the Accountant and conduct regular reconciliation of their balances so that the adjustments made or errors detected by either party will be adjusted and posted in their respective books.

We recommended that Management direct the Provincial Treasurer:
To maintain complete cashbooks for Cash in Bank accounts that are updated and balanced daily, ruled, footed, and certified correct by the accountable officer concerned for easy monitoring of the transactions affecting those accounts;
To issue a certification as to the correctness and completeness of the recorded transactions at month-end after the last entry in the cashbook; and
To periodically reconcile with the Provincial Accountant’s cash records to substantiate validity and correctness of balances of each account.

We recommended that Management:
Require the Collecting Officers to turn over or deposit their collections regularly and intact in accordance with existing rules and regulations to avoid the risk of possible loss, misuse, and misappropriation of government funds.
Monitor and ensure that collections are turned over/deposited by the Accountable Officer on time and intact.

We recommended that Management instruct and require the:
Provincial Accountant to evaluate the monthly disbursements of the AOs based on their PCF transactions;
Accountable Officer to compute an appropriate amount sufficient only for one month’s requirement and request replenishment when the disbursements reach at least 75%, or as the need requires, by submitting a replenishment voucher with all supporting documents duly summarized in Petty Cash Replenishment Report; and
Provincial Accountant to address the delays experienced by the AOs in the processing of their replenishment checks by giving priority to PCF replenishments.

We recommended that the Petty Cash Custodian replenish the Petty Cash Fund when disbursements reach at least 75 per cent or as the need requires and to immediately discontinue the payment for supplies and materials out of the petty cash fund specifically established for meals during committee and public hearings.

We recommended that the Provincial Accountant file the request for authority to write-off dormant receivable accounts, unliquidated cash advances, and fund transfers thru the COA Audit Team Leader and Supervising Auditor.

Likewise, we recommended that Management instruct the Provincial Accountant to:
Conduct regular and periodic verification, analysis, and validation of the existence of the receivables, unliquidated cash advances, and fund transfers,  and  determine  the  concerned  debtors,  accountable  officers
(Regular and Special Disbursing Officers, Collecting Officers, Cashiers) and the source and implementing government entities concerned;
Prepare the quarterly schedule of all receivables, unliquidated cash advances and fund transfers;
Reconcile the unliquidated fund transfers between the source and implementing government entities, prepare the adjusting entries for the reconciling items noted, and require liquidation of the balances;
Prepare the necessary adjusting entry/ies for the following:
Recognition of the computed/determined impairment in accordance with the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards or Philippine Financial Reporting Standards
Correction of inadvertent errors, or inaccurate calculation or computation
Reclassification of accounts
Recovery/settlement of previously written off accounts
Prepare aging of dormant receivables, unliquidated cash advances, and fund transfers on a quarterly basis to support the request for write-off, and indicate in the remarks column the existence of the applicable conditions, as follows:
Absence of records or documents to validate/support the claim and/or unreconciled reciprocal accounts
Death of the accountable officer/employee/debtor
Unknown whereabouts of the accountable officer/employee/debtor, and that he/she could not be located despite diligent efforts to find him/her
Incapacity to pay or insolvency
Exhaustion of all possible remedies by the Management to collect the receivables and to demand liquidation of cash advances and fund transfers
No pending case in court involving the subject dormant accounts.

We recommended that Management direct the Provincial Accounting Office to review and analyze the unexpended balances in the Due to NGAs account and facilitate the immediate return of the unused balances to the Bureau of Treasury or source agencies, if warranted or revert to General Fund the balances which are not required by law or contractual agreement to be returned.

We recommended that Management follow the provisions of COA Circular 2020-006 on the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances and direct the Inventory Committee to complete the inventory taking of all PPE of the Provincial Government and upon completion of the physical count, prepare the RPCPPE.

Likewise, we recommended that Management direct:
the Provincial General Services Officer to –
i. Maintain complete, updated and properly accomplished Property Cards for each category of PPE listed in the accounting records and ensure that the balances shown thereon agree with the prepared RPCPPE and the book balances; and
ii. Undertake collaborative procedures with the PAO in the reconciliation of property and accounting records;
b. the Provincial Accountant to –
i. Maintain complete, updated and properly accomplished PPELCs for each class of PPE, listing therein the data as to its acquisition, description, custody, useful life and other necessary information;
ii. Undertake collaborative procedures with the GSO in the reconciliation of accounting and property records; and
iii. Reconcile subsidiary records against the PPE ledger balances to ascertain the validity and accuracy of the account balances and ensure fair presentation thereof in the Financial Statements.

We recommended that Management continue to pursue improved practices in planning, programming and implementing programs and projects and ensure that the development projects to be funded out of the 20% DF are well-planned and
procurement-and- implementation-ready to maximize the utilization of the Local
Development Fund in attaining desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes for the benefit of the community.

We recommended that Management:
Establish an open and organized monitoring and communication system among offices, particularly those involved with the implementation of pandemic related PPAs;
Incorporate and implement PPAs under the 30 per cent QRF that address disaster response requirements of the COVID-19 emergency with emphasis on PPAs that are geared towards rehabilitation and recovery from the pandemic, in accordance with the provisions of RA No. 11494 or the Bayanihan to Recover as One Act; and
Coordinate with National Government Agencies tasked to implement programs in the Rehabilitation & Recovery from COVID-19 in preparing medium and long-term mandated plans to sustain rehabilitation and recovery initiatives addressing COVID-19 impacts.

Maximize the utilization of the LDRRMF from current year’s appropriation and prior years’ unexpended balance; and
Formulate a comprehensive and integrated LDRRMP in accordance with RA No. 10121 in close coordination with the LDRRMO/APSEMO employees to implement and monitor LDRRM projects and activities related to disaster prevention, mitigation, and preparedness to ascertain that it will be implemented within the scheduled timeframes.

We reiterated our previous years’ audit recommendation that the Provincial Accountant submit the financial reports required under the Schedule of Financial Reports Submission (Annex S) of the PRDP Financial Operations Guidelines within the prescribed periods.

We recommended that Management cause the immediate return of unexpended funds to DA – PSO and the reversion of related Trust Liabilities to the General Fund, to enhance the accuracy and reliability of the amounts presented in the financial statements, and to prevent these amounts from forming part of the Province’s dormant accounts.

We recommended that Management submit a satisfactory explanation and/or justification regarding the disbursement amounting ₱765,500.00 in order for us to arrive at a decision in audit.

Likewise, we recommended that, henceforth, Management strictly adhere to the guidelines of DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017 and its addenda to ensure that disbursements are included in the allowable expenditure therewith and to consider the paramount needs of the teachers and students at this present learning modality.

Henceforth, we recommended that the Provincial Government of Albay strictly observe the guidelines in the preparation of Approved Budget for the Contract to obtain bid prices that will be most advantageous to the government.

We recommended that the Provincial Engineer submit a satisfactory explanation and/or justification on the exceptions/discrepancies noted in the Technical Evaluation Report for further evaluation, without prejudice to the issuance of a Notice of Disallowance, if warranted.

We recommended that the Provincial Government of Albay strictly observe the guidelines in the preparation of the Approved Budget for the Contract to obtain bid prices that will be most advantageous to the government.

We recommended that Management define the roles and responsibilities of the LCE in the administration of the LGU GAD in compliance with the provisions of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01.

We recommended that Management incorporate in their GAD Plan and Budget PPAs directly providing assistance and protection for rape victims, in compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014.

We recommended that Management direct all devolved/field hospitals to submit their respective work and financial plans prior to the release of their budgets and ensure compliance with the provisions of COA Circular 2000-003.

We recommended that Management:
Review personnel complement and consider designating persons-in- charge like FH accountants and cashiers to facilitate the generation of reports and maintenance of accounts.
Create a separate account for each FH instead of all FH accounts being integrated in the GF-proper account.
Prepare all required reports for these FHs and ensure compliance with the provisions of COA Circular 2000-003.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
Consider appointing Job Order personnel as regular employees if qualified and believe that their services are essential and contributory to the improvement of agency’s operations;
Study the need to revisit its current staffing pattern to ensure appropriate manpower complement; and
Formulate a policy and internal control in hiring Job Orders such as providing for specific and detailed description of duties and responsibilities to eliminate overlapping with other functions of regular/permanent employees; and
Fill up the vacant plantilla positions in accordance with Section 463 of RA No. 7160 and CSC rules and regulations, for more effective administration of the offices concerned which in turn will benefit the Province as a whole.

We recommended that Management provide a TOR, or in the contract with each consultant the specific nature of work assignments and/or specific duties and responsibilities and quantity of work to be accomplished and the desired work outputs to facilitate comparison and evaluation of actual work accomplishments of hired consultants.

We recommended that Management develop a standard process for the evaluation of consultants’ services with the consideration of whether their services require technical and professional expertise which could not be undertaken by the employees of the provincial government and that the costs outweighed the benefits and set the criteria for the renewal. Moreover, Management requires the concerned heads of departments/offices with the assistance of the HRMO to evaluate the recommendations for rehiring the consultants’ services to ensure that the renewal is necessary and based on criteria set prior to the recommendation for approval by the Governor.

We recommended that Management:
Ensure awareness of the Reconciliation Billing Issue and compliance with the Agency Remittance Advice requirement to reflect the changes in the employee records;
Conduct the immediate review and regular reconciliation of employees’ deductions against remittances made; and
Remit any balance due to the GSIS after reconciling the amounts  to ensure the continued availment by affected employees of GSIS benefits and privileges.

a. OIC-Municipal Accountant and OIC-GSO to conduct periodic reconciliation of their records and make the necessary adjustments on both records to reflect the correct balance of the PPE accounts; b. OIC-GSO to locate the PPEs not found during the conduct of the physical count, if established missing, take immediate and appropriate action so that the accountable person will be held liable for the loss of the noted properties; c. OIC-GSO to include in the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable property (IIRUP) the unserviceable watercraft for proper disposal; d. OIC-Municipal Accountant and the OIC-Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustment and correct the recorded balance and properly reconcile the PPEs; and e. OIC-Municipal Accountant to draw a Journal Entry Voucher to drop the semi-expendable assets from the PPE accounts and ensure that those items are covered by ICS.
observations.managementAction: The OIC-Municipal General Services Officer has adopted the One-Time Cleansing of Property, Plant, and Equipment in compliance with COA Circular No, 2020-006 dated January 21, 2020. The adjustments were not yet taken up by the Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation: a. Municipal Assessor to prioritize the transfer of title of the parcels of land in the name of the Municipal Government of Bay, in compliance with Section 39(2) of PD No. 1445 and Article 47(c) of IRR of RA No. 7160; b. OIC-Municipal Accountant and MGSO to coordinate with the Municipal Assessor. c. OIC-Municipal Accountant to record in the books of account the school site amounting to P8,852,750.00 by debiting Land Account and crediting Government Equity account; d. Municipal Assessor and the OIC-Municipal Accountant to regularly reconcile their records to ascertain the correctness of the Land account in the Financial Statement; e. Municipal Assessor to determine the current fair market values and assessed values of the recorded parcels of land and provide updated data to the Office of the OIC-Municipal Accountant; and f. OIC-Municipal Accountant to adequately disclose in the Notes to the Financial Statements the complete details of the Land account and its corresponding current fair market value as provided by the Municipal Assessor.
observations.managementAction:
The OIC-GSO has complied with the said RLRN format and submitted it to the COA on June 29, 2021.
No action was taken by the Management.
The OIC-GSO already conducted regular reconciliation of their PPE accounts.
The OIC-Municipal Accountant made the necessary adjustment in the PPE accounts prepared JEVs to drop the semi-expandable expenses (JEV Nos. 2021-09-00376184, 2021-10-4338-39, and 2021-10-434142).
observations.reason: The Municipal Assessor was unable to give the current fair market value to the accounting office.

observations.recommendation: a. instruct the Municipal General Services Officer (MGSO) to prepare and submit RLRN following the prescribed format; b. request the Municipal Engineer to provide the MGSO and OIC-Municipal Accountant the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects as required under COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 25, 2015; and c. OIC-Municipal Accountant to comply with the disclosure requirement/format for the road network system in the NTFS.
observations.managementAction:
The OIC-GSO has complied with the said RLRN format and submitted it to the COA on June 29, 2021.
No action was taken by the Management.
The MGSO has submitted the report of the inventory of the road network to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: The Municipal Engineer was unable to provide the OIC-Municipal Accountant with the cost of segregation of road projects.

observations.recommendation: a. OIC-Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to reconcile the difference between the accounts Receivables and their contra accounts; and b. OIC-Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments, furnishing the Office of the Auditor with a year-end copy of the Journal Entry Voucher taking into account the adjustments made.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Treasurer and OIC-Municipal Accountant are regularly reconciling their accounts on RPT Receivables.
No action was taken by the Management.
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasurer insists that no adjustment is needed.

observations.recommendation: a. OIC-Municipal Accountant and OIC-Municipal General Services Officer (GSO) to strictly adopt the Perpetual Inventory Method in accounting for procured inventory items; b. OIC-Municipal GSO to ensure the conduct of a complete physical count of inventories and the periodic reconciliation of inventories with the accounting records to facilitate timely verification and adjustment of the discrepancy, if there is any.
observations.managementAction: The OIC-MGSO is now applying the perpetual inventory system. The OIC-MGSO and the Municipal Accountant have started the reconciliation of their records.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower.

observations.recommendation: a. OIC-Municipal Accountant to correctly classify the outstanding balance of the long-term loans payable by presenting the portion of loans due for settlement within twelve months in the amount of P6,423,700.48 as part of the current liabilities and the remaining balance of P11,241,475.75 which is due for settlement beyond one year as non-current liabilities in the Statement of Financial Position.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has separated the current and non-current portions of the long-term loans.

observations.recommendation: a. Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer and the OIC-Municipal Accountant to reconcile the amount of unexpended balance of the LDRRMF between the Utilization Report and the Trust Liability – DRRMF account reflected in the Statement of Financial Position; b. OIC-Municipal Accountant, upon reconciliation of the unexpended balances, draw a Journal Entry Voucher to adjust the recorded balance of Trust Liability- DRRMF account under Trust Fund; and c. OIC-Municipal Accountant to disclose the amount and details of the unexpended balances of the LDRRMF in the Notes to Financial Statements as required in Sections 5.1.12 and 5.1.16 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.managementAction:
a. The DRRMO and OIC-Municipal Accountant are reconciling the unexpended balances.
b. A Journal Entry was made for the transfer of the unexpended balances.
No action was taken by the OIC-Municipal Accountant to disclose the details of the unexpended balances of the LDRRMF in the NTFS.
observations.reason: The observation was overlooked by the OIC-Municipal Assessor.

observations.recommendation: a. OIC-Municipal Accountant and other concerned officials to submit the lacking documents and ensure that subsequent payments are properly supported with complete documents; and b. OIC-General Services Officer to properly fill up the Purchase Requests and Purchase Orders in accordance with its official design for stronger internal control.
observations.managementAction:
