The OIC-Municipal Accountant adhered to the observations taken and will ensure that all disbursements shall be supported with complete documents before the approval of such.
The OIC-Municipal General Services Officer apologized for the late submission of the Purchase Orders in the year 2021. It was during that year that Bay, Laguna was at a high rate of COVID-19 positive and most of the signatories and personnel in the preparation of the underlying documents have undergone quarantine procedures and some have become positive patients, thus there was a deferment in the processing of the Purchase.
The BAC Chairperson committed that BAC Resolution will be submitted for the alternative mode of procurement.
The full implementation of the audit recommendation will be in the year 2022.
observations.reason: Some lacking documents were submitted to the Audit Team. The Municipality was at a high rate of COVID-19 positive cases, hence, most of the signatories and personnel involved in the preparation of the POs were COVID-19 positive and required to undergo strict quarantine procedures.

observations.recommendation: a. OIC-Municipal Accountant and other concerned officials to properly submit the Purchase Requests and Purchase Orders to be procured instead of granting cash advances for purposes not allowed under COA Circular No. 97-002 and indicate the necessary data in the granting of cash advances.
observations.managementAction: The OIC-Municipal Accountant has complied with COA Circular No. 97-002 on the granting of cash advances.

observations.recommendation: a. update and improve the existing Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Plan of the Municipality by focusing on the programs, projects, and activities requiring the highest priority in terms of urgency and impact on the four thematic areas of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management; b. adequately plan the PPAs to be included in the AIP and ensure proper allocation of funds to address the needs of the constituents to curtail and eliminate the threat of COVID-19; and c. monitor the available budget and utilization of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (LDRRMF) to effectively implement the planned PPAs within the targeted schedule and to ensure the optimal utilization of the fund.
observations.managementAction:
The MDDRMC had updated the existing plan.
The PPA's were included in the AIP to address the pandemic needs and some seminars were not conducted due to the pandemic.

observations.recommendation: a. Municipal Budget Officer to provide budgetary requirements for personal services, maintenance and other operating expenditures, and capital outlay of the MDRRM Office; and b. Municipal Risk Reduction and Management Officer to refrain from using the five percent LDRRM Fund to pay various operating expenses of the MDRRM Office and other expenses not related to the purpose of the fund.
observations.managementAction:
The PS was included in the CY 2021 Annual Budget.
The MDRRM Officer refrains from using the five percent DRRM Fund to pay for the operating expenses of the MDRRM office & other expenses not related to the purpose of the fund.
observations.reason: There were still disbursements in CY 2021 that should not be disbursed from the LDRRMF.

observations.recommendation: a. require the OIC-Municipal Accountant to prepare the Report on the Sources and Utilization of the DRRMF; and b. instruct the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office (MDRRMO) to submit the monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of the DRRMF to the COA Auditor as required under 5.1.5 of COA Circular 2012-002 for monitoring and evaluation.
observations.managementAction: The monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization was prepared and submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: a. furnish the Audit Team with a copy of GAD AR within five working days from the end of January of the ensuing year, in compliance with COA Circular No. 2014-001; and b. submit the GAD AR together with the required attachments as provided under Section 4.1.C.8 PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01, as amended, namely: (1) summary of the reported program or project; (2) copies of reported policy issuances; (3) results of HGDG tests; and (4) actions taken by the LGU on the COA audit findings and recommendations.
observations.managementAction:
The GAD Focal Person submitted the GAD Accomplishment Report on February 16, 2022 and is in the process of compiling the required attachments.
The GAD Focal Person was newly assigned and still in the process of familiarizing her functions and duties; hence, unable to submit the attachments on time.
observations.reason: The GAD Focal person was newly assigned and was unable to submit the actions taken by the LGU on the COA audit findings and recommendations on time.

observations.recommendation: a. implement the PPAs in due coordination with other Department/Office Heads to adopt a coordinated action plan for the programs, projects and activities for the protection and upliftment of children; and b. ensure compliance with Section 15 of RA No. 9344 and DILG MC No. 2012-120.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality did conduct meetings with the concerned department offices and implemented some PPAs as feasible during the pandemic.
observations.reason: Meetings and gatherings were prohibited due to the COVID-19 pandemic.

observations.recommendation: a. consider conducting an urgent meeting with the Department/Office Heads to tackle the issues and concerns contained in the AAR to adopt a coordinated action plan; b. submit the duly accomplished AAPSI on all audit observations and audit recommendations as soon as the appropriate plan of action is finalized; and c. ensure that the Municipality’s AAPSI is submitted to the Audit Team within 60 days from receipt of the AAR, in compliance with Section 93 of RA No. 10651.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality conducted an urgent meeting with the department to tackle the issues of the AAR and prepared the AAPSI but did not submit within the prescribed period due to other administrative priorities.
observations.reason: Due to different concerns of each department the AAPSI was prepared but not submitted on time.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management strictly adhere to the guidelines on the utilization of government grants for COVID-19, giving priority to programs, projects, and activities that address the pandemic, to curtail and eliminate its threat to the community.
observations.managementAction: The cardiotocography machine and the umbilical cord clamp will be used exclusively for pregnant Locally Stranded Individual (LSIs) who are in the quarantine period. Henceforth, guidelines  will be adhere strictly specially on the utilization of grants, not only with Covid-19 but to all grants for that matter.

observations.recommendation: We also recommended that compliance to posting requirements and other pertinent regulations be maintained to continuously uphold transparency and accountability in the procurement transactions of the Province.
observations.managementAction: Management disseminated information to all the concerned personnel to strictly comply with the pertinent regulations and inform them of management’s thrust to uphold transparency and accountability in all procurement transactions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the PSWDO prepare the categorized Summary/List of Donations Received,  Distributed, and  Balances together with its supporting documents and submit the same to the NDRRMC, through the OCD, pursuant to the guidelines laid out by COA Circular No. 2020-009. We also recommended that a copy of the summary be furnished the Accounting Office for recording purposes in the books of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: The report was already submitted to NDRRMC thru OCD. Henceforth, copy of Summary/ List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances will be regularly prepared and submitted to the concerned agencies and copy furnished COA and the Accounting Department. The delay in submission was due to   the   busy   schedule   in delivering goods and services to the constituents.

observations.recommendation:
We           recommended            that: The Provincial Treasurer forward the unclaimed     stale     checks     to     the Provincial Accountant, together with the Disbursement Vouchers (DVs) for recording.  Henceforth, she shall send a written notice, at least a month before the check becomes stale to payees of checks which have remained unclaimed; and

The Provincial Accountant, based on the Bank Reconciliation Statement, revert the total amount of stale checks outstanding to the Cash-in Bank and Accounts Payable accounts to ensure the proper presentation of the account balances and set up as Accounts Payable the unclaimed stale checks forwarded by the Provincial Treasurer.
observations.managementAction:
A memorandum  was prepared by the PTO to monitor the status of checks drawn to ensure that all checks are accounted for and communicated to the payees.

Various JEV were drawn to revert the stale check to cash- in- bank accounts and other adjusting entries.

BRS were prepared as soon as the documents/reports necessary for its preparation are available.

The management is in the process of reorganizing the Accounting Department to address the issue on the late submission of reports. A Job order personnel was hired to augment      the      personnel complement          of         the Accounting Department.

Management has already made representation with the depository banks with regard to the prompt issuance of bank statements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management ensure that officers/employees who perform duties requiring control, custody or possession of government funds or property be appropriately bonded in accordance with law.
observations.managementAction: A personnel shall be assigned to regularly review the bonding requirements of the accountable officers and recommend necessary adjustments to ensure that sufficient amounts of bond are followed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the OIC- Provincial Treasurer orient  and require the AO on the maintenance of a cashbook  and  accompanying  reports pursuant to Section 28 of the Manual on NGAS for Local Government Units, Volume II.
observations.managementAction: A cashbook for  cash advances has been set up and maintained upon audit instance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the AO stop depositing the fund to his personal savings account and Management provide the Disbursing Officer safe/vault or a secured cabinet to be used for safekeeping of the fund and related documents and records under his custody.
observations.managementAction: A cash box has been provided the Disbursing Officer and a drawer with lock has been installed where the disbursing Officer can keep his cash and other accountabilities.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the OIC- Provincial Treasurer direct the concerned Accountable Officer to maintain separate cashbook for each nature of Petty Cash Fund such as, (1) Drugs and Medicines, (2) Medical, Dental and Laboratory Supplies, (3) Fuel, Oil and Lubricants, (4) Food Supplies and (5) Other Supplies and Materials and Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses (MOOE) to properly account for disbursements charged to the fund and its corresponding balance at any given time.
observations.managementAction:
The petty cash replenishments is henceforth be closely monitored by PTO to make sure that they practice the prescribed maintenance of cashbook.

The PTO re-oriented the Disbursing Officer on the proper handling of cash and the proper recording in the cash book.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management follow the guidelines and procedures prescribed in COA Circular No. 2016-005 on the write-off of dormant receivable accounts and unliquidated fund transfers for the fair presentation of these accounts in the financial statements.
observations.reason: The Accounting Division is continuously working on the dormant receivable accounts. Their  request  for  write-off was delayed due to lack of documents which requires them to conduct thorough verification and preparation of supporting documents.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management instruct the concerned departments and the General Services Office to immediately prepare the inventory utilization reports and prepare/submit the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMIs) to the Provincial Accounting Office for the prompt recording of the issuances and distribution of inventories.

We also recommended that the Provincial Accountant investigate the non-moving inventory accounts and record the necessary adjusting entries to reflect the correct balance of the accounts in the books.
observations.managementAction:
The P150.07M cited inventories are now being checked and the concerned departments were instructed to immediately liquidate the balances especially those inventories in the welfare goods for distribution. Management called the attention of the concerned departments and follow-up their actions until fully liquidated.

As of Dec. 23, 2021, the balance is down to P25.54M of the cited amount.

observations.recommendation:
We           recommended            that: The   Provincial   Engineering   Office coordinate     with     the     Provincial Accountant’s Office and furnish the latter with the necessary documents regarding the turned over construction materials as basis for recording in the books; and

The Provincial Accountant, upon receipt of the relevant documents, prepare the following adjusting entry:

Dr.    Construction    Materials    Invty.    ₱2,372,412.00 Cr.      Flood Control System                        ₱2,372,412.00

To record the cost of construction materials turned over by the contractor to the PEO.
observations.managementAction:
Upon receipt of the memo from PEO, the PAO immediately booked the required entries to take-up the cost of construction materials turned-over to the PEO after completion of project by the contractor.

An explanation was prepared by the PEO on why the amount of P2,372,412 was not the total amount to be recorded by the Accounting Department.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management: Follow  up  with  the  Department  of Agriculture Central Office the release of funds pertaining to the final payment of the sub-project; and

Require the concerned contractor to submit the necessary documents for its final billing request to facilitate the payment thereof and in order that the completed project may be transferred to the Property, Plant and Equipment account as soon as possible.
observations.managementAction:
The final release of funds for this cited PRDP project was already released by Dept. of Agriculture and the contractor has already received the final payment.

The transfer of the completed project was recorded in the GF-Property, Plant & Equipment.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the PGSO: Maintain Property Cards for the Land, Land Improvements, Infrastructure Assets, Buildings and Structures of the Province; and

Conduct regular reconciliation of their PPE subsidiary records with PAO, at least on a quarterly basis, to  ensure that these records agree with each other.
observations.managementAction: There are a number of land accounts	recorded	before which	were	not	fully documented   as   they   have been in the books for a long time and nobody knows the history of each accounts. Subsidiary  records  are  still being put in place and will be updated	soon	for	easier reconciliation of records.
observations.reason: Land accounts whose cost can no longer be ascertained will be requested for appraisal from an independent appraiser which can be used as basis for recording in the books. For other properties whose records are still available, a reconciliation will be made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management continue to pursue improved practices in programming, prioritizing and implementing programs and projects to maximize  the  utilization  of  the  Local Development     Fund     in     attaining desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes for the benefit of the community.
observations.managementAction: The PPAs lined up were implemented according to priority needs of the people to address the pandemic and the effects of the typhoons which  struck  the  province.

observations.recommendation:
We commended Management for its compliance with the Minimum Standards of disaster Risk Reduction and Management.

We recommended however, that Management evaluate the existing plantilla of the APSEMO and take the necessary actions to ensure the availability of permanently trained responders in order that the Office’s ability to respond to all kinds of emergencies will be sustained.
observations.managementAction:
Management continues to train personnel to handle Disaster Risk Reduction and Management.

The Head of APSEMO has made recommendations for the creation of positions to handle the job of sustaining the required compliance in the implementation of disaster risk reduction and management.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous years’ audit recommendation that the Provincial Accountant submit the financial reports required under the Schedule of Financial Reports Submission (Annex “S) of the PRDP Financial Operations Guidelines within the prescribed periods.
observations.managementAction: The complete CY 2020 financial reports were submitted on July 2021.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management direct the Provincial Engineering Office to adhere strictly to the requirements of Annex “E” of R-IRR of R.A.   No.   9184   in   the   granting   of suspension of work and time extensions on infrastructure projects.

Further, require the Provincial Accountant and Provincial Engineer to submit the following documents to support the extension of contract time to enable the Audit Team to evaluate the    validity    for     such     extensions:

a.) Copy of Contractor’s request for Work Suspension/Time Extension;

b.) Copy of Evaluation Report by the Province establishing the need for time extensions including the number of days of extension was determined; and

c.) Copy of the approved revised Program Evaluation Review technique/Critical Path Method (PERT/CPM) Network Diagram reflecting the effect of the subject time extensions, Suspension/Resumption Order/s, and Variation Orders.
observations.managementAction:
The cited lacking documents were already submitted to the Audit Team.

Henceforth, we will see to it that the DVs transferred to COA are attached with the required supporting documents.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management: Direct  the  Provincial  Accountant  to coordinate with the concerned project implementing office to secure the lacking supporting documents and cause its immediate submission to the Auditor; and

Require the Provincial Accountant to ensure that all disbursement vouchers and	journal entry vouchers are adequately supported with the required documents to facilitate its audit.
observations.managementAction:
The employee in charge at he PAO will henceforth see to it that the required supporting documents are attached to the DVs before endorsing its approval.

A re-orientation will be conducted to the employees of the PAO to apprise them of the documentary requirements needed in every transaction to be recorded in the books.

observations.recommendation:
We  recommended  that  Management: Ensure  that  periodic  inspection  and monitoring   of   all   quarry   sites   are conducted by the ENRO to verify strict compliance by permittees with the terms and conditions of the permit and the existing Provincial Ordinance; and

Reconsider the role of the barangays in the monitoring and reporting of quarry activities and make representations with the Sangguniang Panlalawigan to revisit Provincial Ordinance No. 003- 2016 in relation thereto to further strengthen the internal control measures in the quarry operations of the Province, in order to prevent that damages in the quarry areas/sites may occur that will affect the environment and lives of the constituents of the barangays.
observations.managementAction:
The ENRO has been conducting     inspection     of quarry sites in the Province. Additional team of inspectors have been added to the existing two (2)  teams thereby increasing  the number of quarry sites inspected.

Proposed amendment of Provincial Ordinance No. 003-2016 had already been formulated and subject for review of the Provincial Legal Office.

observations.recommendation: We  recommended  that  Management, through   the   Local   School   Board, prepare the SEF Annual Budget strictly in accordance with Section 272 of the Local Government Code and the DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017 and Nos. 1 and 2, s. 2020.
observations.managementAction: The SEF budget for CY 2021 will be programmed to allowable expenses only.

observations.recommendation:
We commended Management, through the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS), in the setting up of the GAD database. However, we recommended that such database be regularly updated to serve as an accurate basis for gender- responsive planning, programming and policy formulation based on relevant data for proper identification of existing gender issues, thus, ensuring that the approved PPAs incorporated in the GPB are gender-responsive and timely.

Further, we recommended that Management immediately designate the replacement to the retired GAD FPS- TWG Chairperson to ensure continuity and  proper  supervision  of  the  GAD Office’s operations.
observations.managementAction:
The GAD database was immediately updated and will continue to be updated as practicable. The Province will continue the implementation of the GFPS since the Province’s GFPS is one of the best GFPS among the province’s in the Region.

The management designated Dr. Rowena L. Ondiz, as the GAD FPS-TWG Chairperson effective July 9, 2021 per Memorandum Order No.   19 s. 2021

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Make representations with the Sangguniang Panlalawigan to pass an ordinance requiring these commercial establishments to strictly follow the solid waste segregation procedures pursuant to the existing City Ordinance and the contracts of lease provisions regarding the maintenance of the commercial establishments’  premises in a clean and hygienic condition and its proper solid waste management. Otherwise, impose penalties and sanctions or consider terminating lease contracts, if warranted.
observations.managementAction: The Lease Contracts with the lessees has already a provision for proper disposition of solid wastes which is in consonance with the City Ordinance, hence there is no need for an ordinance, management will just monitor the lessee’s adherence.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Provincial Government expedite the development and operation of the computerized information system on RPT as soon as possible to achieve efficient and effective tax administration and improved collection of real property taxes by the 15 municipalities. The Provincial Government may require the municipalities to subsidize its development cost considering that the Municipalities’ share in the RPT is 40 percent, which is even more than the share of the Province.

It is further recommended that the system should be online so that entries posted by the MTOs, such as payments of RPT or newly registered property owners that are included in the tax database, shall also update the PTO’s own records on a real-time basis. However, if this is not possible due to constraints on internet connectivity by some municipalities, the PTO’s database may be updated thru the submission by the concerned municipalities of e-copies of the recorded entries on a monthly basis or at an even shorter interval.
observations.managementAction:
It will take time to develop the system since it needs to do stage by stage preparation of the system and deal with the constraints which are sometimes beyond our control.

This will be implemented if the system has already been developed.

The Provincial Administrator conducted several meetings with the  OIC-Provl. Treasurer to be able to develop an efficient and effective tax administration and improve collection  of real property taxes for the 15 municipalities while the system is not yet digitized. The OIC-Prov’l. Treasurer has coordinated with the 15 Municipal Treasurers and required them to submit to her Office every Friday the soft copies of Real Property Tax Account Register (RPTAR).

The PTO will consolidate the RPTARs of the Municipalities and will validate the veracity of the total assessed value as compared to the Summary of RPUs from the Provincial Assessor’s Office. Each RPU will be determined if payments are updated or delinquent. A report on the monthly, quarterly and annual collection and delinquency will then be generated based on the consolidated and validated RPTARs that will serve as basis for collection of delinquent real property taxes.

As part of the technical supervision of the PTO, series of technical visitations are on-going to monitor the implementation of the digitization. To date, the MTOs of Daraga, Bacacay and Sto. Domingo were already visited by PTO.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Provincial Assessor agreed with our recommendation to:

Ensure the consistency of the records of his office with the records of the Municipal Assessors of the 15 municipalities of Albay by reconciling the discrepancies noted and recording the necessary adjustments;

Conduct proper review of the QRRPAs submitted by the MTOs before its uploading to the eSRE system; and immediately make the necessary reconciliation and adjustment, if any, in the Provincial Assessor’s record, prior to its uploading by the MTOs in the eSRE system; and

Provide the Provincial Treasurer with the reconciled/adjusted QRRPA to serve as basis for the computation of the annual collection targets of the Province for RPT and SEF.
observations.managementAction:
The Prov'l. Assessor was required by the Prov'l. Administrator to submit to PTO the list of 233,391 RPUs encoded in e- TRACS during a meeting conducted to address the implementation of audit recommendations of the audit team.

A memorandum signed by the Governor of Albay was issued to all Municipal Assessors reiterating the provisions of Chapter 1, Sec. 2-B of MRPAAO, requiring them to submit monthly assessment reports and other assessment records or forms for reconciliation with the PAssO records and make necessary adjustments  for any discrepancies noted. Also, as directed under DOF Department Order No. 9-059- 2015, MAssOs submits electronic QRPPA for proper review and approval of the Provincial Assessor, BLGF Regional and Central Office prior to its uploading to the eSRE system to ensure reliability, accuracy and integrity of the reports.

Approved QRRPA uploaded to eSRE is forwarded to the Provincial Treasurer to serve as basis for the computation of the annual collection targets of the province for the RPT and SEF.

A subsequent memorandum was issued to MAssO for the time table of the schedule of submission of reports for PAssO to have ample time for review, reconciliation and timely submission of reports to BLGF.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:

40) the PTO and the MTOs ensure that the required	posting	of	the	notice	of delinquency in all the concerned barangays be strictly complied with;

Management make representation with the Sangguniang Panlalawigan to pass a resolution authorizing the participation of the barangays in the delivery of demand letters to delinquent taxpayers who are its residents; and

The PTO, through the MTOs and barangays, strive to send demand letters to all delinquent taxpayers in order to enforce collection of uncollected real property taxes.
observations.managementAction:
Notice of delinquencies have been disseminated and posted in the concerned barangays by Municipal Treasurers.

The Sangguniang Panlalawigan passed Resolution  No.  00830-2021, adopted on January 26, 2021 entitled: Authorizing the Barangay Officials to participate in the distribution of demand letters to resident delinquent taxpayers within their territorial jurisdiction.

The MTO and Barangays have already been directed by the OIC Provincial Treasurer to help deliver demand letters to delinquent taxpayers. With our information dissemination campaign, we expect to increase our tax collection.

observations.recommendation:
45) the  OIC-Provincial  Treasurer  notify the owners of the idle lands in the Province regarding the imposition of the additional tax which shall become due, in addition to the basic real property tax, at the beginning of each year.

46) Management make representations with the Sangguniang Panlalawigan to revisit Section 10 of the Provincial Tax Code	to amend, if possible, said provision in order that the municipalities may receive their share from the ad valorem tax imposed on idle lands within their respective jurisdictions for making and  keeping an updated record of all idle lands located within their area of jurisdiction pursuant to Section 239 of the RA No. 7160.
observations.managementAction:
The   PTO   will   notify   the owners of idle lands upon receipt of the final list.

PTO and PAssO already forwarded a letter to the Legal Officer last Dec. 14, 2021 requesting review and to recommend to the Sanggunian possible amendment of Sec. 10 of the Provincial Tax Code.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our previous year’s recommendations that Management direct the Provincial Accountant to:

47) As regards the Trust Fund accounts, return the unutilized balances to the respective source agencies.
observations.managementAction: PAO has been coordinating with the concerned Funds Provider agencies to determine the availability of funds. However, if everything has been done, the proof of payable has not been set, it will then be endorsed for its proper disposition.

observations.recommendation: Lastly, we recommended that the OIC- Provincial Assessor and the Provincial Legal Officer coordinate with the DA for the processing of the Deed of Donation in order that ownership of the said property may be effected as soon as possible.
observations.managementAction: Referred to Legal Office for proper disposition, it is being worked out.

observations.recommendation: Further, instruct the Provincial Accountant to reconcile the balances of the trust receipts against the books of the DOH ROV and other SAs, especially those accounts that are significant, in order that the correct amounts may be determined before liquidation thereof.
observations.managementAction: Trust funds received from the DOH RO V and other source agencies have been reconciled after series of meetings with the representatives of the agencies and the Province’s concerned personnel.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the difference between the accounts Real Property Tax Receivables and Special Education Tax Receivables and make the necessary adjustments, furnishing the Office of the Auditor with a copy of the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) taking into account the adjustments made.
observations.managementAction: No action was taken, the Municipal Treasurer firmly stand with the ruling on Aboitiz.
observations.reason: A difference of P5,017,917.66 still exists as of December 31, 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to correctly classify the outstanding balance of the long-term loans payable by presenting the portion of loans due for settlement within twelve months in the amount of P7,757,033.92 as part of the current liabilities and the remaining balance which is due for settlement beyond one year as non-current liabilities in the Statement of Financial Position.
observations.managementAction: No action was taken by the Management. The OIC-Municipal Accountant has overlooked the recording of the current liabilities portion in the Statement of Financial Position.
observations.reason: The OIC-Municipal Accountant has overlooked the recording of the current liabilities portion in the Statement of Financial Position.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. OIC-Municipal Accountant and the OIC-GSO to conduct periodic reconciliation
of their records and make the necessary adjustments on both records to reflect
the correct balance of the PPE accounts;
b. OIC-GSO to locate the PPEs not found during the conduct of the physical
count, if established missing, take immediate and appropriate action so that
the accountable person will be held liable for the loss of the noted properties;
c. OIC-GSO to include in the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable
Property (IIRUP) the unserviceable watercraft for proper disposal;
d. OIC-Municipal Accountant to continue in identifying various properties
reported in lumpsum to make the necessary adjustment and properly reconcile
the PPEs reflected in both records; and
e. OIC-Municipal Accountant to draw a Journal Entry Voucher to drop the semi-
expendable assets from the PPE accounts and ensure that those items are
covered by ICS.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Assessor to prioritize the transfer of title of the parcels of land in the
name of the Municipal Government of Bay, in compliance with Section 39(2) of
Presidential Decree (PD) No. 1445 and Article 47(c) of IRR of RA No. 7160;
b. OIC-Municipal Accountant and the OIC-GSO to coordinate with the Municipal
Assessor relative to the three parcels of land which was identified as not
owned by the Municipality. Upon further verification that the parcels of land are
not owned by the Municipality, drop in the books of account the parcels of land
totaling P591,990.00;
c. OIC-Municipal Accountant to record in the books of account the school site
amounting to P8,852,750.00 by debiting Land Account and crediting
Government Equity account;
d. Municipal Assessor and the OIC-Municipal Accountant to regularly reconcile
their records to ascertain the correctness of the Land account in the Financial
Statement;
e. Municipal Assessor to determine the current fair market values and assessed
values of the recorded parcels of land and provide updated data to the Office
of the OIC-Municipal Accountant; and
f. OIC-Municipal Accountant to adequately disclose in the Notes to the
Financial Statements the complete details of the Land account and its
corresponding current fair market value as provided by the Municipal
Assessor.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the OIC-Municipal Accountant
to draw Journal Entry Vouchers to effect the necessary adjustments to correct the
misclassified accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. instruct the OIC-GSO to prepare and submit RLRN following the prescribed
format;
b. request the Municipal Engineer to provide the OIC-GSO and OIC-Municipal
Accountant the complete description and cost segregation of road
components for road projects as required under COA Circular No. 2015-008
dated November 25, 2015; and
c. OIC-Municipal Accountant to comply with the disclosure requirement/format
for the road network system.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a. OIC-Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to reconcile the difference
between the accounts Real Property Tax Receivables and Special Education
Tax Receivables and their contra accounts; and
b. OIC-Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments, furnishing the
Office of the Auditor with a copy of the Journal Entry Voucher taking into
account the adjustments made.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. OIC-Municipal Accountant and OIC-General Services Officer (GSO) to strictly
adopt the Perpetual Inventory Method in accounting for procured inventory
items; and
b. OIC-GSO to ensure the conduct of a complete physical count of inventories
and the periodic reconciliation with the accounting records to facilitate timely
verification and adjustment of the discrepancy, if there is any.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
correctly classify the outstanding balance of the long-term loans payable by
presenting the portion of loans due for settlement within twelve months in the
amount of P6,423,700.48 as part of the current liabilities and the remaining balance
of P11,241,475.75 which is due for settlement beyond one year as non-current
liabilities in the Statement of Financial Position.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer and the OIC-
Municipal Accountant to reconcile the amount of unexpended balance of the
LDRRMF between the Utilization Report and the Trust Liability – DRRMF
account reflected in the Statement of Financial Position;
b. OIC-Municipal Accountant, upon reconciliation of the unexpended balances to
draw a Journal Entry Voucher to adjust the recorded balance of Trust
Liability- DRRMF account under Trust Fund; and
c. OIC-Municipal Accountant to disclose the amount and details of the
unexpended balances of the LDRRMF in the Notes to Financial Statement as
required in Sections 5.1.12 and 5.1.16 of the COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant
to review all transactions and accounting entries that affect the Due from Other
Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts and determine the cause of the
discrepancy. Likewise, make the necessary adjusting entries to bring both
balances in agreement, thus ensuring the reliability of the reciprocal accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a. OIC-Municipal Accountant and other concerned officials to submit the lacking
documents and ensure that subsequent payments are properly supported
with complete documents; and
b. OIC-General Services Officer (GSO) to properly fill-up the Purchase Requests
and Purchase Orders in accordance with its official design for a stronger
internal control.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant
to refrain from granting cash advances for purposes not allowed under COA
Circular No. 97-002 and indicate in the necessary data in the Purchase Request
and Purchase Orders to be procured.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the MPDC to:
a. include in their Annual Investment Plan (AIP) programs, projects, and
activities that have an impact on health and environmental issues to control
the spread of the virus;
b. provide additional allocation on PPAs for COVID-19 preventive measures as
listed under Section 2.1 of the DILG-DBM JMC No. 01 dated March 27, 2020;
c. evaluate the necessity of the unimplemented projects and reprogram the same
to more important development projects that will cater to the present needs of
its constituents; and
d. ensure the optimal utilization of the fund to help achieve the desired socio-
economic development and environmental outcomes of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Disaster Risk
Reduction and Management Council to:
a. update and improve the existing Local Disaster Risk Reduction and
Management Plan of the Municipality by focusing on the programs, projects,
and activities requiring the highest priority in terms of urgency and impact on
the four thematic areas of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management;
b. adequately plan the PPAs to be included in the AIP and ensure proper
allocation of fund to address the needs of the constituents to curtail and
eliminate the threat of COVID-19; and
c. monitor the available budget and utilization of the Local Disaster Risk
Reduction and Management Fund (LDRRMF) to effectively implement the
planned PPAs within the targeted schedule and to ensure the optimal
utilization of the fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Budget Officer to provide budgetary requirements for personal
services, maintenance and other operating expenditures, and capital outlay
for the MDRRM Office; and
b. Municipal Risk Reduction and Management Officer to refrain from using the
five percent LDRRM Fund to pay for operating expenses of the MDRRM
Office and other expenses not related to the purpose of the fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. require the OIC-Municipal Accountant to prepare the monthly Report on the
Sources and Utilization of the DRRMF; and
b. instruct the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office
(MDRRMO) to submit the monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of the
DRRMF to the COA Auditor as required under 5.1.5 of COA Circular 2012-002
for monitoring and evaluation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Person to:
a. furnish the Audit Team with a copy of GAD AR within five working days from
the end of January of the ensuing year, in compliance with COA Circular No.
2014-001; and
b. submit the GAD AR together with the required attachments as provided under
Section 4.1.C.8 PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01, as amended, as
follows:
1. summary of the reported program or project;
2. copies of reported policy issuances;
3. results of HGDG tests; and
4. actions taken by the LGU on the COA audit findings and
recommendations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Social Welfare
and Development Officer (MSWDO) to implement the PPAs in coordination with
other Municipal officials for the benefits and protection of children, in compliance
with Section 15 of RA No. 9344 and DILG MC No. 2012-120.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. consider conducting urgent meeting with the Department/Office Heads to
tackle the issues and concerns contained in the AAR to adopt a coordinated
action plan;
b. submit the duly accomplished AAPSI on all audit observations and audit
recommendations as soon as the appropriate plan of action is finalized; and
c. ensure that the Municipality’s AAPSI is submitted to the Audit Team within 60
days from receipt of the AAR, in compliance with Section 93 of the General
Provision of RA No. 10651.

observations.managementAction:
Reconciliation was made by the OIC-Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer.
Journal Entry Voucher to drop the small tangible assets from the PPE account was made to drop the small tangible assets from the PPE account.
Minor repairs were reclassified and capitalized in the books as PPE instead of outright expense.
Report of the Physical Count of PPE was prepared in the prescribed format.
observations.reason:
A discrepancy of P222,501.76 still exists between both records as of December 31, 2020.
Semi-expendable assets totaling P389,650.00 were still classified as PPE in the books of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Provincial Accountant to address the difference in the accounts Due to/from LGUs and make the necessary adjustments to ensure that the balances of the accounts are in agreement.
observations.managementAction: Necessary adjustments were made to reconcile the balances of the account in both records.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Administrator to make representation with the Department of Energy officials to resolve the issues on the transfer of ownership of E-trikes;
b. Municipal Accountant to draw JEV together with the Property Transfer Report and provide copies to the Department of Energy, in case important matters were resolved and amenable to the supposed recipients; and
c. If the transfer of ownership cannot be possible, return the units to the Department of Energy to avoid deterioration within the vicinity of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: A meeting with the Department of Energy was requested. The Municipal Administrator wrote a letter to the Department of Energy requesting a meeting regarding the E-Trikes. No actions were taken by the Management. Transfer of ownership over the motor vehicles was still not transferred to the barangays. The Municipality is still in the process of accumulating the papers needed to facilitate the transfer of the vehicles.
observations.reason: Transfer of ownership is in process. Donated motor vehicles were still not insured as of December 31, 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments, furnishing the Office of the Auditor with a copy of the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) reverting the unexpended balance of DRRMF for CY 2013 to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund. We also recommended for the Sangguniang Bayan to pass a resolution appropriating the unexpended balance of DRRMF for CY 2013 for specific programs, projects and activities relating to other social services. Further, we recommended that the LDRRMO, in coordination with the Municipal Accounting Office, submit to the Audit Team the monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF on or before the 15th day after the end of each month in compliance with COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: Most of the unexpended balances of DRRMF for CYs 2013 to 2020 were utilized during CY 2020 to respond to the needs of the constituents during the outbreak of the Coronavirus Disease 2019 (COVID-19). The Monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF was not submitted to the Audit Team in the correct format. The correct format of the report was being submitted to the Audit Team for the year 2021.
observations.reason: The Monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF was not submitted to the Audit Team in the correct format.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Budget Officer to allocate in the Annual Budget an amount for the titling of real property acquired by the Municipality; and
b. Municipal Assessor to facilitate the transfer of title of the land in the name of the Municipal Government of Bay to protect its interest from third party claims and illegal dispositions.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2020, the Municipal Budget Officer appropriated an insufficient amount to cover the titling of all real property acquired by the Municipality. Completion of the necessary documents to process the transfer of title of the land was still ongoing.
observations.reason:
The amount allocated by the Municipality is insufficient to cover the titling of the real property acquired by the Municipality.
Completion of the Certificate of Title of the 15 parcels of land was still not transferred in the name of the Municipality as of December 31, 2020.

observations.managementAction: Most of the insurable properties were covered with insurance. The Municipality is still in the process of accumulating the papers needed to facilitate the transfer of the donated motor vehicles. Unserviceable properties were already reported in the IIRUP and requested for disposal.
observations.reason:
Donated motor vehicles were still not insured as of December 31, 2020.
The Municipality is still gathering the supporting documents needed for transfer of ownership.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer see to it that appropriation for EME does not exceed the limitation prescribed under Section 325(h) of RA No. 7160 to avoid disallowance in the audit. We also recommended that the Management justify the excess charging of expenses in EME totaling P1,308,513.21. Otherwise, refund the expenses in excess of the two percent limitation.
observations.managementAction: The charges made to the EME were analyzed and adjusted in the books of account of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the stall owners execute MOA or contract to contain all the terms and conditions on the lease of 156 stalls and the basis for the collection of rental and penalties, in case of late payment, to generate additional income for the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: First Public Hearing has been made in 2020, thorough process due to the participation of the Sangguniang Bayan Members and the Municipal Treasurer. The second hearing will be done in the year 2021.
observations.reason: The process is lengthy due to the thorough participation and revision of terms; second hearing will be conducted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to review the accounts in the financial statements and reclassify them to the most appropriate account for proper presentation in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Reclassification of the charges made to the Other Professional Services account is ongoing to be classified to the appropriate account.
observations.reason: Evaluation of payments for JOs is ongoing to be classified to the appropriate account.

observations.managementAction: Most of the programs, projects, and activities included in the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Plan for CY 2020 were still the same in CY 2019. Consultations with the constituents were not conducted. Utilization of the fund decreased from 69.58 percent in the previous year to only 51.34 percent in CY 2020 due to the outbreak of COVID-19. Regular expenditures totaling P1,009,572.57 were still charged against the five percent DRRM Fund. The Municipality partially utilized its 30 percent Quick Response Fund to finance various COVID-19 related expenditures.
observations.reason: Low utilization rate due to COVID-19; regular expenditures amounting to P1,009,572.57 were still charged against the LDRRMF.

observations.managementAction: The GAD Plan and Budget (GPB) for CY 2020 was submitted and reviewed by DILG as evidenced by the Certificate of Endorsement. Update of the GAD database of the Municipality was being conducted. The GPB of the Municipality for CY 2020 was updated to prioritize various COVID-19 related PPAs. For CY 2020, a total amount of P10,453,228.65 or 99.49 percent of the total approved budget of P10,506,775.65 was utilized by the Municipality.
observations.reason: The update of the GAD database of the Municipality was still being conducted as of December 31, 2020.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Office to:
a. effectively plan the programs, projects, and activities to be included in the AIP of the LCPC through consultations with the constituents to identify other PPAs that will surely improve the lives of the youth and will reduce or eliminate juvenile delinquency in the Municipality;
b. regularly monitor the status of implementation of the planned PPAs to ensure the optimal utilization of the fund; and
c. include in the LCPC budget PPAs that can be funded by the one percent of IRA in accordance with DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 dated July 4, 2012.
observations.managementAction: The new MSWDO Officer took charge only in August 2020. The Municipality planned to implement programs, projects and activities on the protection of children in CY 2021.
observations.reason: The new MSWDO Officer took charge only in August 2020.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:

a. Municipal Accounting Office and the General Services Office to conduct
periodic reconciliation of their records and make the necessary adjustments
on both records, if warranted, to reflect the correct balance of the PPE
accounts;
b. Municipal Accountant to draw a Journal Entry Voucher to drop the semi-
expendable assets from the PPE accounts and ensure that those items are
covered by ICS;
c. Municipal Accountant to reclassify minor repairs amounting to P52,830.22
erroneously capitalized in the books as PPE instead of outright expense;
d. General Services Office to prepare an inventory report using the prescribed
format separating the PPE accounts with semi-expendable assets and
submit to the Office of the Auditor not later than January 31 of each year.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
reconcile the difference between the accounts Real Property Tax Receivables and
Special Education Tax Receivables and make the necessary adjustments,
furnishing the Office of the Auditor with a copy of the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV)
taking into account the adjustments made.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
coordinate with the Provincial Accountant to address the difference in the
reciprocal accounts Due to/from LGUs and make the necessary adjustments to
ensure that the balances of the accounts are in agreement.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
correctly classify the outstanding balance of the long-term loans payable by
presenting the portion of loans due for settlement within twelve months in the
amount of P7,757,033.92 as part of the current liabilities and the remaining balance
of P17,665,176.23 which is due for settlement beyond one year as non-current
liabilities in the Statement of Financial Position.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:

a. Municipal Administrator to make representation with the Department of Energy
officials to resolve the issues on the transfer of ownership of E-trikes;
b. Municipal Accountant to draw JEV together with the Property Transfer Report
and provide copies to the Department of Energy, in case important matters
were resolve and amenable to the supposed to be recipients; and
c. If the transfer of ownership cannot be possible, return the units to the
Department of Energy to avoid deterioration within the vicinity of the
Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to
make the necessary adjustments, furnishing the Office of the Auditor with a copy
of the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) reverting back the unexpended balance of
DRRMF for CY 2013 to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund.

We also recommended for the Sangguniang Bayan to pass a resolution
appropriating the unexpended balance of DRRMF for CY 2013 for specific
programs, projects and activities relating to other social services.

Further, we recommended that the LDRRMO, in coordination with the Municipal
Accounting Office, submit to the Audit Team the monthly Report on the Sources
and Utilization of LDRRMF on or before the 15th day after the end of each month
in compliance with COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:

a. Municipal Budget Officer to allocate in the Annual Budget an amount for the
titling of real property acquired by the Municipality; and

b. Municipal Assessor to facilitate the transfer of title of the 19 parcels of land in
the name of the Municipal Government of Bay to protect its interest from third
party claims and illegal dispositions.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Acting General Services
Officer to:

a. coordinate with the Accounting Office to reconcile the building and motor
vehicle accounts and prepare and submit the updated list of insurable
property not yet covered with the General Insurance Fund of the GSIS;
b. facilitate the transfer of ownership of the donated motor vehicles in the
name of the Municipality and see to it that it will be included in the list of
insurable properties;
c. transfer the ownership of the motor vehicles currently used by various
Barangays so that payment of insurance will be shouldered by the
concerned Barangays; and
d. report the unserviceable properties in the Inventory and Inspection Report
of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) for proper disposal and drop them from
the books of the agency.

We also recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan appropriate sufficient amount
to cover the insurance premiums of all insurable properties.

We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer see to it that appropriation for
EME does not exceed the limitation prescribed under Section 325(h) of RA No.
7160 to avoid disallowance in audit.

We also recommended that the Management justify the excess charging of
expenses in EME totaling P1,308,513.21. Otherwise, refund the expenses in excess
of the two per cent limitation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the stall owners execute MOA or
contract containing all the terms and conditions on the lease of 156 stalls and
basis for the collection of rental and penalties, in case of late payment, to
generate additional income for the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
review the accounts in the financial statements and reclassify them to the most
appropriate account for proper presentation in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Disaster Risk
Reduction and Management Council to:

a. update and improve the existing Local Disaster Risk Reduction and
Management Plan of the Municipality by focusing on the programs,
projects and activities requiring the highest priority in terms of urgency
and impact on the four thematic areas of Disaster Risk Reduction and
Management;
b. conduct consultations with the constituents to determine PPAs aligned
with the purpose of the fund;
c. monitor the available budget and utilization of the Local Disaster Risk
Reduction and Management Fund (LDRRMF) to effectively implement the
planned PPAs within the targeted schedule and to ensure the optimal
utilization of the fund;
d. stop the practice of charging regular expenditures against the LDRRMF,
otherwise the transactions shall be disallowed in audit; and
e. analyze the PPAs and identify if they are chargeable against the 30 per cent
Quick Response Fund or the 70 per cent Preparedness and Mitigation
Fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Point System
(GFPS) to:

a. ensure that the GAD Plan and Budget of the Municipality was submitted and
reviewed by the DILG as evidenced by the Certificate of Endorsement;
b. coordinate with the Municipal Planning and Development Office to update its
GAD database to have a source of reliable information necessary for its GAD
planning, programming and policy formulation;
c. improve the GAD Plan and Budget of the Municipality through identifying new
gender issues and concerns within its locality by the use of sex-
disaggregated data;
d. coordinate with other offices to avoid duplication of projects and monitor the
effective and efficient implementation of the GAD Plan and Budget so that the
intent of GAD on promoting gender-responsive governance, protecting and
fulfilling women’s human rights and promoting women’s empowerment as
mandated could be fully achieved; and
e. ensure that funds no longer needed for a particular program should be
realigned before using it to another program to avoid disallowance in audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Local Council for the
Protection of children to:

a. effectively plan the programs, projects and activities to be included in the AIP
of the LCPC through consultations with the constituents to identify other PPAs
that will surely improve the lives of the youth and will reduce or eliminate
juvenile delinquency in the Municipality;
b. regularly monitor the status of implementation of the planned PPAs to ensure
the optimal utilization of the fund; and
c. Include in the LCPC budget PPAs that can be funded by the one per cent of
IRA in accordance with DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 dated July
4, 2012.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a) Municipal Accountant and Acting General Services Officer (GSO) to evaluate the discrepancies between their records and make the necessary adjustments;
b) Municipal Accountant to draw a Journal Entry Voucher to drop the small tangible assets from the PPE account; and
c) Acting GSO to ensure that all tangible assets are covered by ICS.
observations.managementAction:
The Acting Municipal Accountant and the Acting GSO Office coordinates with each other on the process of reconciling the discrepancies between their records.
The Acting GSO is in the process of issuing ICS to end-users of tangible assets.
No action was taken by the Management.
observations.reason:
The Acting Municipal Accountant assumed office only on June 10, 2020.
Issuance of ICS was not given enough time because there are other things to be done.
Reconciliation of PPE account is in process.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to recognize in the books the accrued interest of P861,196.59 by debiting account “Prior Period Adjustment” and crediting “Interest Payable” both by the same amount in compliance with the accrual method of accounting.
observations.managementAction: JEV No. 2019-12-010A dated December 2, 2019 was drawn to recognize the accrued interest payable in CY 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to intensify a tax campaign to maximize collections, especially on the delinquent taxpayers and avail of the remedies provided under Sections 254, 256 and 258 of RA No. 7160 to enforce collection of delinquent taxes and generate additional income for the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer submitted list of properties for auction to the Provincial Treasurer’s Office.
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasurer submitted list of properties for auction was scheduled on June 2019. Process for the auction is on-going.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Budget Officer to allocate in the Annual Budget an amount for the titling of real property acquired by the Municipality.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Acting GSO and Municipal Assessor to prioritize the transfer of titles of the 23 parcels of land in the name of the Municipal Government of Bay in compliance with Section 39(2) of PD No. 1445, Article 47(c) of the IRR of RA No. 7160 and Section 148 of COA Circular No. 92-386 to protect the interest of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Budget Officer allocated in the CY 2019 Annual Budget an amount for the titling of real property acquired by the Municipality.
Titling of land acquired by the Municipality valued at P644,350.00 is now in process.
observations.reason:
The amount allocated in the CY 2019 Annual Budget P200,000.00 is insufficient to cover the titling of all real property acquired by the Municipality.
Titling of land acquired by the Municipality valued at P644,350.00 is now in process. Completion of implemented documents is in process.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Acting GSO to:
a) see to it that the amount necessary for the insurance premiums of all insurable property is included in the annual appropriation; and
b) direct the Acting GSO to facilitate the preparation and submission of the list of insurable property not yet insured so that the necessary insurance coverage for all the assets can be applied with the GSIS under the General Insurance Fund.
observations.managementAction:
The OIC-General Services Officer is currently in the process of completing the requirements for the insurance of buildings. Per inquiry with GSIS, the following requirements are necessary: a) building plan, b) location map and c) title. The GSO already requested for titles or tax declarations to Assessor’s Office while the building plans are already requested to the Municipal Engineer. Upon obtaining the amount of insurance, it will be endorsed to the Municipal Budget Officer for the allocation of fund for CY 2021.
Moreover, the GSO is also facilitating the transfer of ownership of vehicles that are currently being used by some barangays and other government agencies. Unserviceable vehicles which are beyond economic repair and in which maintenance cost outweighs the benefits that can be derived from its continuous use will be reported to Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) and will be requested for disposal.
observations.reason:
The amount allocated in the CY 2019 Annual Budget P940,500.00 is insufficient to cover the insurance of all insurable property acquired by the Municipality.
Non-submission of list of insurable properties.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the officials concerned to adopt the ATM payroll system to safeguard government funds from risks of loss or misapplication associated with the manual payroll system.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality adopted the ATM payroll system.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to return the unutilized balances of fund transfers to source agencies.
observations.managementAction: A total of P1,000,000.00 was returned to the source agencies thru check no. 716357 dated April 8, 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Local Council for the Protection of Children to effectively plan the programs, projects and activities to be included in the AIP of the LCPC through consultations with the constituents; regularly monitor the status of implementation of the planned PPAs to ensure the optimal utilization of the fund; and include in the LCPC budget PPAs that can be funded by the one per cent of IRA in accordance with DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 dated July 4, 2012.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality utilized P829,617.00 or 58.46 per cent of the PPAs or an increase of 20.89 per cent compared to CY 2018.
observations.reason: Poor planning and lack of supplemental PPAs to replaced those financed by the Province and those charged to other funds.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Officer to:
a) Comply with the minimum requirements for the operation of the controlled dumpsite particularly on the leachate treatment necessary for the facility and clay liner, pursuant to Section 37 of RA No. 9003 until such time that the existing private sanitary landfill became fully compliant with the criteria enumerated in pertinent provisions of RA No. 9003; and
b) To coordinate with the operator of the landfill to prioritize the submission of complete documents needed in securing permit from the PENRO to dispose filling materials in the proposed sanitary landfill.
observations.managementAction:
Regular coordination with the operator is being undertaken.
Finding suitable open space for the six remaining barangays is still ongoing.
Completion of documents needed in securing permit from the PENRO is ongoing.
observations.reason: Finding suitable open space for the other controlled dumpsites necessary for the operation of the controlled dumpsite are still not complied.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as Chairman of the Municipal Solid Waste Management Group, direct the concerned officials to find a suitable open space for establishment of MRFs in every barangay or cluster of barangays, in compliance with Section 1, Rule XI of the IRR of RA No. 9003 and DILG MC No. 2009-168 dated October 27, 2009.
observations.managementAction: Finding suitable open space for the six remaining barangays is still ongoing.
observations.reason: MRFs for the six remaining barangays are still not established.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the MDRRMO to fully implement its LDRRMP and maximize the utilization of the LDRRMF prioritizing the procurement of life saving PPAs and rescue equipment, among others.
observations.managementAction: A total of P4,615,024.75 or 69.58 per cent of the Mitigation Fund in CY 2019 was utilized or an increase of 29.55 per cent compared to CY 2018.
observations.reason: Poor planning and lack of consultation with the constituents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to undertake the actual cash transfer of the unspent amount of the Mitigation Fund in the General Fund to the Trust Fund books, in compliance with COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: A total of P28,496,356.56 was transferred from the General Fund to Trust Fund as of December 31, 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Person to prepare GAD Plan and Budget that clearly address gender-related issues and concerns as enumerated in PCW-NEDA-DBM JC No. 2012-001 and implement the same using the HGDG tool, gender and analysis to come up with a gender-responsive GAD Plan.
observations.managementAction: GAD Plan and Budget for Calendar Year 2019 was submitted to the DILG Office and reviewed and approved by the Provincial Director.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Person to submit the GAD Plan and Budget and GAD Accomplishment Report within the period prescribed under Item V of COA Circular No 2014-001.
observations.managementAction: GAD Plan and Budget and GAD Accomplishment Report for Calendar Year 2019 were submitted to the Office of the Auditor.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:

a. Municipal Accountant and Acting General Services Officer to evaluate the
discrepancies between their records and make the necessary adjustments;
b. Municipal Accountant to draw a Journal Entry Voucher to drop the small tangile
assets from the PPE accounts; and
c. Acting General Services Officer to ensure that all small tangible assets are
covered by ICS.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
recognize in the books the accrued interest of P861,196.59 by debiting the account
“Prior Period Adjustment” and crediting “Interest Payable” both by the same
amount, in compliance with the accrual method of accounting.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to
intensify a tax campaign to maximize collections, especially on the delinquent
taxpayers and avail of the remedies provided under Sections 254, 256 and 258 of
RA No. 7160 to enforce collections of delinquent taxes and generate additional
income for the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Budget Officer to
allocate in the Annual Budget an amount for the titling of real property acquired by
the Municipality.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Acting General Services
Officer and the Municipal Assessor to prioritize the transfer of title of the 23 parcels
of land in the name of the Municipal Government of Bay in compliance with Section
39(2) of PD No. 1445, Article 47(c) of IRR of RA No. 7160 and Section 148 of COA
Circular No. 92-386 to protect the interest of the Municipality.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor:
a. see to it that the amount necessary for the insurance premiums of all insurable
property is included in the annual appropriation; and
b. direct the Acting General Services Officer to facilitate the preparation and
submission of the list of insurable property not yet insured so that the necessary
insurance coverage for all the assets can be applied with the GSIS under the
General Insurance Fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the official concerned to adopt
the ATM payroll system to safeguard government funds from risk of loss or
misapplication associated with the manual payroll system.

We reiterated our recommendations that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Treasurer to return the unutilized balances of fund transfers to Source Agency.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Social Welfare and
Development Officer to seriously undertake steps to fully implement the LCPC
PPAs as mandated under RA No. 9344 and enumerated under DILG MC No. 2012-
120 dated July 4, 2012 for the protection of children and improvement of the lives
of the youth and reduction of, if not eliminate juvenile delinquency in the
Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Environment and
Natural Resources Officer to:

a. comply with the minimum requirements for the operation of controlled
dumpsites particularly on the leachate treatment facility and clay liner, pursuant
to Section 37 of RA No. 9003 until such time that the existing private sanitary
landfill became fully compliant with the criteria enumerated in pertinent
provisions of RA No. 9003.
b. to coordinate with the operator of the landfill to prioritize the submission of
complete documents needed in securing permit from the Provincial
Environment and Natural Resources Office to dispose filling materials in the
proposed sanitary landfill.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as Chairman of the Municipal Solid
Waste Management Board, direct the concerned Officials to find a suitable open
space for the establishment of MRFs in every Barangay or cluster of Barangays, in
compliance with Section 1, Rule XI of the IRR of RA No. 9003 and DILG MC No.
2009-168 dated October 27, 2009.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Disaster Risk
Reduction and Management Officer to fully implement its LDRRMP and maximize
the utilization of the LDRRMF prioritizing the procurement of life saving and rescue
equipment, among others.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
undertake the actual cash transfer of the unspent amount of the Mitigation Fund in
the General Fund to the Trust Fund books in compliance with COA Circular
No. 2012-002.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Point System
through the GAD Focal Person to prepare GAD Plan and Budget that clearly address
gender-related issues and concerns as enumerated in PCW-NEDA-DBM JC
No. 2012-001 and implement the same using the HGDG tool, gender and analysis to
come up with a gender-responsive GAD Plan.

We also recommended that Management refrain from charging to GAD fund those
expenditures which are deemed not supportive of gender issues and concerns.
Charge those expenditures against appropriate fund of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Person to submit
the GAD Plan and Budget and GAD Accomplishment Report wihtin the period
prescribed under Item V of COA Circular No. 2014-001.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Accountant and the OIC-General Services Officer (GSO) to evaluate and address the discrepancies; 
b. Municipal Accountant to reclassify to Intangible Assets the carrying amount of all Computer Software acquired by the Municipality; and
c. Municipal Accountant to reclassify PPE with a value of less than P10,000.00 and life expectancy of more than one year as inventories upon acquisition and expenses upon issuance and the OIC-GSO to issue Inventory Custodian Slip (ICS) for control and monitoring purposes.
observations.managementAction:
The difference between the accounting and inventory records of P9,452,393.46 was fully accounted and recorded under Infrastructure assets.
Computer systems are now recorded under ICT account.
The Management is in the process of identifying small tangible assets that are already unserviceable, obsolete or no longer needed.
Dropping small tangible assets from PPE accounts is in progress.
Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 1, Page Nos. 34-36 of this Report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct:
a. the Municipal Accountant to stop the practice of granting additional cash advances to any officials, officers and employees with outstanding cash advances; and
b. increase the amount of bond of the SDOs pursuant to Treasury Circular No. 02-2009 dated August 6, 2009 to protect the interest of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipality stopped the practice of granting additional cash advances.
Aside from the Special Disbursing Officer (SDO), the Municipal Treasurer has now been utilized to draw cash advances for payroll fund and other special purpose.
The SDO’s fidelity fund has increased to P2,500,000.00 during the year.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the OIC-GSO and/or the Municipal Assessor to facilitate the immediate transfer of ownership of the acquired parcels of land in the name of the Municipality to protect the interest of the municipal government.
We also recommended that the concerned municipal officials request the Sangguniang Bayan to set aside a fund for the titling of the land.
observations.managementAction:
The OIC-GSO has already started to facilitate the transfer of title of the parcels of land under the name of the Municipality.
The Municipality has already set aside fund for the titling of land.
observations.reason: The number of parcels of land not titled in the name of the Municipality increased from 22 last year to 24 this year.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and Municipal Administrator determine and address the delays in the implementation of projects. We also recommended that a system be in place to monitor the timely and full implementation of municipal programs and projects to ensure that the constituents are provided with immediate and adequate social services and livelihood projects.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to return the unexpended balances of unutilized transfers to the Source Agency or request authority from the Source Agency to use the fund balance for other projects.
observations.managementAction:
Fund transfer from National Government of P927,216.21 had already been utilized.
Implementation of PPAs is already observed.
The amount of P278,493.91 has been refunded to respective source agencies or fully implemented through the Bureau of Treasury.
observations.reason: The amount of P5,659.00 has not been refunded yet.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our prior recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-GSO to facilitate the preparation and submission of the list of insurable properties so that the necessary insurance coverage for all the assets can be applied with the GSIS under the General Insurance Fund.
We also reiterated our previous recommendation that the Municipal Mayor see to it that the amount necessary for the premiums for all insurable property is included in the annual appropriation.
observations.managementAction: Aside from motor vehicles, the Municipality had already insured some of its insurable properties with the GSIS property Insurance Fund. The Municipal Main building and Municipal Market were insured. The Municipality included in its budget the payment of insurance expenses on some properties. The Annex Building and Rural Health Unit (RHU) are now in progress.
observations.reason: Not all of the insurable property is insured with the GSIS Property Insurance Fund.
