observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Secretariat and the responsible persons to post all procurement transactions of the Municipality at PhilGEPS or in the website of the Municipality, pursuant to Sections 37.1.6 and 37.4.2 of the RIRR of RA No. 9184 to ensure wider dissemination of procurement opportunities.
observations.managementAction: Posting all procurement transactions of the Municipality at PhilGEPS.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to continue effective, efficient and aggressive tax collection effort and develop strategies to improve their tax collection efficiency. Aside from sending Notice of Delinquency, update the Provincial Treasurer on matter of legal remedies for application of administrative and judicial actions to enforce collections from delinquent real property taxpayers.
observations.managementAction: Sent final notice and warrant of levy to delinquent taxpayers. They also requested the assistance of the Provincial Treasurer to take legal remedies to delinquent taxpayers. Delinquent taxpayers with Notice of Delinquency are already scheduled for publication in the newspaper of general circulation in June 2019 in preparation for public auction.
observations.reason: Real Property Tax delinquency decreased from P42,378,643.14 last year to P39,257,223.20 this year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to undertake actual cash transfer of the unspent amount of the LDRRMF to the Trust Fund books in compliance with COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: The unexpended balance of the Quick Respond Fund (QRF) was already transferred as Trust Liabilities -DRRMF in the Book.
observations.reason: The unutilized balance of the Mitigation Fund was still taken up as Trust Liabilities -DRRMF in the General Fund Book.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Management optimize the utilization of the Mitigation Fund to strengthen its capacity to prevent and mitigate disaster risks. Management should prioritize the procurement of basic rescue and response equipment and conduct municipal-wide training on disaster risk management.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipality is planning to buy a rescue vehicle, rescue boat and other equipment. They are planning to prioritize the training of DRRM rescue team.
Also, they are planning to conduct municipal-wide training on disaster risk management.
observations.reason: The utilization of the Mitigation Fund increased to 40.03 per cent this year as against 26.50 per cent last year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to allocate and fully utilize one per cent of the IRA for programs, projects and activities intended for the protection of children’s rights, upliftment of their lives and elimination of juvenile delinquency in the locality as envisioned under RA No. 9344.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality has already allocated one per cent of its IRA for LCPC amounting to P1,289,237.58.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant
and the OIC-General Services Officer (GSO) to evaluate and address the discrepancies
between their records to ensure the fair presentation of the PPE account.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the OIC-GSO, Municipal
Assessor and the Municipal Treasurer to facilitate the immediate transfer of ownership
of the acquired parcels of land in the name of the Municipal Government to protect the
interest of the government and to ensure the reliability of the land account.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-General Services Officer to
facilitate the preparation and submission of the list of insurable properties so that the
necessary insurance coverage for all the assets can be applied with the GSIS under the
General Insurance Fund.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor see to it that the amount necessary for
the premiums for insurable properties is included in the annual appropriation,

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer
and Municipal Accountant to strictly comply with the provisions of Section 20 of the
MNGAS, Volume I, for LGUs, to be able to establish with certainty the RPT/SET
Receivables.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-GSO to effect the
immediate disposal of the unserviceable properties to protect them from further deterioration
and to obtain higher economic value in case of their disposal thru sale.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the BAC to handle the
procurement process, which includes the evaluation and documentation, of all
Alternative Method of Procurement, in compliance with Section 12.1 of the RIRR of RA
No. 9184.

We also recommended that the BAC Secretariat post the Notice of Award in the
PhilGEPS and in the Municipality’s website to ensure transparency of government
procurement.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor adhere to the provisions of RA No.
9003 governing the operation and management of open dumpsite for the protection of
public health and the environment. Immediate actions should be undertaken to eliminate
the accumulated wastes in the open dumpsite.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant the certified list of taxpayers showing the amount due and collectible to set up a more reliable RPT/SET Receivable.
observations.managementAction: The  Municipal Treasurer submitted  the Summary List of Orders/Contracts including supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor consider that the hiring of job order personnel be based on necessity and identified/attributed to specific projects for a period not exceeding six months pursuant to Section 2, Rule X1 of the Revised Omnibus Rules on Appointments and Other Personnel Actions, under CSC Memorandum Circular No. 40 and Section 77 of RA No. 7160.
observations.managementAction: The  Municipal Mayor has instructed the MHRMO, with Municipal Budget Officer to review the procedures in the hiring of Job Orders to adhere with the provision of csc Memorandum Circular No. 40 and Section 77 of RA No 7160.
observations.reason: There were still hired JOs not being identified and projects specific.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC Chairman and the General Services Officer to strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular on the submission of contract and purchase orders.
observations.managementAction: Copies of Purchase Orders already submitted to the Office of the Auditor

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor adhere to the provisions of RA No. 9003 governing the operation and management of open dumpsite for the protection of public health and the environment. Immediate actions should be undertaken in the accumulated wastes in the open dumpsite.
observations.managementAction: Follow-up has been made to the confractor SB Hain Enterprises for the status of development activities and other enhancement programs are on-going.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No.7, Part II of the report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BSWMB headed by the Municipal Mayor strictly comply with the provisions and establish Materials Recovery Facilities (MRF) and pursue the 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan.
observations.managementAction: The 10-Year Solid Waste Management Plan 2015-2024 has been approved by the National Solid Waste Management Commission under NSWMC Resolution No. 849 dated December 13, 2016. Based on Environmental Compliance Audit (ECA) data for the fourth quarter of 2016, only seven barangays have their own MRF out of fifteen barangays.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer to properly identify and categorize the programs, projects and activities that are to be charged to LDRRMF and utilize the fund strictly for calamity-related activities and stop the utilization of LDRRMF for activities other than those specified under RA No. 10121.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor has instructed the LDRRMO to properly identify and categorize the programs; projects and activities that are to be charged to LDRRMF and to comply with the guidelines mentioned in Section 1, Rule 18 of RA No.10121.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor together with the Municipal Planning and Development Office (MPDO) & the Municipal Economic Development Council (EDC) to comply with the provisions of Section 287, RA No. 7160 or the Local Government Code, Section 6 of Administrative Order No. 103 dated August 31, 2004 and DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1, Series of 2005 dated September 20, 2005.
observations.managementAction: The Head, MPDC has instructed the MPDO and MBO to facilitate the proper timely implementation of the projects and to maximize benefits that can be derived by the constituents in general.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No.11, Part I of the report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Chairperson, GAD Focal Point Officer and the Budget Officer provide the DILG Regional Office with a copy of Annual Gender and Development (GAD) Plan and Budget for review and approval.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the municipal officials concerned to fast track the creation of sex disaggregated database that will support the gender related issues identified  and prioritized in the GAD Plan.
observations.managementAction:
The Chairperson, GAD Focal Point Person has submitted the Annual GAD Plan and Budget to the DILG  Regional Office.

As of year-end, the GAD Focal Point Person has prepared the Sex disaggregated database for the following Sectors: Youth, Women, Solo Parent, PWD and Family in Disaster - Prone Areas.
